AI — Semis & Compute

NVDAFull-stack AI-factory platform at +85% rev; debate is demand sustainability vs ASIC erosion.
AVGO#1 custom-ASIC/TPU partner; binary setup on the ">$100B" FY27 guide vs $250-300B FY28 (JPM).
AMDAI-infra long with a server-CPU leg; risk concentrated in MI450 execution.
TSMSole-source leading-edge foundry (~70% share); pricing headroom vs overseas-fab dilution.
ASMLSupply-chain chokepoint (EUV near-monopoly); Street "behind the curve" on 2027/28 vs a 2028 DRAM air-pocket.
MUPure-play on AI memory intensity (HBM sold-out CY26); debate = peak earnings vs run-rate.
MRVL#2 merchant custom-ASIC (Trainium/Maia) + optical DSP; debate = hyperscaler concentration + 1.6T share.
INTCBinary IDM turnaround; 18A yielding, but the equity is a call option on the first big external foundry customer.
IFX#1 power/auto-semi IDM; cyclical recovery + 800V AI-datacenter-power ramp (NVIDIA anchor); bear = DC-power share loss.

AI — Hyperscalers & Software

MSFTAI ARR >$37B/+123%; swing is capex (CY27 ~$276B) vs Azure revenue + OpenAI concentration.
AMZNAWS re-acceleration (+28%) + in-house silicon (Trainium); debate = $200B capex/ROIC.
GOOGFull-stack compounder (Search +19%, Cloud +63%); debate is valuation, not fundamentals.
METAFastest grower in Mag7 ex-GPU; bear = capital intensity and negative incremental ROIC.
ORCLTurned into an AI-infra hyperscaler (RPO $638B/+363%); bear = dot-com-peak capex/sales + dilution.
NOWBeat-and-raise (cRPO +21%) + agentic pivot to consumption; on-disk transcripts are paraphrased (fidelity).

AI labs (private)

ANTHROPICPrivate frontier lab (Claude); enterprise/coding leadership + Amazon/Google compute; ~$40B+ ARR. No public financials.
OPENAIPrivate frontier lab (ChatGPT/GPT); scale + distribution vs cash burn + sub-50% share; Microsoft-anchored. No public financials.

Semiconductor equipment (semicap)

AMATBroadest-line front-end WFE; share-gainer into a $200B/$250B '27-'28 WFE cycle; bear = memory beta + crowded positioning.
KLACProcess-control near-monopoly (~7x peers); only semicap to guide CY27 WFE > CY26; back-half-of-cycle revision story.
TERCleanest back-end proxy for AI test intensity (~70% AI); bear = ~2-customer concentration + H1-weighted book.
VECOSpecialty process equipment (LSA / ion-beam / MBE / MOCVD); now an Axcelis all-stock merger-arb / ACLS proxy.
AIXANear-monopoly compound-semi MOCVD (~100% InP, >85% GaN); AI-optical inflection (Jefferies Buy €73); thin internal coverage.
LRCXEtch/deposition WFE — completes the AMAT/KLAC/LRCX trio; most memory-capex-levered (NAND/HBM).

Memory & storage

SAMSUNGCheapest way to own the memory super-cycle; HBM4 catch-up vs SK Hynix + foundry optionality. ₩, no SEC.
SKHYNIX#1 HBM, NVDA's primary supplier, sold out through 2026; bear = HBM4 commoditization. ₩, no SEC.
KIOXIACleanest listed NAND/SSD-cycle proxy; no DRAM/HBM offset on the downturn. ¥, no SEC, IPO'd 2024.
SNDKPost-spin pure-play NAND on AI storage; price-driven blowout + $42B LTA backlog vs blowoff-top risk.
WDCPure-HDD post-SanDisk-spin; nearline exabyte growth + HAMR; the "1B" to Seagate's "1A".
STXHAMR/Mozaic front-runner in a supply-constrained nearline cycle; pricing escalators into CY27/28.

Analog & processors

ADIHighest-quality large-cap analog; industrial recovery + datacenter-power (Empower); crowded/premium.
TXNCrowded analog long; 300mm capex→FCF inflection + 800V DC-power share; valuation the bear (GS Sell).
QCOMHigh-FCF handset franchise eroding (Apple modem); re-rating hinges on auto/IoT/PC diversification.
ARMUpstream IP toll on every SoC; ARMv9/CSS royalty + >$100B server-CPU TAM; own-silicon pivot the risk. 20-F filer.
NXPIHighest auto-beta analog/embedded; content-per-vehicle + datacenter doubling; concentration the risk.
MCHPTextbook deep-cyclical MCU/analog recovery; GM 54%→62%; supply-gated, already re-rated.
ONBottoming auto/SiC power IDM + AI-DC-power (800V) kicker; share not TAM (Citi Neutral vs TXN).

Optical / Networking

ANETBest-positioned merchant AI-Ethernet name; risk = 2H26 backlog drawdown + rich multiple.
CIENOptical/DCI leader; demand outruns supply, debate purely valuation (~85x NTM).
COHRInP chokepoint in AI interconnect; NVIDIA stake + CPO deal; bear is relative vs LITE.
LITEUS photonics pure-play (rev +90%); demand isn't the question, supply/execution is.
AAOIVolatile small-cap with in-house InP fab; 2027 projection trading on FinTwit conviction.
GLWRe-rate to AI-infra (Optical +36%, Meta/Nvidia LTAs); bear = valuation + CPO payoff 2028+.
CSCOIncumbent re-rating to AI-infra (orders +35%); bear prefers ANET, flags pull-forward.
CRDOAI-connectivity pure-play — active-copper (AEC) + SerDes/optical DSP; ~68% GM, hyperscaler-concentrated.
ALABScale-up connectivity (PCIe/CXL retimers + Scorpio fabric); >$1k content/accelerator; concentration + rich multiple.

Power & Energy

CEGOwns what the AI-power cycle is short: 24/7 carbon-free nuclear; risk is PPA timing.
VSTLargest US merchant power turning into an AI-power play; debate is pace, not direction.
TLNPJM merchant IPP (Susquehanna nuclear + AWS PPA); risk = concentration + co-location regulation.
GEVCleanest AI-power earnings-revisions story (orders +71%); bear = order peak + Wind.
PWRLabor pick-and-shovel on the grid/data-center super-cycle (backlog $48.5B).
WMBGas-midstream compounder (Transco + Power Innovation); debate = valuation + PI earnings quality.
AGXEPC pure-play on the firm-power super-cycle (backlog ~79% gas); nil coverage on disk.

Power components & datacenter infra

APHAI-interconnect compounder; 800V busbar/connector content + CommScope optical; debate = copper terminal value.
TELTE Connectivity — connector power/signal AI-DC content (AI rev →~$2.4bn FY26); auto-mix the drag.
NVTnVent — AI-DC power + liquid cooling (DC ~$1B, backlog $2.6B); NVIDIA CDU partner; order lumpiness.
VRTVertiv — cleanest AI-DC power+cooling proxy; co-designs NVIDIA 800VDC; $15B backlog; order opacity.
FLEXEMS re-rating to AI-DC power (Anord Mardix) + JetCool cooling; CPI spin Q1-CY27; margin-dilutive near-term.
WOLFWolfspeed — post-Ch11 SiC; 800V datacenter call-option but negative GM + restructuring overhang.
NVTSNavitas — GaN/SiC; binary on the NVIDIA 800V design win (rev 2027); cash burn + small-cap volatility.

Software / security / internet

PANWCybersecurity platformization land-grab + agentic SecOps; debate = organic vs M&A-flattered NGS ARR.
CRWDFalcon consolidation flywheel + Charlotte AI; net-new ARR re-accel; valuation/crowding + 2024-outage tail the bear.
CRMAgentforce $0→$1B+ ARR is the AI-monetization test case; bear = ~10-13% growth + SaaS-seat-reset (BofA UW $160).
RDDTDual engine: ads + AI data-licensing; binary on Google AI-Overviews referral-traffic cannibalization.
SHOPCommerce OS at 30%+ GMV; agentic-commerce binary (TAM-expander vs LLM-storefront disintermediation).

Systems & neoclouds

DELLAI-server integration winner ($53B backlog, $60B FY27 guide); bear = thin AI-server margins + pull-forward.
HPEMulti-segment beat + Juniper synergies; durability debate (GS bull $79 vs MS/Bernstein EW).
SMCIFastest NVIDIA rack integrator + DLC leader; thin assembly margin + governance overhang (Hold).
CRWVHighest-visibility GPU neocloud ($99B backlog) but ~$25B high-coupon debt + customer concentration.
NBISHighest-octane listed neocloud (ARR→$7-9B, NVDA stake); capex burn + circular-financing scrutiny.
CEREBRASWafer-scale fast-inference vs NVDA; OpenAI take-or-pay; UAE concentration. Recent IPO, no public consensus.

Consumer / Internet / Other

AAPLStrong franchise + memory-dislocation advantage; bear = AI catch-up + price elasticity.
NFLXClean organic algorithm (12-14% rev, 31.5% margin); near-term setup is a catalyst vacuum.
BKNGWorld's #1 OTA; binary debate on agentic LLMs (TAM expander vs disintermediation).
UBERWinner-take-most demand aggregator (~$10B FCF); everything hinges on the AV binary.
SPOTHigh-quality audio compounder; debate = AI-music monetization (Remix) vs <2% rev 2030.
TMHEV volume leader generating cash; margin compressed 3 years by tariffs/FX; thin coverage.

NVDA — NVIDIA Corporation

Wiki · generated 2026-06-18 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\NVDA (filings + transcripts + decks) · _equity_calls · _briefings\by-ticker\NVDA.md. Master index: ../INDEX.md.

🔖 Themes: 800V DC Data-Center Power · AI Data-Center Power & Nuclear · CoWoS / Advanced Packaging · Custom ASIC / TPU (vs merchant GPU) · HBM / Memory Super-Cycle · Optical / CPO (Co-Packaged Optics & AI Interconnect) · Tokenmaxxing / AI Monetization

Snapshot

The accelerated-computing platform that sits at the center of the AI buildout — GPUs, CPUs (Grace/Vera), NVLink + Spectrum-X/InfiniBand networking, and the CUDA software moat sold as full-stack "AI factories." Data Center is ~90%+ of revenue ($75B of $82B in Q1 FY27); the rest is Gaming, Pro Viz, and Automotive/physical AI. From Q1 FY27 NVDA re-segmented Data Center into Hyperscale (~50%) and ACIE (AI clouds, industrial, enterprise, sovereign, ~50%) plus a new Edge Computing platform. Whole thesis is leverage to hyperscaler + sovereign + neocloud capex, where NVDA captures the compute, networking, and increasingly the CPU.

Current state (latest quarter)

Q1 FY27 (2026-05-20): Revenue $82B, +85% y/y / +20% q/q — 3rd straight quarter of y/y acceleration, 14th straight q/q gain. Data Center $75B (+92% y/y); compute $60B, networking $15B (nearly tripled y/y). Hyperscale $38B (+12% q/q); ACIE $37B (+31% q/q, AI cloud >3x y/y); sovereign +80% y/y. Non-GAAP GM 75% (flat). Record FCF $49B (vs $35B in Q4). Returned record $20B to holders; dividend raised $0.01→$0.25, new $80B buyback on top of $39B remaining; ~50% of FCF to be returned this year.

  • Guide: Q2 FY27 revenue $91B ±2%, growth led by Data Center. GM mid-70s for FY. No China DC compute in the outlook (H200 licenses approved but no revenue recognized).
  • Roadmap: Vera Rubin production shipments begin 2H, starting Q3 (Oct qtr), ramping into Q4/Q1; mgmt expects every cloud/model builder to deploy it. Reiterated $1T Blackwell+Rubin revenue visibility cal-25 through cal-27. New Vera standalone CPU = $200B TAM, with ~$20B CPU revenue visibility this year (Vivek Arya Q&A: the $20B is standalone). Jensen frames AI infra spend reaching $3–4T/yr by end of decade; hyperscale capex >$1T in 2027.
  • Q4 FY26 (2026-02-25) context: rev $68B (+73%), FY26 DC $194B (+68%), FY26 FCF $97B; ~9 GW Blackwell deployed; SBC now in non-GAAP from Q1 FY27.

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: Joe Moore (MS) — Top Pick since March, OW PT $288 (briefing 2026-06-03; 2026-06-16 bus-tour call): "definitive best product on the market" shipping in weeks, best token-per-watt / longest asset life / best AI-factory ROI; trades mid-teens on next year vs speculative AI names that have outperformed — value setup, multiple-compression-elsewhere thesis. Arcuri (UBS, 2026-05-21 recap call): supply (PCs + inventory) at $145B; purchase-commitment build all pushed to FY28/29 → enough supply to do ~$130B revenue exiting the year, well above Street; GM mid-70s defensible into FY28 even as BOM shifts to ~2x-pricier HBM4E (possible HBM consignment helps). DC business out-grew hyperscale capex for 5 straight quarters. Vera CPU genuinely incremental (agentic harnesses run on CPU). Briefings: Jensen "2H next year so much bigger than this year" (dnystedt 2026-06-09); memory shortage to "persist several years."
  • Bear: Vital Knowledge / Crisafulli (briefing 2026-06-11): low PE = market doubting demand sustainability — Oracle F27 capex/sales >100%, hyperscalers can't generate FCF, "picks-and-shovels earning a fortune but PEs low"; price war in a capital-intensive industry is "toxic." Barclays/Rand (2026-06-15): NVDA still the "popular funding short", persistently lagging SOX/AMD, "hard to find a high-conviction catalyst." Chanos (2026-06-13) needling the GPU-DC cost economics. Structural: ASIC share erosion (GOOG TPU/AVGO, Anthropic/OpenAI custom silicon long-term), Arcuri's caveat that NVDA is still correlated to y/y revenue growth which is peaking ~FQ2 → potential sentiment headwind. China DC TAM structurally impaired (Huawei 80%+ stack target, export controls).
  • Where the sell-side/buy-side stands: MS OW $288 Top Pick · UBS (Arcuri) constructive, house bull on supply · DB (Seymore) Hold PT $255 on valuation (2026-06-02) · UBS house PT $285 (Abrams) · Redburn/Rothschild Buy (2026-06-06, dismisses Rubin DRAM "mis-reading"). On the CPU $20B number Arcuri cuts it to ~$5B "apples-to-apples" chip equivalent (rest is systems + DRAM), so don't over-extrapolate the TAM. Joe Moore pushes back on the Intel/AMD ratio framing — agentic CPU is a new vector, not GPU-correlated. Broad buy-the-dip consensus post-AVGO selloff; NVDA the "cleaner AI infra name."

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q2 FY27 print: 2026-08-26 (guided $91B). Watch hyperscale vs hyperscale-capex growth under new segmentation (Arcuri: becomes a debate if NVDA ever under-grows it).
  • Vera Rubin production ramp Q3 (Oct qtr) — slope vs GB300 (Colette: too early to call); NVL72 October ramp.
  • Stockholder meeting 2026-06-24. $20B+ benchmark bond (first HG bond in ~5 yrs; 424B5 filed mid-June) — read as AI-capex confidence.
  • China: whether any H200 imports actually clear; longer-term Huawei/domestic substitution.
  • Supply-chain bottlenecks to monitor: CoWoS (180k/220k wpm by end-27/28E, Jefferies), HVLP4 copper foil / T-glass / PTFE backplane shortages, HBM4E pricing, optical (COHR/LITE locked to 2028), 800V DC pulled into Q3 2026.

Risks

  • Demand sustainability / AI-capex ROI — hyperscaler + neocloud FCF and financing dependence (Crisafulli, Chanos).
  • Customer concentration — top-5 cloud/hyperscalers ~50%+ of DC revenue; large direct/indirect customer exposure.
  • Supply concentration — TSMC (N3/N2 + CoWoS), HBM (SK Hynix/Samsung/Micron), advanced packaging are gating; "supply-constrained throughout life of Vera Rubin."
  • Competition — custom ASIC (GOOG TPU, AWS Trainium, AVGO/MRVL), AMD MI-series, inference-specialist silicon (Cerebras/Groq).
  • China / export controls — China DC compute zeroed in guidance; geopolitical and licensing risk.
  • Valuation/sentiment — "funding short" status; correlation to decelerating y/y growth as comps peak.

Consensus estimates (BBG)

BBG consensus, as of 2026-06-19 · figures in $bn (EPS in $, margin %). Green = street-high (capex high = max spend). CY = calendar-year (BBG actuals for reported quarters + consensus for the rest; fiscal quarters snapped to nearest calendar quarter).

AVGO — Broadcom Inc.

Wiki · generated 2026-06-18 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\AVGO (filings + transcripts) · _equity_calls · _briefings\by-ticker\AVGO.md. Master index: ../INDEX.md.

🔖 Themes: CoWoS / Advanced Packaging · Custom ASIC / TPU (vs merchant GPU) · Optical / CPO (Co-Packaged Optics & AI Interconnect)

Snapshot

Two-segment infrastructure-tech compounder. Semiconductor Solutions (~68% of rev in Q2 FY26) is now dominated by AI semis — custom XPUs/ASICs co-designed for hyperscalers/frontier labs plus the merchant AI-networking franchise (Tomahawk switches, Jericho fabric, 200/400G SerDes, 1.6T DSPs, CPO/optics). Infrastructure Software (~32%, GM ~93%) is VMware/VCF + legacy CA/Symantec, a sticky ARR annuity. The investment case is almost entirely AI-ASIC: AVGO is the de-facto primary silicon partner to Google TPU (12-yr relationship, 14 ASICs to date), plus Anthropic, OpenAI, Meta MTIA, and two unnamed customers (JPM believes ByteDance + Alibaba; per Harlan Sur, 2026-06-05). It also designs custom XPUs at TSMC and depends on outsourced foundry/HBM/substrate supply (FY25 10-K).

Estado atual (Q2 FY26, reported 2026-06-03)

  • Rev $22.2B (+48% y/y), EPS $2.44 vs Street $22.1B/$2.39 — beat consensus but below buyside bogeys (~$11.5B AI / ~$18.5B Jul-Q AI). GM 77.1%, op margin record 67.3%, EBITDA 69%. FCF $10.3B (Q2 FY26 earnings, 2026-06-03).
  • AI semis $10.8B (+143% y/y); networking ~40% of AI rev — Hock flagged 40% as "the ceiling," normalizing toward ~30%. AI bookings >$30B vs $10.8B shipped; visibility "all the way to 2028."
  • Q3 FY26 guide: total rev $29.4B (+84% y/y, above Street $28.6B); AI semis $16.0B (Street $16–17B, buyside ~$18.5B — the clear miss); GM down ~310bps q/q to ~74% on ASIC mix; op margin held at 67%.
  • FY26 AI semis $56B (~+180% y/y). FY27 AI reiterated ">$100B" ("easily exceed") — no raise vs Street ~$116B; that omission, not the print, drove the -12.6% reaction (briefing 2026-06-06).
  • New: 20GW XPU platform (Apollo/Blackstone SPV) financing compute for Anthropic + OpenAI through 2028; first $35B tranche launching. ~10GW shipments in FY27, back-half loaded, with "a lot more gigawatts" in FY28 (Hock, Q2 FY26). Hock: "No racks… all a chip business only" — content/GW rises as CPU cores/HBM embed into XPUs (agentic).

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: FY28 is the real story. JPM Harlan Sur (2026-06-05 / 2026-06-17 calls): ~20–22GW deploy in FY28 → $250–300B AI rev, ~$35–38 EPS vs cons ~$25. TPU V8i "Sunfish" = ~70% of Google's FY27 TPU demand, ~6.5M units ≈ ~$100B alone; +~$45B networking. The April 5-yr (through 2031) Google LTA carries rising guaranteed annual revenues and spans next 4 TPU gens (V8–V11); V9 2nm (4 compute die / 16 HBM) "no delay," ramps C28. Citi (Malik) #1 semis pick 2026, PT $500, AI →~81% of sales by F4Q28 (briefing 2026-05-12). Internal Capstone model (Felipe, 2026-06-08): 2026E rev $115B / 2027E $190B / 2028E $315B; Anthropic largest AI client by GW in 2028.
  • Bear: (1) FY27 not raised despite better visibility → the binary debate (Street $116B vs buyside $150B+). (2) GM compression — ASIC mix drags consolidated GM toward low-70s; HBM pass-through pricing an added headwind (Jefferies Curtis, 2026-06-04). (3) Google diversification — Hock conceded "diversity of sources"; the CLT/MediaTek "Zerafish" training chip exists, though Sur argues AVGO keeps the higher-value inferencing roadmap and the COT chip "still needs optimization/respins." (4) Power/datacenter readiness is now the binding constraint on multi-GW greenfield ramps (SIG/Susquehanna callback, 2026-06-03) — Sur reads this as why Hock won't raise FY27. (5) Independent caution: EnerTuition "Competitive Risk Starting To Materialize" (2026-06-07).
  • Where the sell-side stands: Unanimous Buy/OW post-print, PT range $485–$580. JPM (Sur) OW $580; Barclays (O'Malley) OW $500 (desk Rand: AVGO a "funding short" near-term); Bernstein (Rasgon) OP $550, cut multiple 30x→25x; MS (Moore) OW $502 (prefers NVDA); Jefferies (Curtis) Buy $550; DB (Seymore) Buy $515; UBS (Arcuri) Buy $485; Citi (Malik) Buy $500. FinTwit @ParadisLabs (2026-06-15) notes a de-rate to 16.2x 2028E, planning to add.

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q3 FY26 print: Wednesday, Sep 2, 2026 AMC (call 2pm PT) — watch AI-semi print vs $16B guide, GM trajectory, and any FY27 AI raise.
  • Sunfish/V8i volume ramp (shipping ~now), Tomahawk 6 (102T) ramp into Oct-Q (margin-accretive), 200T switch tape-out this quarter.
  • First $35B Apollo SPV tranche execution; Anthropic FY27 ~3.5GW, OpenAI 1.3GW, Meta 1GW initial (deliveries 2H FY27).
  • Advanced packaging: Singapore substrate ramps Aug; in-house advanced packaging (Applied Materials Singapore) 2H FY27 (Sur, 2026-06-05).
  • V9 2nm milestones / any COT (MediaTek) progress signals on Google share.

Risks

  • Customer concentration: top-five end customers a significant share of net revenue (FY25 10-K); AI rev increasingly tied to a handful of frontier labs — Sur flags AVGO is more exposed to OpenAI/Anthropic frontier-lab risk than NVDA (2026-06-17).
  • Supply chain: dependence on a limited number of contract manufacturers/foundries (TSMC), HBM and substrate (FY25 10-K); back-end packaging cited as the FY27 supply constraint (UBS Arcuri, 2026-06-04).
  • Margin mix: structural GM dilution as low-margin ASICs scale faster than software/networking.
  • Share loss / COT: Google CLT-team (MediaTek) optionality; longer-term COT-model bypass risk (Jukan, briefing 2026-05-18).
  • Macro/AI-capex cyclicality & power availability gating multi-GW deployments; geopolitical/China (InP, export controls).

Consensus estimates (BBG)

AMD — Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.

Wiki · generated 2026-06-18 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\AMD (filings + transcripts + decks) · _equity_calls · _briefings\by-ticker\AMD.md. Master index: ../INDEX.md.

🔖 Themes: CoWoS / Advanced Packaging · Tokenmaxxing / AI Monetization

Snapshot

Fabless designer of x86 CPUs, GPUs and adaptive/embedded silicon, fabbed at TSMC (2nm/5nm) with some GF, UMC, Samsung capacity. Four segments: Data Center (EPYC server CPUs + Instinct AI GPUs — ~56% of revenue and the entire growth story), Client & Gaming (Ryzen PCs + Radeon + semi-custom consoles), and Embedded (Xilinx FPGA + adaptive SoC, highest-margin business at ~38-40% segment OM). Competes head-to-head with Intel (x86), Nvidia (AI GPU), Arm-based merchant/custom CPUs, and the custom-ASIC complex (Broadcom/Marvell). The thesis is fully an AI-infrastructure story: AMD is the credible #2 merchant GPU and the share-gaining merchant server CPU into an agentic-AI demand pull (10-K FY25, 2026-02-04).

Current state (latest quarter)

Q1 FY26 (2026-05-06): Revenue $10.3B, +38% y/y (beat high end of guide), flat q/q. GAAP-equiv diluted EPS $1.37, +43% y/y. Non-GAAP gross margin 55% (+170bps y/y); op income $2.5B, 25% OM; record FCF $2.6B (25% of revenue). Cash/STI $12.3B.

  • Data Center record $5.8B, +57% y/y / +7% q/q; segment OM 28%. Server CPU 4th consecutive record, >50% y/y (Turin crossed >50% of server mix). Data Center AI down modestly q/q purely on China step-down ($390M MI308 in Q4 → ~$100M Q1, per Q4 FY25 call 2026-02-03); ex-China not material per Jean Hu.
  • Client & Gaming $3.6B, +23% y/y (Client +26% to $2.9B; Gaming +11% to $720M); segment OM 16%.
  • Embedded $873M, +6% y/y; segment OM 39%.

Guidance Q2 FY26: Revenue ~$11.2B ±$300M (+46% y/y, +9% q/q), GM ~56%, opex ~$3.3B, tax 13%, ~1.66B shares. Server CPU guided >70% y/y in Q2, expected to persist through 2H. 2H gaming guided to decline >20% vs 1H on memory/component-cost demand drag; 2H PC shipments also planned lower for the same reason, though Client still grows y/y. MI450/Helios ramps Q3 (initial) → significant Q4, into 2027.

Raised framing (Q1 FY26 call): Server CPU TAM lifted to >$120B by 2030 at >35% CAGR (vs the ~$60B/~18% put out at the Nov-2025 FAD just six months earlier), driven by agentic AI orchestration / CPU-per-GPU ratios moving toward 1:1. Long-term targets reiterated from FAD (2025-11-11): >35% revenue CAGR, DC segment >60% CAGR, GM 55-58%, "tens of billions" of AI revenue in 2027, and >$20 EPS over the strategic horizon.

Debate / thesis

  • Bull:
    • CPU is the underappreciated upside, and it carries the margins. Server CPU >70% y/y in Q2, sustainable through 2H, mostly unit-driven (~2/3 units, 1/3 ASP) at 60-65%+ gross margins vs lower-margin GPU+HBM (AMD callback, 2026-05-06). Joe Moore (MS, 2026-06-16) "very enthused" on Venice (2nm, ramps 2H), views agentic CPU as incremental net-new demand, not a GPU ratio, and flags an x86 security edge for enterprise agents over Arm.
    • GPU optionality on top of an under-owned stock. Each gigawatt deal ≈ $15-20B; OpenAI + Meta ≈ 2GW of the ~3GW in play for 2027; potential additional customer(s) (Anthropic per Citi checks, 2026-05-18) could add big chunks. AI revenue path $14-15B FY26 → $30-40B+ FY27. Citi (Atif Malik) upgraded to Buy, PT $575, ~+12-13% above Street CY26-28E, SOTP-based (2026-06-13). Bernstein (Rasgon) Outperform PT $600 (2026-06-17). Stock owned by only ~1-in-3 large-caps at ~0.41x relative weight — structurally under-owned (BofA, AMD callback 2026-05-06).
    • GS upgraded to Buy $410 (2026-05-06), BofA Buy $450, MS OW $410 — broad sell-side reset on the agentic-CPU thesis.
  • Bear:
    • GPU is still "show-me." MI450 is AMD's first rack-scale product; Nvidia took ~5-6 months to ramp first Blackwell. Joe Moore (2026-06-16) "still in a show-me" on GPU with the stock at ~45x next year. JPM (Sur) Neutral PT $385 vs stock ~$511 — explicitly cautious on valuation (2026-06-15).
    • OpenAI/Meta warrant dilution: ~26M shares per gigawatt (~160M shares per 6GW deal); ~50M incremental shares modeled into 2027 and again 2028. Final OpenAI tranche only vests at $600/share (AMD callback, 2026-05-06).
    • Customer-concentration / allocation risk: OpenAI is simultaneously tied to Nvidia, Broadcom, Cerebras and Arm; how it splits 2027-28 share is unknown (expert call, 2026-04-22). Supply chain tight (HBM, CoWoS-L, wafers); 2H Client/Gaming demand hit by memory inflation.
    • Q4 FY26 GM steps down ~100-150bps on MI450 mix (below corporate avg), though management sounded notably less worried about it on the Q1 call than historically.
  • Where the sell-side stands: Skewed bullish post-Q1. Buy/OW: Citi $575, Bernstein $600, GS $410, BofA $450, MS OW $410, Jefferies Buy $300, Barclays OW. Neutral/cautious: JPM $385. Debate is no longer the CPU (broad consensus AMD has the stronger roadmap vs Intel) — it's GPU execution + valuation (~45x NTM) + warrant dilution.

Catalysts / what to watch

  • "Advancing AI" event, July 2026 — next-gen Instinct (MI450/Helios detail), Venice/Verano EPYC, customer engagements; possible additional GPU customer announcement (Anthropic speculated).
  • Venice (Zen 6, 2nm) launch later in 2026 + Verano AI-optimized EPYC; sustainability of >70% server CPU growth into 2H/2027.
  • MI450/Helios production ramp — initial Q3, significant Q4; lead-customer forecasts already above plan; watch rack-scale revenue recognition (recognized on rack-builder shipment).
  • Q2 FY26 print (early Aug) against the $11.2B guide; gross-margin trajectory Q3→Q4 with MI450 mix-in.
  • China MI308 license dynamics (export-control dependent); HPE/Lenovo Helios systems in 2026; MI500 (cDNA6, 2nm, HBM4e) 2027.

Risks

  • Intense competition vs Intel (improving x86 supply / Diamond Rapids, Coral Rapids pulled to mid-2027) and Nvidia (Vera Rubin), plus merchant/custom Arm and ASIC scale-up (10-K FY25).
  • Foundry concentration on TSMC for leading-edge HPC/AI wafers + advanced packaging (CoWoS-L) — supply tightness in HBM/wafers a gating risk to the ramp.
  • Customer concentration in Data Center AI (OpenAI/Meta) and uncertain hyperscaler allocation of 2027-28 GPU share.
  • Export-control / China revenue volatility (MI308/MI325 licensing "dynamic").
  • Memory/component cost inflation dragging 2H Client + Gaming demand; console cycle in year 7 (semi-custom down significant double-digits in 2026).
  • Warrant-related share dilution; GPU gross margin dilutive during ramp.

Consensus estimates (BBG)

TSM — Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company

Wiki · generated 2026-06-18 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\TSM (20-F + transcripts + decks) · _equity_calls · _briefings\by-ticker\TSM.md. Master index: ../INDEX.md.

🔖 Themes: CoWoS / Advanced Packaging · Semicap / WFE (Wafer-Fab Equipment)

Snapshot

World's largest pure-play foundry, ~70% of leading-edge foundry share (TSMC former exec, 2026-04-14). Sells wafer capacity to fabless logic designers (NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom, Apple, Qualcomm, MediaTek) plus advanced packaging (CoWoS, SoIC). No products competing with customers — the structural moat per the former exec call. Mix has flipped to compute: in FY25 HPC was 58% of revenue (+48% y/y), smartphone 29%, IoT/auto/DCE the rest; advanced nodes (≤7nm) were 74% of wafer revenue, up from 69% in 2024 (Q4 FY25, 2026-01-15). AI accelerators (GPU/ASIC/HBM controllers — excludes data-center CPU) were "high teens %" of total revenue in 2025 (Q4 FY25). The 20-F (filed 2026-04-16) reports FY25 revenue of TWD 3.8tn / USD 122bn, +35.9% in USD.

Estado atual (Q1 FY26, 2026-04-16)

Revenue USD 35.9bn, +6.4% q/q, above the high end of guidance. GM 66.2% (+390bp q/q), OpM 58.1% (+410bp), EPS TWD 22.08, ROE 40.5% (Q1 FY26). Mix: HPC +20% q/q to 61% of revenue, smartphone −11% to 26%. N3 = 25% of wafer revenue, N5 36%, N7 13%. Capex USD 11.1bn in the quarter.

Guidance / messages (Q1 FY26):

  • Q2 FY26 revenue USD 39.0–40.2bn (+10% q/q, +32% y/y at midpoint); GM 65.5–67.5%, OpM 56.5–58.5%; Q2 tax ~20% (undistributed-earnings accrual), FY tax 17–18%.
  • FY26 revenue now guided "above 30%" in USD (raised from "close to 30%" at Q4 FY25, 2026-01-15) — "AI-related demand continues to be extremely robust… shift from generative to agentic AI is another step-up in tokens" (C.C. Wei).
  • FY26 capex revised to the high end of USD 52–56bn (i.e. ~USD 56bn); 70–80% advanced process, ~10% specialty, 10–20% packaging/test/mask (Q4 FY25). CFO: next 3 years of capex "significantly higher" than the USD 101bn of the prior 3 years (Q1 FY26).
  • N2 in HVM since Q4 FY25 with good yield, ramping at Hsinchu + Kaohsiung. N3 capacity being expanded (a first — historically TSMC doesn't add to a node post-target): new Tainan N3 fab (1H27), Arizona Fab 2 N3 (2H27), Japan Fab 2 N3 (2028), plus N5→N3 tool conversion. A14 on track for 2028.
  • Margin puts/takes: N2 ramp dilutes FY26 GM by 2–3pts; overseas fabs dilute 2–3pts early / 3–4pts later; N3 GM crosses corporate average in 2H26; Middle East situation may lift chemical/gas costs (unquantified). Long-term targets reaffirmed: GM 56%+ and ROE high-20s through cycle (Q1 FY26, raised from 53%+ a year ago).

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: Sole credible leading-edge supplier — ~70% foundry share, Samsung ~1yr behind, Intel ~1.5–2yrs behind on yield/density (TSMC former exec, 2026-04-14). Wafers remain the binding AI constraint into 2027 ("we expect this to continue to be very tight," C.C. Wei, Q1 FY26). Bernstein (Mark Li) OP, PT $430 vs ~$351, calling TSM the "most trustworthy compounder in AI" — 28% EPS CAGR thru '28, +50% EPS this year, '27 capex $68B, trading at ~20% discount to SOX (briefing, 2026-05-18). AI-accelerator revenue still guided to mid-/high-50s % CAGR 2024–29 (Bruce Lu Q, Q1 FY26). Pricing has real headroom — N3 hot-run/super-hot-run premiums rising further in Q3, regular N3 hikes ~10% and N2 ~5–6% into Jan-27 (Gokul/JPM CoWoS update, 2026-05-27).
  • Bear: Margin dilution stack is real and structural — overseas (US/Japan/Germany) wafers genuinely cost more (US labor ~30% higher) yet TSMC prices uniformly, so Arizona/JASM/ESMC ramps compress GM (former exec, 2026-04-14; CFO Q4 FY25). Pricing capped by monopoly-scrutiny fear, not just customer goodwill — "we could have 70–75% gross margins… they're worried about being called a monopoly" (former exec, 2026-04-14). Demand-double-counting risk: customers' forecasts historically sum to multiples of the real end market; TSMC itself discounts them (former exec). Consumer softness from memory price hikes hitting price-sensitive PC/smartphone (C.C. Wei, Q1 FY26). Packaging is the bottleneck shifting away from TSMC: CoWoS increasingly farmed to ASE/Amkor, EMIB (Intel) and panel-level CoPoS threaten to move value to substrate vendors (Gokul, 2026-05-27).
  • Where the sell-side stands: Bernstein OP, $430 (2026-05-18). JPM (Gokul) constructive — raising CoWoS capacity forecasts again to 115k/175k/220k WPM by end-26/27/28, total CoWoS consumption +86% this year / +30% next (2026-05-27). Consistent Street view that supply stays tight through 2027.

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q2 FY26 print (mid-July 2026) — C.C. Wei deferred the precise above-30% FY number to July ("a more precise number to share in July," Q1 FY26).
  • 2027 capacity-balance commentary ("we'll announce when we enter '27," C.C. Wei).
  • N3 GM crossover to corporate average in 2H26; N2 dilution onset in 2H26.
  • Jan-2027 annual price reset (N3 ~10%, N2 ~5–6% per JPM, 2026-05-27).
  • Arizona ramp (Fab 2 N3 in 2H27; second land parcel → ~5–7 fabs + CoWoS); A14 HVM 2028.
  • EMIB vs CoWoS-L decision on Google TPU V9 (MediaTek "Puma Fish"), expected late-2026/early-2027 (Gokul, 2026-05-27).

Risks

  • Overseas-fab GM dilution (2–3pts early → 3–4pts later) as US/Japan/Germany footprint scales (Q1 FY26; former exec).
  • Geographic/geopolitical concentration in Taiwan — earthquake + cross-strait risk is the stated rationale for diversification (former exec, 2026-04-14).
  • U.S.–China export controls (BIS entity list); TSMC self-limits to ~3 generations behind for restricted destinations (former exec).
  • Monopoly/antitrust scrutiny constrains pricing power despite sole-source position (former exec).
  • N2/A14 capex-per-wafer rising sharply (N2 CapEx/kwpm "substantially higher" than N3; A14 higher still) — capital intensity risk if revenue growth fails to outpace capex (CFO Q4 FY25; though mgmt expects revenue to keep outpacing capex, Q1 FY26).
  • Input-cost shock from Middle East (specialty chemicals/gases) — unquantified (Q1 FY26).
  • Advanced-packaging value migration to OSATs/substrate (EMIB, CoPoS) (Gokul, 2026-05-27).
  • Cyclical consumer/smartphone exposure amid memory-driven component inflation (C.C. Wei, Q1 FY26).

Consensus estimates (BBG)

ASML — ASML Holding N.V.

Wiki · generated 2026-06-18 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\ASML (20-F + transcripts) · _equity_calls · _briefings\by-ticker\ASML.md. Master index: ../INDEX.md.

🔖 Themes: Semicap / WFE (Wafer-Fab Equipment)

Snapshot

ASML is the sole supplier of EUV lithography and the dominant DUV/immersion vendor — the binding constraint of the leading-edge semiconductor supply chain. Revenue splits across system sales (EUV Low-NA + High-NA, plus non-EUV DUV/dry/immersion and Metrology & Inspection) and a high-margin recurring Installed Base Management (service + upgrades) business. FY25 net sales €32.7B: EUV €11.6B (48 systems, +39% y/y), DUV €12.0B (-6%), M&I €0.825B (+28%), IBM €8.2B (+26%); by segment Logic €16.1B (+22%), Memory €8.4B (-2%) (Q4/FY25, 2026-01-28). The franchise is a near-monopoly levered directly to the AI capex cycle via rising litho intensity in advanced Logic (3nm/2nm/A14) and DRAM (1c→1d EUV layer adoption).

Current state (latest quarter)

Q1 2026 (2026-04-15): net sales €8.8B (in guide); system sales €6.3B of which EUV >€4.1B (incl. 2 High-NA) and non-EUV >€2.1B; mix ~50/50 Logic 49% / Memory 51%. IBM €2.5B (above guide). GM 53.0% (high end, helped by very-high-margin IBM components). Net income €2.8B, 31.4% margin, EPS €7.15. FCF -€2.6B on down-payment timing. Q2 guide: sales €8.4–9.0B, GM 51–52%.

Management raised and narrowed FY26 guidance to €36–40B (from €34–39B at Q4), GM held 51–53%, H2-weighted (Q1 2026, 2026-04-15). Drivers: EUV up significantly; non-EUV now expected to grow (vs. prior "flat") as the immersion supply-chain bottleneck reversed; IBM up significantly. China share held ~20% of sales, in line with backlog (Q4/FY25, 2026-01-28). The €36–40B band is explicitly built to "accommodate potential outcomes of ongoing export-control discussions."

Capacity ramp is the story: Low-NA EUV output at least 60 systems in 2026, scaling to at least 80 in 2027 (Q1 2026, 2026-04-15) — up from 44 in 2025. Productivity uplifts lift ASPs: NXE:3800E to 230 wph (from 220), NXE:3800F to 260 wph (from 250), shipping 2027 / volume 2028; 1,000W source demo underpins 330 wph by ~2031. High-NA: >500k wafers processed, >80% availability; Intel accepted its EXE:5200B for HVM (Q4/FY25, 2026-01-28). FY25 backlog ~€38.8B; Q4 bookings €13.2B (EUV €7.4B / non-EUV €5.8B), 56% Memory. New €12B buyback to 2028; FY25 dividend €7.50 (+17%). Long-term CMD'24 frame reiterated: 2030 revenue €44–60B at 56–60% GM (Q4/FY25, 2026-01-28).

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: AI demand structurally outstrips supply through 2027+; litho is "clearly the binding constraint" (BofA/Didier, 2026-04-16). DRAM is the new leg — market changed materially vs. CMD'24, EUV layer adoption climbing 1b (~3-4) → 1c (~6-7) → 1d (~10 by ~2028); this litho-intensity thesis was the basis for BofA moving ASML to Top Pick Dec'25 (ASML callback, 2026-04-15). ASP + GM step-up locked in: D→E (~50%→~55% GM)→F tool (high-50s/low-60s GM, immersion-like) means wafer capacity more than doubles even as EUV units ~double (BofA callback, 2026-04-15). Street numbers seen as behind: JPM (Deshpande) to PT €1,900, FY27/28 rev €55.4B/€63.8B (~+17%/+22% vs cons), EPS €54.4/€64.4 (~+32/35%); 90 EUV units 2027 (briefing 2026-06-03). MS (Simpson) OW Top Pick PT €1,660; Rothschild/Redburn raised to €1,900 Buy, named top semicap pick post-SV bus tour (briefing 2026-06-17). BofA (Scemama) Top Pick €1,710, 2030 EPS €78 mid (€66-92). BofA bull-case 2030 EPS €89 without flexing GM (BofA, 2026-04-16).
  • Bear: Management cautious on pricing/GM (FY26 GM range unchanged despite richer immersion mix) — better mix offset by cost of ramping capacity; team still "traumatized" by the 2024 profit warning, rebuilding credibility (BofA callback, 2026-04-15). Near-term reaction risk: ASML's guide raise was driven by immersion + IBM upgrades, "nothing to do with EUV," so it does not by itself signal incremental EUV strength (BofA, 2026-04-16). 2028 air-pocket risk on DRAM 6F2→4F2 (4F²) transition — BofA models EUV-to-DRAM down y/y in 2028 as prudence (callback, 2026-04-15); 4F² could shift share to Lam/ASMI. Buy-side already at €43-47 FY27 EPS, so this year's raise "largely irrelevant" — "nothing wrong with ASML, just more juice elsewhere" was the bus-feedback (BofA, 2026-04-16). Europe/China valuation discount and capacity constraints flagged as the main pushbacks (briefing JPM/BofA, 2026-06-07). China cut sharply in Q1 (CXMT pulled forward to Q4'25); immersion-ban policy swings are an unmodeled wildcard (callback, 2026-04-15).
  • Where the sell-side/buy-side stands: Uniformly OW/Buy/Top Pick across the desk. JPM OW PT €1,900 (2026-06-03); MS OW Top Pick €1,660 (2026-06-03); BofA/Scemama Buy Top Pick €1,710 (2026-05-08); Rothschild/Redburn Buy €1,900 (2026-06-17). JPM (briefing 2026-06-03) explicitly: "Street behind the curve." Buy-side ASML FY27 numbers already aggressive (€45-50 EPS) — further momentum needs capacity-expansion confirmation above 80 units (Spec Sales, briefing 2026-06-03).

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q2 2026 print (mid-July): immersion recovery confirmation, IBM-margin durability, any High-NA rev-rec slippage (BofA modeled 2 tools Q3/Q4, none Q2) (callback, 2026-04-15).
  • Capital Markets Day — expected early/Q1 2027: management to formally revisit DRAM assumptions and likely raise the 2030 €44-60B frame; BofA models €70B mid-case (callback, 2026-04-15).
  • 2027 Low-NA EUV unit confirmation above 80 — the key buy-side trigger (briefing, 2026-06-03).
  • Export-control / China: Trump-Xi negotiation track; potential immersion-ban changes vs. MATCH Act counter-legislation (callback, 2026-04-15). Terafab (SPCX + TSLA US fab JV, $55B+) — ASML CEO calls Musk "very serious"; potential incremental tool demand (briefings 2026-06-07/08).
  • High-NA HVM insertion (Intel A14A ~2029; first Logic layer adoption at TSMC A14 ~2028).

Risks

  • Customer concentration: sells to "a relatively small number of customers"; 3 largest = 35.4% of receivables; heightened by industry consolidation (20-F, 2026-02-25).
  • Single-source supply: Carl Zeiss SMT is sole supplier of optics under an exclusive arrangement; many EUV parts single-sourced / not economically multi-sourceable (20-F, 2026-02-25).
  • Export controls / geopolitics: China policy (immersion/DUV) directly governs ~20% of sales; outcomes embedded in FY26 guide bandwidth (20-F, 2026-02-25; Q1 2026).
  • Cyclicality + concentrated demand: WFE cyclicality; AI-capex dependence — a hyperscaler capex air-pocket would propagate to DRAM/Logic litho orders.
  • 2028 DRAM 4F² transition air-pocket; High-NA HVM timing slippage; FCF lumpiness on down-payment timing.

Consensus estimates (BBG)

MU — Micron Technology

Wiki · generated 2026-06-18 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\MU (filings + transcripts + decks) · _equity_calls · _briefings\by-ticker\MU.md. Master index: ../INDEX.md.

🔖 Themes: HBM / Memory Super-Cycle · Semicap / WFE (Wafer-Fab Equipment)

Snapshot

Micron is one of three scaled DRAM makers (with Samsung and SK Hynix) and a top-tier NAND supplier, selling DRAM/NAND/NOR under the Micron and Crucial brands (FY25 10-K, 2025-10-03). DRAM is ~79% of revenue, NAND ~20-21% (Q2 FY26, 2026-03-18). The franchise is reorganized around four data-center-centric BUs: Cloud Memory (CMBU), Core Data Center (CDBU), Mobile & Client (MCBU), and Auto & Embedded (AEBU). The thesis hinges on memory being repositioned "from a system component to a strategic asset" for AI — Micron supplies the full memory hierarchy that agentic inference consumes: HBM next to the accelerator, LPDDR/DDR5/SoCAM expansion memory on the CPU, and high-capacity QLC/TLC data-center SSDs for the growing KV-cache/context tier (Sathya Kumar via UBS Arcuri call, 2026-05-15). It is the #2-3 HBM player behind SK Hynix; only credible third source on HBM4 given Samsung's product issues.

Current state (latest quarter)

Q2 FY26 (reported 2026-03-18) — a blowout. Revenue $23.9B, +75% q/q, +196% y/y (4th straight record). DRAM $18.8B (+74% q/q), pricing up "mid-sixties percent" sequentially; NAND $5.0B (+82% q/q), pricing up "high-seventies percent." Consolidated gross margin 75% (+18pts q/q); operating margin 69%; non-GAAP EPS $12.20. FCF a record $6.9B; net cash $6.5B (highest ever); dividend +30% to $0.15. Supply remains the binding constraint — management can fill "only 50% to two-thirds" of key-customer demand (Mehrotra, 2026-03-18, reiterating Q1).

Guidance — Q3 FY26 (reports June 24): revenue $33.5B ±$750M, GM ~81% (+600bps q/q), EPS $19.15 ±$0.40, capex ~$7.0B; FCF to "roughly double sequentially" (Murphy). FY26 capex raised to >$25B (was $20B at Q1, 2025-12-17); FY27 to step up "meaningfully" with >$10B incremental construction capex y/y.

Structural setup (Q1 FY26, 2025-12-17): entire CY26 HBM supply — including HBM4 — sold out on price and volume. HBM TAM ~$35B (2025) → ~$100B by 2028 (~40% CAGR), pulled in two years vs prior. CY26 industry DRAM and NAND bit-shipment growth capped at ~20% by supply; Micron grows in line. 1γ DRAM and G9 NAND become majority bit mix by mid-CY26. First five-year Strategic Customer Agreement (SCA) signed — multiyear, specific volume/price commitments, distinct from the old one-year LTAs; in discussion with multiple customers.

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: This is a durable up-cycle, not a normal one. DB (Melissa Weathers, Buy) raised PT 2026-06-17: "DRAM tightness extends to 2028+; agentic AI drives memory intensity." GS (Schneider) sees "ongoing tightness through CY27; significant upside to Street estimates" (briefing 2026-06-09). The marquee bull is UBS Arcuri (Buy, PT $1,625, raised from $535) on a "path to >$100 EPS," LTAs locking ~30% of industry DDR bits and ~$400bn FCF C27-29E (briefings 2026-05-26 / 2026-05-31). Citi (Atif Malik, Buy, PT $840) re-rated to 8x C27 EPS from 5x (2026-05-19); MS reiterates bull case PT $1,050 (2026-06-04). Mechanism: HBM's 3:1 DDR5 die trade-off + node-driven bit decline + multi-year cleanroom lead times keep supply structurally short while LTAs/SCAs convert the equity into an earnings-durability story (JPM memory-China call, 2026-05-28). CPU upside is incremental fuel — NVIDIA Vera standalone CPU ~500k units this year, ~3M next year, could lift total server DRAM demand >15-20% (JPM/Gokul, 2026-05-28).
  • Bear: Memory is still a commodity and the cycle always turns. The sharpest bear voice on disk is the ex-VP Procurement (Micron), 2026-06-10: the market is pricing peak earnings as a sustainable level — "a highly catastrophic assumption." His view: HBM differentiation is temporary (once a second/third vendor is qualified at each customer it commoditizes like DRAM), Micron's cost structure is worse than Samsung's and roughly at SK Hynix, and "your $900 stock right now will become a $50 stock again" once new fabs (Micron/SK/Samsung) bring disruptive capacity online ~a year out — possibly normalizing "within this calendar year or early next." He frames the AI buildout as a bull-whip ("beer game") several layers removed from end demand that can't amortize ~$6-700B of infra spend. Second-order: customers are being forced to qualify Chinese suppliers (CXMT) on cheap DRAM Micron won't supply — "once they're in, they're in." UBS's own desk called the Arcuri PT "the most controversial UBS note recently" (briefing 2026-05-31).
  • Where the sell-side stands: Near-unanimous bullish — DB Buy (raised PT, 2026-06-17), GS PT up (2026-06-09), MS PT $1,050 (2026-06-04), UBS Arcuri Buy $1,625 (2026-05-26/31), Citi Buy $840 (2026-05-19), Susquehanna $1,750 (2026-05-30), JPM OW (TMC recap, 2026-05-21). MU +30% on the week and +9.9% in a single session into June (briefings). The debate is no longer direction but duration — the bull case explicitly underwrites tightness "beyond CY26" / into 2028; the bear case is that this is a textbook top.

Catalysts / what to watch

  • F3Q26 print — June 24, 2026. Street rev $34.7B vs guide $33.5B; FY27/28 EPS pegged $111.3/$115.7 (briefing 2026-06-12). Watch GM trajectory past 81%, HBM4 ramp commentary, and any SCA disclosures.
  • HBM4 volume ramp in CQ2 2026 for NVIDIA Vera Rubin; HBM4E (custom base die) into CY27.
  • Additional SCA signings / disclosure of structure (floor pricing, cancellation terms) — the re-rating catalyst.
  • Capacity: Idaho fab 1 first wafers mid-CY27 (pulled in); Idaho 2 by 2028; New York groundbreaking early CY26; Singapore HBM packaging contributing CY27, NAND fab CY28.
  • EUV / tool availability as a 2028 supply governor (JPM: ASML ~90 tools end-CY27 insufficient for industry; Micron only recently adopting EUV at 1γ — call 2026-05-28).

Risks

  • ASP volatility / cyclicality — 10-K flags "significant volatility in average selling prices" and cash-flow/operating-result volatility that can pressure the credit rating (FY25 10-K).
  • Commodity normalization — capacity from Micron/SK/Samsung new fabs landing ~CY27-28 could break the price structure (ex-VP, 2026-06-10).
  • Cost-structure disadvantage vs Samsung (geographically dispersed footprint); roughly at SK Hynix (ex-VP, 2026-06-10).
  • Geopolitics/regulation — export restrictions, tariffs, trade measures; China DRAM substitution via CXMT gaining low-end LPDDR share (10-K; JPM 2026-05-28).
  • HBM share/competition — SK Hynix leadership (NVIDIA multiyear tech partnership), Samsung HBM4 qualifying; customers dual-source by design.
  • Demand elasticity in price-sensitive PC/smartphone/consumer segments at elevated ASPs (Mehrotra, 2025-12-17).

Consensus estimates (BBG)

MRVL — Marvell Technology, Inc.

Wiki · generated 2026-06-19 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\MRVL (filings + transcripts) · _equity_calls · _briefings\by-ticker\MRVL.md (none on disk). Master index: 00_INDEX.md. Themes: custom-asic-tpu · optical-cpo.

Snapshot

Fabless data-infrastructure semiconductor supplier "spanning the data center core to the network edge" — core IP in complex SoCs integrating analog, mixed-signal, DSP, electro-optics and silicon photonics (FY26 10-K, 2026-03-11). One reportable segment; the data center end market is 74% of revenue ($6.10B of $8.19B in FY26, +46% y/y) with comms/enterprise/carrier the rest after the August-2025 divestiture of the automotive-Ethernet unit to Infineon for $2.5B (FY26 10-K). Three AI franchises drive the story: custom compute ASICs/XPUs for hyperscalers (the #2 merchant custom-silicon partner behind AVGO), electro-optics (1.6T PAM DSPs, 2nm coherent DCI, now CPO via Celestial AI), and data-center switching/interconnect (51.2T/100T, AEC/retimers, CXL via XConn). Highly concentrated: top-10 customers = 82% of FY26 revenue; one direct customer 14%, one distributor 37% (FY26 10-K).

Position in the value chain

MRVL is a fabless designer sitting between the foundry/IP/memory supply base and the hyperscalers. It owns no fabs — it co-designs custom ASICs and electro-optic/DSP/switching silicon, manufactured at TSMC, and sells the bulk of its data-center output to the four US hyperscalers (AWS Trainium, Microsoft Maia roadmaps it owns that AVGO does not; per JPM Sur, 2026-06-17). It is the #2 merchant custom-silicon partner behind Broadcom, levered to (a) hyperscaler ASIC insourcing and (b) the copper-to-optical interconnect transition.

Current state (latest quarter)

Q1 FY27 (reported 2026-05-27, quarter ended May 2, 2026):

  • Revenue $2.418B, +9% q/q, +28% y/y (10-Q, 2026-05-28; transcript 2026-05-27); data center $1.8B = 76% of total, +27% y/y, driven by AI demand (data-center end market +27% per 10-Q).
  • Non-GAAP EPS $0.80 (+29% y/y), ~$0.05 above the ~$0.75 Street; non-GAAP GM 58.9%, GAAP GM 52.1% (GAAP gross profit $1,260.8M on $2,417.5M).
  • Custom silicon: >20% growth in FY27, more than double in FY28; management gave new "line of sight to >$10B annual custom silicon revenue for FY29" across existing programs, XPU-attach, and new tier-1 wins (transcript 2026-05-27).
  • Interconnect raised to >70% y/y growth in FY27 (from ~50%); DCI to $1B annualized in FY28; Celestial AI closed in the quarter (Photonic Fabric / EAM modulators, already selected by a tier-1 hyperscaler for next-gen XPU scale-up), plus Polariton (plasmonic SiPh, >1THz modulator, 3.2T+). NVIDIA partnership across optics, NVLink Fusion, AI-RAN.
  • Guidance: Q2 FY27 ~$2.7B (±5%, +35% y/y); FY27 ~$11.5B (~+40%); FY28 raised to ~$16.5B (~+45%), +$1.5B vs prior, data center ~+55% y/y; op-margin target upper-end 38–40%. ~$1B FY27 supply prepayments (transcript 2026-05-27).

Debate / thesis

  • Bull:
    • JPM Harlan Sur (2026-06-17) calls MRVL his "#2 pick in semis," and explicitly defended both the AWS Trainium and Microsoft Maia 3nm roadmaps as un-lost — refuting Asia/US-bear claims from a year ago that have since been disproven by the stock's outperformance. MRVL "owns the Trainium and Maia XPU roadmaps Broadcom does not," plus CXL and Ethernet scale-up, and a possible Google LPU win (Sur, 2026-06-05 / 2026-06-17; theme custom-asic-tpu).
    • Self-reinforcing custom pipeline: Murphy — "every program we looked at a year ago is larger a year later"; >$10B custom revenue line-of-sight for FY29, off a $75B lifetime-opportunity funnel and 18+ XPU/XPU-attach sockets (transcripts 2025-12-02, 2026-05-27).
    • Optical leadership is real and being extended: first to 200G/lane and 1.6T into production; 2nm coherent DCI to all five US hyperscalers; Celestial AI + Polariton push MRVL into scale-up CPO (theme optical-cpo, which notes MRVL still has the Google 1.6T DSP design and ~80% of the prior-gen 800G DSP market — Susquehanna, 2026-05-19).
    • Trainium 4 scale-up optionality: of four T4 variants, MRVL is in the Ethernet-scale-up and Celestial-AI/NVLink-Fusion paths; "Marvell can do Ethernet scale up" (Sur, 2026-06-17).
  • Bear:
    • Customer concentration is extreme — top-10 = 82% of FY26 revenue, and the four US hyperscalers are where the capex sits (FY26 10-K; Murphy: "that is where the dollars are going to go"). Any single design-win loss/slip is material.
    • Merchant-DSP share erosion at 1.6T: Susquehanna (2026-05-19) flags pushback on Marvell's 1.6T DSP performance; NVDA is not sourcing MRVL for 1.6T (Broadcom + captive only), and "it's going to look very different than the ~80% share Marvell had at 800G" — Broadcom gains share as the market broadens beyond Google.
    • GM mix: custom/ASIC scales faster than higher-margin optics/switching — same structural drag the Street debates at AVGO; non-GAAP GM has drifted from ~59.7% (Q3 FY26) to 58.9% (Q1 FY27).
    • "Hock's dig at Marvell": Sur relays that AVGO won't ratchet guidance "every quarter — a dig at maybe Marvell" (2026-06-17), implying MRVL's higher-frequency guide-raises carry less durability; MRVL's ASIC business is also "where Broadcom was 2–3 years ago" in scale.
  • Where the sell-side stands: No Outlook/email tool available in this environment — no sell-side notes pulled. On-disk attribution is JPM (Sur) only: #2 semis pick, roadmaps defended (2026-06-05 / 2026-06-17). The optical-cpo theme places MRVL in the "stronger copper+optics" bucket (vs weaker pure-play optics) per @crux_capital_ (2026-06-19). Briefing roll-up for MRVL not present on disk.

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q2 FY27 print: ~late August 2026 — watch the ~$2.7B/+35% guide, custom-silicon ramp, and any FY28 raise above ~$16.5B.
  • Custom: new tier-1 XPU program announcements feeding the >$10B FY29 line-of-sight; Trainium 4 scale-up protocol resolution (UALink vs Ethernet vs NVLink Fusion).
  • Optical: 1.6T ramp pace and share vs AVGO/NVDA captive DSP; 2nm coherent DCI to 5 US hyperscalers; Celestial AI CPO ($500M run-rate 2028 → $1B 2029) tier-1 selection progress; Polariton/3.2T.
  • Switching: 51.2T ramp + 100T sampling (H1 FY27); UALink 115T sampling H2 FY27 → volume FY28; XConn CXL 3.1.

Risks

  • Customer concentration — significant revenue from a few customers; top-10 = 82%, with sales heavily concentrated in the data center end market (FY26 10-K). Loss/reduction at a major customer "would likely harm" results.
  • Design-win dependence — revenue swings on timing/number of design wins; custom programs are multi-year and binary.
  • Foundry/supply dependence — fabless; relies on TSMC and third-party manufacturers/substrates/HBM; $1B FY27 capacity prepayments to secure supply (10-Q).
  • Merchant-silicon competition — AVGO (custom + DSP share gains at 1.6T), captive hyperscaler/COT teams, NVDA captive DSP; pricing/share pressure as customers diversify suppliers.
  • Margin mix — ASIC growth dilutive to gross margin vs optics/switching.
  • Macro / AI-capex cyclicality & geopolitics — China/export-policy exposure; substantial non-US operations (FY26 10-K).

In the inbox (Outlook — recent sell-side flow)

  • JPM Index Research (M. Moon) _(2026-06-18)_: MRVL added to the S&P 500 in the June rebalance ($88bn notional, 2nd-biggest quarterly) — passive demand tailwind.
  • Harlan Sur (JPM) call note _(2026-06-17)_: Marvell tied to the Trainium 3 program; flags a US sell-side competitor downgraded MRVL on 1.6T DSP share concerns.
  • AlphaSense expert alert _(2026-06-17)_: Marvell AVP on yield optimization for advanced nodes (initial challenges, improving over time).

INTC — Intel Corporation

Wiki · generated 2026-06-19 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\INTC (filings + transcripts) · E:\equity_calls_transcripts · E:\Wiki Felipe\_briefings. Master index: 00_INDEX.md.

Snapshot

The only scaled IDM left that straddles both sides of the chip value chain: it designs x86 CPUs / accelerators / custom silicon (sold through the Intel Products business — segments CCG client PCs and DCAI data-center & AI) and manufactures them (plus, increasingly, chips for external customers) through Intel Foundry — leading-edge nodes (Intel 3, 18A, 14A) and advanced packaging (EMIB/EMIB-T, Foveros). Three reportable segments — CCG, DCAI, Intel Foundry — plus an "all other" bucket (Mobileye; Altera deconsolidated after the 51% divestiture closed 2025-09-12) (10-K FY25, 2026-01-23). FY25 revenue $52.9B, roughly flat y/y on supply constraints (Q4 FY25 call, 2026-01-22). The entire thesis is the turnaround under CEO Lip-Bu Tan (in seat ~15 months): get 18A to yield, win a first major external 14A/foundry customer, and turn the foundry P&L from a ~$2.4B/qtr loss toward break-even by exit-2027 — against a near-breakeven product P&L and a heavily restructured, government- and partner-recapitalized balance sheet.

Position in the value chain

Intel is the rare name that occupies the middle box and reaches into both neighbors: it buys WFE/materials from the same suppliers as TSMC, designs its own CPUs/accelerators, fabs them in-house, and now sells leading-edge wafers + advanced packaging to external customers — i.e. it competes with its own foundry suppliers' biggest customer (TSMC) while remaining their customer. The swing factors are the 18A ramp (yields, Panther Lake → Nova Lake → server) and landing the first big external foundry/14A customer plus the packaging-as-trojan-horse wins (EMIB/EMIB-T).

Current state (latest quarter)

Q1 FY26 (call 2026-04-23; quarter ended 2026-03-28): Revenue $13.6B, +7% y/y, beating the guide midpoint by $1.4B — sixth straight beat. Non-GAAP GM 41% (~650bps above guide); non-GAAP EPS $0.29. But GAAP printed an operating loss of $(3.1B) and EPS $(0.73) on a $4.07B restructuring charge — the headline that auto-injected financials will show as near-breakeven once normalized (10-Q, 2026-04-24).

  • CCG $7.7B (-6% q/q, above plan); FY26 PC unit TAM guided down low-double-digit %, supply-constrained, sell-in holding up better than sell-through on historically low channel inventory (StoneX, 2026-05-12).
  • DCAI $5.1B (+7% q/q, +22% y/y); server prioritized over client given scarcity; server CPU guided to grow double-digits through year-end. Intel Products op income $4.06B (CCG $2.5B + DCAI $1.5B) (10-Q).
  • Intel Foundry $5.4B (+20% q/q) but op loss $(2.4B); external third-party foundry + A&T revenue just $174M (10-Q) — the external book is still tiny. Management targets quarterly loss reduction through FY26 toward exit-2027 break-even.
  • 18A yields tracking ~2-3 months ahead of internal benchmarks; "fastest new-product ramps in 5 years" (Xeon 6, Core Series 3 / Panther Lake) (Q1 FY26 call). Capex flat y/y but equipment spend +25% y/y; shell capacity already built (Arizona Fab 62, Ireland), so adds are facilitization not greenfield (StoneX, 2026-05-12).
  • Guidance Q2 FY26: revenue $13.8–14.8B (+2-9% q/q). BBG consensus Q2-26E (auto-injected below): ~$14.4B rev, ~39% GM, EPS ~$0.21.

Debate / thesis

  • Bull:
    • 18A/14A is finally executing. 18A yields ahead of internal plan; 14A "maturity, yield and performance outpacing 18A" at the equivalent point, with external customers engaged in the definition phase (vs only development for 18A) and management landing more internal tiles on 14A — a confidence tell (StoneX 2026-05-12; Q1 FY26 call). ASML 18A/14A commentary on customer wins / capacity-ramp needs is one of the pillars of the bullish semicap trade (BofA/Evans via briefing, 2026-06-15).
    • Packaging as the trojan horse. EMIB/EMIB-T reticle ~30% larger than CoWoS; per-engagement value re-rated from "hundreds of millions" to multiple billions/yr; backlog growing, ramp visible 2027 (StoneX 2026-05-12). Redburn (bus tour, briefing 2026-06-17): Intel "put on a brave face" but advanced packaging is the real play — "using packaging as a trojan horse into customer wins."
    • CPU-per-GPU re-rating + supply scarcity. Tan frames the ratio moving from ~1:8 (training) → 1:4 (inference) → parity (agentic); NVIDIA host-node win (custom Xeon in NVLink for Rubin NVL8) levels the field vs Arm. Server is supply-, not speeds-and-feeds-, constrained today — an IDM with internal wafers has more levers, and tightness likely extends into 2027 (StoneX 2026-05-12).
    • Recapitalized balance sheet. Cash/STI ~$37.4B exiting FY25 after NVIDIA $5B (215M shares @ $23.28), SoftBank $2B (87M @ $23.00), US government (CHIPS Secure Enclave; up to 433M shares to DOC + 241M warrants @ $20), Altera 51% ($4.3B) and Mobileye monetizations (10-K FY25; Q3/Q4 FY25 calls).
  • Bear:
    • Foundry still loses ~$2.4B/qtr and the external book is $174M. Break-even is an exit-2027 aspiration that management has repeatedly caveated will slip the moment a big customer is won (you layer cost ahead of revenue) (StoneX 2026-05-12). Long-run target is only ~40% GM / ~30% OM for external wafers & packaging — structurally below Intel's historical product margins, and ASICs/packaging/external wafers all dilute corporate GM as they scale (StoneX 2026-05-12).
    • Roadmap still behind on speeds-and-feeds. Management concedes Diamond Rapids' lack of SMT was "a mistake" (8-channel non-hyperscale SKU pulled); real competitiveness vs AMD waits for Coral Rapids, with no firm date. Near-term share stabilization is a who-gets-more-wafers story, not a competitiveness story (StoneX 2026-05-12).
    • Show-me on the external customer. A former Marvell AVP was "skeptical about customer shift to Intel for leading-edge nodes — yield/delay risks too high" (AlphaSense expert via briefing, 2026-06-17). 14A funding is explicitly gated on a binding external commitment that has not yet been disclosed (Q2 FY25 call, 2025-07-24).
    • Dilution + GAAP losses. Share count jumped to ~5,083M (from 4,343M y/y) on the SoftBank/NVIDIA/DOC issuances; GAAP EPS $(0.73) on restructuring; 10-K flags a potential pause/discontinuation of 14A and next-gen nodes if no significant external customer materializes.
  • Where the sell-side/buy-side stands: Improving but still skeptical — the move is sentiment, not yet earnings. Bernstein (Stacy Rasgon) raised PT to $100 from $65, Market-Perform, on 18A-P process milestones + CPU TAM read-through and IFS packaging optionality (briefing 2026-06-17). INTC +9% on 2026-06-18 on an Apple chip deal (GS TMT, briefing 2026-06-18) and +2.6% pre-mkt 2026-06-17 on the 18A-P update. MS bus tour: bullish, citing CEO commitment to foundry and positive microprocessor view (briefing 2026-06-15); INTC included in MS West Coast Semis bus tour bullish names (briefing 2026-06-16). Intel is the most-mentioned name across the equity-call/briefing repo — the read-throughs run through ASML/ASMI (18A/14A capacity), TSMC (foundry competition) and the packaging complex.

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q2 FY26 print (late July 2026) vs the $13.8–14.8B guide; GM trajectory and the quarterly foundry-loss reduction toward exit-2027 break-even.
  • First major external 14A / foundry customer announcement — the single biggest swing; 14A design-win window "opens 2H-2026 into 1H-2027"; risk production 2H-2027 internal / 2H-2029 external (StoneX 2026-05-12). Watch the Apple chip deal disclosed mid-June for substance.
  • 18A ramp milestones — Panther Lake (notebook) through 2026, Nova Lake (notebook + desktop) late-2026/early-2027, then server onto 18A; 18A-P process update (post-close, mid-June 2026).
  • Advanced packaging (EMIB/EMIB-T) order conversion — first ramp year 2027, billions/engagement by year two; capacity adds and tool lead-times into 2027.
  • Server roadmap: Granite Rapids (Intel 3) workhorse 2026; Diamond Rapids; Coral Rapids pull-in (no firm date) — the true AMD-competitiveness gate.
  • Capex/Ohio: equipment spend +25% y/y FY26; Ohio facilitization "after 2030" unless external wins pull it forward; FY27 capex number not yet given.

Risks

  • No external 14A customer → potential pause/discontinuation of 14A and next-gen leading-edge nodes (explicit 10-K risk factor).
  • Foundry losses persist; break-even slips when a big customer is layered on; ~40/30 endpoint GM/OM structurally below legacy product margins; ASIC/packaging/external-wafer mix dilutive to corporate GM.
  • Competitive position vs AMD/Arm in servers still gated on Coral Rapids; Diamond Rapids SMT misstep; hyperscaler BYO (internal Arm) silicon a structural share headwind in DCAI.
  • Execution / yield — 18A still below management's bar; "7-8% yield improvement/month" but not yet at industry standard.
  • PC TAM down low-double-digits FY26 on memory-cost-driven inflation hitting sell-through; supply-constrained on older nodes (Intel 10/7).
  • Government/partner overhang — DOC equity (up to 433M shares) + warrants (241M @ $20) contingent on Intel keeping ≥51% of the foundry; NVIDIA/SoftBank/DOC issuance diluted share count ~+17% y/y; geopolitical/trade and China exposure.
  • Note on financials: Intel's reported EPS is near-breakeven on a normalized basis; GAAP is swung by large restructuring/impairment charges and equity-investment gains/losses — do not read the GAAP loss as run-rate.

In the inbox (Outlook — recent sell-side flow)

  • BofA TMT sales _(2026-06-18)_: INTC +8% on the Apple deal — sentiment inflection.
  • Morgan Stanley TMT (T. Wigg) _(2026-06-15)_: INTC / MRVL / ALAB — bullish MS bus-tour takeaways.
  • Rothschild & Co TMT Daily (R. Adley) _(2026-06-19)_: INTC featured in the EU/US semis colour (SOX +6.4% tape).

IFX — Infineon Technologies AG

Wiki · generated 2026-06-19 · German issuer — NO SEC 10-K/10-Q/20-F filings exist. Covered via earnings-call transcripts (E:\Wiki Felipe\IFX\transcripts) + Infineon IR + BBG consensus (EUR). Equity calls: E:\equity_calls_transcripts. Briefing roll-ups: E:\briefings\2026. Master index: INDEX.md.

Snapshot

Infineon is the world's #1 power-semiconductor vendor and #1 automotive-semiconductor vendor — an IDM (own fabs) spanning silicon MOSFETs/IGBTs, SiC and GaN power devices, automotive microcontrollers (AURIX), sensors and security controllers. FY2025 revenue EUR 14.66bn (Q4 FY25 call, 2025-11-12). Four segments today: Automotive (ATV, ~50% of revenue), Green Industrial Power (GIP, ~11%), Power & Sensor Systems (PSS, ~29%), Connected Secure Systems (CSS, ~10%) (segment-split per IR / Bristlemoon, FY25). Re-orgs into 3 divisions — Automotive, Power Systems, Edge Systems — effective 2026-07-01 (Q2 FY26 call, 2026-05-06). For a tech/semis book the live story is the pivot of a legacy auto/industrial power franchise into the AI data-center power supply chain (800V DC), which is re-rating the whole analog/power group.

Position in the value chain

IFX is a vertically-integrated (IDM) power & systems semi player: it buys Si/SiC/GaN wafers and uses some foundry partners upstream, fabs and packages its own power devices, MCUs, sensors and security chips, and sells into auto OEMs/Tier-1s, industrial, renewables/EV, and — increasingly — data-center power (800V DC). The AI-datacenter-power read-through is the key incremental driver: IFX guides AI-power revenue EUR 1.5bn FY26 → EUR 2.5bn FY27, on ~$175 of semi content per kW (Q2 FY26 call, 2026-05-06), via its NVIDIA 800V centralized-DC collaboration.

Current state (latest quarter)

Q2 FY26 (quarter ended 2026-03-31, call 2026-05-06): Revenue EUR 3.812bn (+6% YoY, +4% QoQ; ~+14% currency-adjusted); segment result EUR 653m, margin 17.1% (down from 17.9% in Q1 — idle/underutilization costs and CSS drag). Order backlog ~EUR 25bn (+EUR 4bn QoQ, +25% YoY) — the standout. FCF EUR -63m (seasonal; FY build in H2). Management raised FY26 guidance to >EUR 16bn revenue (~+10%) and ~20% segment margin (from high-teens). AI-power business is in allocation; reconfirmed EUR 1.5bn AI-power revenue FY26 → EUR 2.5bn FY27. Segment detail: ATV EUR 1.83bn @18.1% (MCU share 32%→36%; HV-EV powertrain ~7% of ATV is the profit drag), PSS EUR 1.26bn @20.4% (AI DC momentum), GIP EUR 403m @11.7% (+15% QoQ), CSS EUR 319m @5.6%. GaN ramping for AI DC power with select customers; SiC low-double-digit growth from AI. (All Q2 FY26 call, 2026-05-06.)

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: Backlog +25% YoY to ~EUR 25bn and a raised ~20% margin guide point to a clean cyclical recovery layered on a structural AI-power ramp (Q2 FY26 call, 2026-05-06). AI-power EUR 0.7bn FY25 → EUR 1.5bn FY26 → EUR 2.5bn FY27, into an EUR 8–12bn addressable market by decade-end, with the NVIDIA 800V DC collaboration as the anchor (Q4 FY25 call, 2025-11-12). Citi groups IFX among power-semi names guiding 40–100% DC-power growth this year; sell-side framing is that >20% DC sales mix re-rates the analog group (800v-dc-power theme; Citi 2026-06-16, via theme page). IFX flagged at 12m relative highs alongside the AI-semi complex (Redburn, briefing 2026-05-26).
  • Bear: "Less about the size of the AI power pie and more about IFX's share of it" — TXN/ADI/ON are gaining DC-power share with TXN explicitly "gaining share vs Infineon/MPWR/Renesas" (Citi/BofA, briefing 2026-06-15). The legacy book is the drag: auto HV-EV semis have "unacceptable profitability" amid China EV slowdown + US incentive expiry (Q2 FY26 call, 2026-05-06; Q4 FY25 call), CSS margin is sub-6%, and FY25 carried ~EUR 1bn idle costs (~EUR 800m guided FY26). The 800V ramp is also a "slower than hype" risk and China can flood SiC/GaN supply (800v-dc-power theme; @bubbleboi, briefing 2026-06-15).
  • Where the sell-side/buy-side stands: Constructive into the AI-power ramp but IFX is read more as a beneficiary-by-association than a top pick — BofA's 800V "best ideas" are TXN/ADI/ON, not IFX (BofA Vivek Arya, briefing 2026-05-26). JPM spec sales lists IFX among SiC names doing "more work" on 800V at 15–20% adoption (JPM AI-power pitch, 2026-06-11). Recent tape was technical: IFX sold off then +5% on "rotation from analog into WFE, not fundamentals" (Beavington/Jefferies, briefing 2026-06-18). Note: a desk-level Outlook pull was attempted but the Outlook script is not callable in this environment (no working Python launcher) — sell-side stance above is sourced from the briefing archive.

Catalysts / what to watch

  • 2026-06-29 — IFX AI-power fireside chat (BofA / Didier Scemama) with Adam White, head of PSS AI-power division — direct read on the EUR 2.5bn FY27 AI-power target and 800V traction (briefing 2026-06-19).
  • 2026-07-01 — re-org into 3 divisions (Automotive / Power Systems / Edge Systems) — new segment reporting.
  • Q3 FY26 print (early Aug 2026) — guidance is for AI-power in allocation; watch the FY26 >EUR 16bn / ~20% margin bridge and HV-auto commentary.
  • ams OSRAM sensor-portfolio acquisition close (~EUR 570m, targeted Q2 2026; ~EUR 230m annual revenue) (Q1 FY26 call, 2026-02-04).
  • Smart Power Fab Dresden completion targeted summer 2026 — capex/idle-cost trajectory (Q4 FY25 call).

Risks

  • Auto/EV cyclicality + pricing: ~50% of revenue is Automotive; HV-EV powertrain semis at "unacceptable profitability," China EV slowdown, US incentive expiry (Q2 FY26 / Q4 FY25 calls).
  • DC-power share loss: TXN/ADI/ON/MPWR competing aggressively for the same 800V content; share, not TAM, is the swing (Citi/BofA, briefings 2026-06-15/05-26).
  • Underutilization / idle costs: ~EUR 1bn in FY25, ~EUR 800m guided FY26; margin highly utilization-sensitive (~80% util) (Q4 FY25 call).
  • China exposure: ~38% of revenue (~29ppts mainland) — demand and geopolitical risk (Q4 FY25 call).
  • 800V ramp timing: "slower than hype," SST/architecture timeline pushed to 2028–29; potential SiC/GaN oversupply from China (800v-dc-power theme).
  • FX: EUR reporting vs USD-priced semis — currency-adjusted growth materially higher than reported (Q2 FY26 call).

In the inbox (Outlook — recent sell-side flow)

  • JPM Euro TMT Daily (S. Silver) _(2026-06-18)_ & BofA EU TMT _(2026-06-18)_: Infineon carried in the EU power/analog semis complex (BESI/AMS context); cyclical-recovery tape.
  • _(IFX-specific broker notes are thinner in the 90-day inbox window; sell-side stance leans on the dated briefings + the 800V theme — see below.)_

Sources

  • Filings: none — Infineon is a German issuer with no SEC 10-K/10-Q/20-F. Financials sourced from IR/earnings calls + BBG consensus (EUR).
  • Transcripts: Q2 FY26 (2026-05-06) · Q1 FY26 (2026-02-04) · Q4/FY25 (2025-11-12) · Q3 FY25 (2025-08-05).
  • Themes: 800v-dc-power · ai-datacenter-power.
  • Equity calls: UBS 800V power (2026-05-14), JPM AI-power pitch (2026-06-11) — E:\equity_calls_transcripts\Semis.
  • Briefings: E:\briefings\2026 — 2026-06-19 (IFX fireside chat catalyst), 2026-06-18 (WFE rotation), 2026-06-15 / 2026-05-26 (DC-power share, BofA 800V best ideas).
  • IR / press: Infineon IR "at a glance"; press release INFXX202508-130 (Q3 FY25). BBG estimates: _data/estimates.json (IFX GR Equity, EUR).

MSFT — Microsoft Corporation

Wiki · generated 2026-06-18 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\MSFT (filings + transcripts) · _equity_calls · _briefings\by-ticker\MSFT.md. Master index: ../INDEX.md.

🔖 Themes: Tokenmaxxing / AI Monetization

Snapshot

Mega-cap software + hyperscale cloud, three reporting segments: Productivity & Business Processes (M365/Office, LinkedIn, Dynamics), Intelligent Cloud (Azure, server, GitHub, enterprise services) and More Personal Computing (Windows OEM, Search/Bing, Gaming/Xbox). In the AI stack MSFT sits across every layer it can monetize: own datacenters + first-party silicon (Maia 200 accelerator, Cobalt 200 CPU), the agent app platform (Foundry, Agent 365, IQ/Work IQ context layer), and high-value agentic apps (M365 Copilot, GitHub Copilot, Security Copilot, Dragon). Anchor relationship with OpenAI (equity stake + frontier-model IP rights to 2032 + Azure compute customer), plus Anthropic as both a model on Foundry and an Azure compute customer. Microsoft Cloud cleared $54.5B in revenue in the latest quarter; AI ARR >$37B, +123% y/y (Q3 FY26, 2026-04-29).

Current state (latest quarter)

Q3 FY26 (reported 2026-04-29): Revenue $82.9B, +18% (+15% cc); EPS $4.27, +21% y/y (ex-OpenAI impact); operating margin 46%; company GM 68%, down y/y on AI infra. Microsoft Cloud $54.5B, +29% (GM 66%).

  • Azure +40% cc, ahead of consensus, capacity delivered earlier than planned (Fairwater Wisconsin online 6 weeks early). Q4 FY26 guide: Azure +39–40% cc, with management expecting modest acceleration in 2H CY26 vs 1H; supply expected to remain constrained at least through 2026 (Q3 FY26).
  • M365 Copilot: record seat adds, +250% y/y; >20M paid seats (vs 15M in Q2 FY26, 2026-01-28); customers >50k seats quadrupled y/y; Accenture >740k seats. Weekly engagement now "at the same level as Outlook" (Satya, Q3 FY26).
  • Capex $31.9B in Q3 (down sequentially from $37.5B in Q2 FY26); ~2/3 short-lived assets (GPU/CPU). CY26 capex guided ~$190B, of which ~$25B is higher component pricing (memory); Q4 capex steps to >$40B (Q3 FY26).
  • OpenAI: ~45% of $625B commercial RPO was OpenAI-related as of Q2 FY26; ex-OpenAI RPO grew 28%. Restructured deal — MSFT keeps royalty-free frontier-model IP to 2032, no longer pays rev-share to OpenAI, while retaining rev-share from OpenAI through 2030 (margin-accretive; Q3 FY26 + JEF NDR recap, 2026-06-12).
  • FY27 framing: "another year of double-digit revenue and operating income growth"; headcount down y/y; opex growth mid-to-high single digits (Q3 FY26).

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: AI is a real, large engine, not eating SaaS — Copilot is sold separately, not bundled/discounted, seat growth accelerating into E7 launch (Bernstein hyperscaler review, 2026-04-30). Margins expanded despite record capex; Bernstein's read is this "rerates" exactly like the prior cloud transition — opex grows slower than revenue, and capex eventually grows slower than cloud revenue, inflecting AI/Azure margins up (Bernstein, 2026-04-30). First-party silicon (Maia 200 >30% better tokens/$, Cobalt in ~half of regions) and first-party MAI models lower COGS; FinTwit flags MSFT at ~16.8x 2028E as "stupid cheap," targeting ~50% of AI compute on own silicon by 2028 (@ParadisLabs, 2026-06-15). Ackman built a new core MSFT position at ~21x fwd, M365+Azure ~70% of profits (13F, 2026-05-15/18). GS a "Buyer" post-Build (Borges, 2026-06-07).
  • Bear: Capex is the battleground — MS/Altimeter revised CY27 capex est. from $178B to $276B, the single biggest hyperscaler revision (@Midnight_Captl, 2026-06-13); 1-yr return ~ -18% with the "lowest ROI on AI capex to-date" critique (@ParadisLabs, 2026-06-12). OpenAI concentration in Azure backlog (~45% RPO; TCI flagged single customer >40% of Azure backlog before cutting its $8B stake, 2026-05-11). DeepSeek/"tokenmaxxing" substitution risk — MSFT reportedly weighing hosted DeepSeek V4 + usage pricing on Copilot Cowork, raising questions on enterprise willingness to pay for frontier models (Axios via briefing, 2026-06-17; @zerohedge, 2026-06-18). Barclays desk: investors see MSFT "stuck in the mud" near-term, used as a funding long vs SaaS, with Azure growth decelerating relative to GCP/AWS accelerating (Rand, 2026-06-03); positioning moved from +2 to -1 post-Q2 (Barclays, 2026-05-29).
  • Where the sell-side/buy-side stands: Skews positive. Jefferies (Thill) Buy $575 reaffirmed (2026-06-06); MS (Weiss) OW $650 (2026-06-01); DB (Zelnick) Buy $550 post-Build (2026-06-03); Barclays (Lenschow) OW $545 (2026-05-07/06-03). Outliers cautious: Redburn/R&Co (Adley) Neutral, citing active+passive selling and Russell growth-to-value reclass (2026-05-26). Stock at 12-month relative lows alongside CRM/ADBE/INTU/ADSK (Rothschild, 2026-06-18).

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q4 FY26 earnings (late Jul 2026): Azure cc growth + the 2H acceleration claim; CY26 $190B capex confirmation; M365 Copilot net seat adds (guided up sequentially).
  • E7 launch (eff. May 31 2026): first time Office carries a variable consumption component on top of seats — read on per-user + usage monetization.
  • GitHub Copilot usage-based pricing (eff. June 1 2026) — watch consumption-driven top-line acceleration vs near-term cloud GM dilution (guided ~64% Q4).
  • Capex revision watch: any move toward the $276B CY27 number (MS/Altimeter) vs the ~$190B CY26 base.
  • OpenAI/DeepSeek model-economics: hosted DeepSeek/MAI substitution and Copilot Cowork usage pricing.
  • Datacenter/power: CVX-MSFT CCGT (GE Vernova turbines) FID expected later 2026 (UBS, 2026-06-11); self-build campuses (Conover NC, Chase City VA, Castroville TX) reactivating.

Risks

  • Capex/ROI mismatch: capex compounding far faster than Azure revenue; component (memory) inflation adds ~$25B to CY26 capex and will pressure cloud GM as 6-yr depreciation builds.
  • Customer concentration: ~45% of commercial RPO tied to OpenAI; partnership economics and OpenAI's ability to multi-cloud post-April-2026 restructuring.
  • AI cost discipline / model commoditization: enterprises substituting cheaper models (DeepSeek/open source) erodes frontier-model monetization; "tokenmaxxing" pullback.
  • Competitive: Azure cc growth trailing GCP/AWS acceleration; Anthropic Office-suite apps (Claude for Excel/Word GA) pressuring Copilot.
  • Consumer drag: Windows OEM guided down high-teens (memory-price PC headwind, inventory normalization); Gaming/Xbox declining; impairment charges in gaming.
  • Standard 10-K factors (FY25 10-K): intense competition, cybersecurity, AI execution/legal/IP, datacenter capacity & power, and government regulation/antitrust.

Consensus estimates (BBG)
