QCOM — QUALCOMM Incorporated

Wiki · generated 2026-06-19 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\QCOM (10-K FY25, 10-Q Q2 FY26, transcripts) · _briefings roll-up. Master index: ../INDEX.md.

Snapshot

Qualcomm is the leading merchant supplier of premium mobile SoCs and cellular modem/RF, plus the dominant 3G/4G/5G patent licensor. Two reporting engines: QCT (chips — Handsets, Automotive, IoT) and QTL (licensing). FY25 revenue $44.3B, +14% YoY (10-K, 2025-11-05). QCT $38.4B (Handsets $27.8B / Auto $4.0B / IoT $6.6B); QTL ~flat YoY but ~70%+ EBT margin and the bulk of pre-tax profit. The structural debate: Handsets (≈72% of QCT) is a mature, China-heavy, Apple-eroding base, so the equity story rests on diversification (auto Snapdragon Digital Chassis, IoT/edge-AI, Snapdragon X PCs) and a nascent data-center CPU push offsetting Apple's modem in-sourcing.

Position in the value chain

QCOM is fabless: it designs Snapdragon SoCs, modem/RF and auto/IoT silicon (built at TSMC/Samsung foundries) and sells to smartphone, auto and PC OEMs, while QTL monetizes its cellular IP across the industry regardless of who makes the chip. Key tension: Apple is in-sourcing its baseband modem ("we expect that Apple will increasingly use its own modem products… which will have a significant negative impact on our QCT revenues" — 10-K FY25), pushing QCOM to diversify non-handset revenue (auto exiting FY26 at a >$6B run-rate; data-center shipments to a hyperscaler starting Dec-2026).

Current state (latest quarter)

Q2 FY26 (reported 2026-04-29, quarter ended 2026-03-29) — Revenue $10.6B, -3% YoY; non-GAAP EPS $2.65 (high end of guide). QCT $9.1B (10-Q QCT total $9.08B; Handsets $6.02B, -13% YoY; Auto $1.33B, +38%; IoT $1.73B, +9%). QCT EBT margin compressed to 27% (from 30%). QTL $1.4B at 72% EBT margin (+5% YoY on better mix). GAAP net income $7.4B was flattered by a $5.7B income-tax benefit releasing the DTA valuation allowance that had been charged in Q4 FY25 — not operating strength (10-Q, 2026-04-29).

  • Guidance Q3 FY26: revenue $9.2–$10.0B, non-GAAP EPS $2.10–$2.30; QCT $7.9–$8.5B. CFO: China handset revenue "will reach a bottom in the third quarter" (Q2 FY26 call).
  • Diversification proof points: Automotive crossed >$5B annualized for the first time, exiting FY26 at a >$6B run-rate; data-center initial shipments to a leading hyperscaler expected December, framed margin-accretive (Q2 FY26 call). VW Group (Audi/Porsche) LOI signed (Q1 FY26 call). Snapdragon X2 → 150 PC designs targeted for 2026.

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: Diversification is real and accelerating — Auto +38% YoY, FY29 Auto+IoT target $22B; data-center CPU optionality (Alphawave Semi + Ventana acquisitions, first hyperscaler shipments Dec-26) opens a new TAM; QTL remains a high-margin cash engine; capital return ~$3.7B/quarter. Server-CPU thesis circulated buy-side (CPU:GPU ratio toward 1:1 in agentic AI; lead times stretching) (@fundaai, briefing 2026-05-15).
  • Bear: Handsets still ~70% of QCT and shrinking — Apple modem in-sourcing is an explicit, management-acknowledged "significant negative" (10-K FY25); China handset units soft on the DRAM/memory shortage (Q1/Q2 FY26 calls). The rally was called the "problem child" of the chip complex — Tae Kim newsletter flagged +80% off lows as overdone given DC ambition; QCOM -11% on 2026-05-13 on that substack (UBS/Ruple, briefing 2026-05-13). AMD overtook QCOM in semi revenue in 1QC26 with the gap "widening from here" (@SKundojjala, briefing 2026-05-06).
  • Where the Street stands: No live ratings/PTs in inbox (Outlook unavailable this session — see Sources). Briefing color is mixed-to-cautious: JPM ran a QCOM bogey survey into the print (briefing 2026-05-12); semis broadly flagged extended (R&Co: "63% above 200d, largest since Dot-Com," briefing 2026-05-12). Longer-term modem-displacement read-through repeatedly cited vs Intel 14A smartphone wins (briefing 2026-05-14).

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q3 FY26 print (late Jul-2026) — does China handset trough confirm; QCT margin trajectory.
  • Data-center first shipments to lead hyperscaler — December 2026; any DC revenue disclosure (FY27 ramp guided on Q1 FY26 call).
  • Apple modem share — QCOM held "20% share assumption for fall Apple phones" (Q2 FY26 call); watch the glidepath as Apple's in-house modem scales.
  • Auto run-rate — exit FY26 >$6B; FY29 Auto+IoT $22B target progress.
  • Memory/DRAM availability — gating China handset units near-term.
  • 6G — coalition launched; silicon 2028, launches 2029 (long-dated).

Risks

  • Apple modem in-sourcing — explicit material headwind to QCT (10-K FY25).
  • Customer concentration — Apple, Samsung and Xiaomi each ≥10% of consolidated revenue in FY25 (10-K).
  • China exposure — large share of handset demand; Huawei export license revoked, no further product revenue expected; share shifts to Huawei-supplied OEMs a risk (10-K).
  • QTL durability — licensing renewals / per-unit royalty caps / regulatory scrutiny; QTL carries the profit mix.
  • Customer in-house silicon — competition "from products internally developed by our customers, including some of our largest customers" (10-K).
  • Premium-tier cyclicality + memory pricing — DRAM shortage constraining handset units (Q1/Q2 FY26 calls).
  • Data-center execution — unproven entry vs entrenched x86/Arm incumbents; revenue not until FY27.

In the inbox (Outlook — recent sell-side flow)

  • UBS (Tim Arcuri) — 'Qualcomm: Animal Spirits Evident, But Still A Company In Transition' _(2026-04-30)_: data-center entry could stir animal spirits, but 'hard to get excited' — handset/Apple-modem transition overhang.
  • Samik (JPM) QCOM/MediaTek call (repo): handset/China + diversification (auto/PC/edge-AI) debate.

ARM — Arm Holdings plc

Wiki · generated 2026-06-19 · 20-F filer (UK foreign private issuer) — files annual 20-F, no 10-K/10-Q · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\ARM (20-Fs FY24–FY26 + transcripts) · _briefings\2026 roll-up · theme custom-asic-tpu. Master index: ../INDEX.md.

Snapshot

Arm is the upstream IP-licensing layer of the compute industry: it designs CPU/GPU/NPU architectures and processor IP and collects (i) up-front license fees and (ii) a per-unit royalty on substantially every chip shipped using its designs — typically a % of chip ASP or a fixed fee per unit, rising as more Arm IP is included (FY26 20-F, Item 4). FY26 revenue $4.92B, +23% YoY, split License & other $2.31B (47%) / Royalty $2.61B (53%); operating margin ~18% GAAP, ~49% non-GAAP in Q4 (FY26 20-F; Q4 FY26 call, 2026-05-06). The franchise is in two transitions that define the thesis: (1) royalty mix-up from ARMv9 + Compute Subsystems (CSS) lifting per-chip rates, and (2) a move downstream into its own silicon — the Arm AGI CPU launched at Q4 FY26 with Meta as lead partner. ~57% of revenue comes from top-5 customers (incl. Arm China and SoftBank); SoftBank controls Arm (~controlled-company / FPI status). Mobile app processors are ~43% of royalty (FY26 20-F).

Position in the value chain

Arm sits upstream of nearly every SoC in the world — virtually all smartphones, a majority of tablets/digital TVs, and a rising share of data-center CPUs run an Arm-architected core. It ships no volume silicon historically (AGI CPU is the first, production end-CY26); revenue is a license + recurring per-unit royalty model with a long tail (chips ship for years). The swing variable is the server / data-center CPU TAM — hyperscaler custom CPUs (AWS Graviton, Google Axion, Microsoft Cobalt, NVIDIA Vera/Grace) are Arm-architected, and agentic AI is lifting CPU:GPU ratios toward ~1:1.

Current state (latest quarter)

Q4 FY26 (call 2026-05-06): revenue $1.49B (+20%); License & other $819M (+29%); Royalty $671M (+11%); non-GAAP EPS $0.60 (record); FY26 $4.92B (+23%), non-GAAP EPS $1.77. Vs Street (Capstone briefing, 2026-05-06): license beat ($780M), royalty slightly light ($691M); stock +13% in session. Q1 FY27 guide: rev $1.26B ±$50M (~+20%), non-GAAP EPS $0.40 ±$0.04.

  • Data center royalty more than doubled YoY again; Arm targets a >$100B data-center CPU TAM by 2030 as agentic AI needs >4x today's CPU capacity (Q4 FY26 call). Haas: "by the end of the decade, the largest market share by CPU type will be Arm."
  • Own silicon — Arm AGI CPU: launched with Meta as lead partner; ~$2B of customer demand across FY27+FY28 (>2x the analyst-day figure), 50+ ecosystem partners (AWS, Broadcom, GCP, MSFT, NVDA, Oracle, Samsung, TSMC). FY27 AGI revenue held at ~$1B on supply constraints; first-gen GM ~30%+ (dilutive to the ~49% non-GAAP IP model). FY31 framing: ~$15B AGI + ~$10B IP, combined EPS >$9.
  • CSS the royalty engine: CFO Child (Q3 FY26 call) — CSS could be "upwards of 50% of royalties within a couple of years"; CSS royalty rates now >10% of ASP (Q1 FY26).

Debate / thesis

  • Bull (Bernstein, Rasgon): PT raised $300 → $500, Outperform (2026-06-17) on a server CPU TAM raise to $223B; Arm targeting $22B revenue by 2030, FY31E EPS lifted $9.83 → $11.79. The init thesis (David Dai, 2026-05-18, $300): agentic AI = ~120M CPU cores/GW, ~4x a traditional data center; Arm holds ~50% CPU share among top hyperscalers (Graviton/Axion/Cobalt/Vera all Arm-architected — Moorhead, 2026-05-07). CPU:GPU ratio moving from ~8:1 (training) to ~1:1 (agentic/inference). Axion is actively displacing x86 host CPUs in Google TPU 8t/8i — "50% less power, 80% improvement over x86" (Arm CEO at GCP Next, 2026-05-11). PC optionality: NVDA RTX Spark / N1X Arm PC with Microsoft (briefings, 2026-06-01; ARM +12% pre-mkt).
  • Bear: royalty came in light vs Street in Q4 FY26 even as license beat — the highest-multiple part of the story (per-unit royalty compounding) is lumpy. The AGI CPU pivot trades Arm's pristine IP margins for ~30% hardware GM and puts Arm into direct competition with its own licensees (explicit new risk factor, FY26 20-F). Customer concentration is acute: top-5 = ~57% of revenue, Arm China alone ~16%; PRC ~18% of revenue with US/UK–China trade & national-security overhang, and Arm does not control Arm China (FY26 20-F risk factors). Mobile is ~43% of royalty into a soft smartphone unit backdrop (memory-driven; Q3 FY26 flagged 1–2% total royalty risk). Competition from x86 and free RISC-V (some customers are RISC-V backers).
  • Where the sell-side stands: Bernstein OP $500 (Rasgon, 2026-06-17). SoftBank read-through bundled: Bernstein lifted SoftBank to OP ¥11,200 (from ¥8,200) on the same ARM CPU-TAM revision (2026-06-17); SoftBank "is not interested in selling one share" of Arm (Haas, Q3 FY26 call). Arm is on the Jefferies Chicago Semis Conf roster (Aug 24) and ran an IR fireside 2026-06-15.

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q1 FY27 print (~late July 2026): first read on royalty re-acceleration vs the light Q4 royalty; AGI CPU order-book updates.
  • Arm AGI CPU production — expected by end of CY2026 (FY26 20-F); first revenue ~$90–100M in Q4 FY27; watch GM trajectory and whether the $2B FY27+28 demand converts.
  • Server/data-center share milestones — Graviton5 / Axion / Cobalt 200 / Vera ramps; whether Arm crosses ~50% hyperscaler CPU share (Q3/Q4 FY26 framing).
  • CSS royalty mix crossing into the "teens" then toward ~50% of royalties.
  • China / Arm China commercial-relationship and export-control headlines; SoftBank capital actions (read-through both ways).
  • Cross-theme: agentic-AI CPU:GPU ratio and custom-silicon mix — see custom-asic-tpu (Vera, Axion, Graviton all carry embedded Arm cores).

Risks

  • Own-silicon pivot (AGI CPU): new competitive/brand/operational/financial risk; channel conflict with licensees; ~30% GM dilutive to IP model (FY26 20-F).
  • Customer concentration: top-5 ~57%, Arm China ~16–21%, mobile app processors ~43% of royalty (FY26 20-F).
  • China: PRC ~18% of revenue; no control over Arm China; US/UK–PRC trade & national-security exposure (FY26 20-F).
  • Architecture license substitution: customers can take an architecture license and build their own cores, or shift to RISC-V / x86 (FY26 20-F).
  • Cyclicality / smartphone units: royalty tied to end-market chip shipments; memory-driven handset unit softness (Q3 FY26 call).
  • Governance: SoftBank-controlled company + FPI exemptions limit minority influence (FY26 20-F).

In the inbox (Outlook — recent sell-side flow)

  • BofA — 'The CPU Debate with Vivek Arya' _(2026-06-11)_: evolving CPU market / AI infra — ARM-architected server CPUs (Graviton/Axion/Cobalt) the TAM driver; agentic AI pushing CPU:GPU toward 1:1.
  • Bernstein (per Morning Briefing): server-CPU TAM raised to $223B, ARM Outperform PT $500.

NXPI — NXP Semiconductors N.V.

Wiki · generated 2026-06-19 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\NXPI (10-K/10-Q + transcripts) · _equity_calls · E:\briefings\2026. Master index: 00_INDEX.md.

Snapshot

A Dutch-domiciled (Eindhoven), 70+-year-old mixed-signal / embedded-processing IDM — embedded MCUs/processors, analog & power management, RF, connectivity, and security — run as one reportable segment but managed across four end markets (10-K, 2026-02-19). FY2025 revenue $12,269m (vs $12,614m FY24, -3% YoY in a down-cycle). End-market mix: Automotive ~$7.1bn (~58%), Industrial & IoT ~$2.3bn (~19%), Mobile ~$1.6bn (~13%), Communication Infrastructure & Other ~$1.3bn (~10%) (Q4 FY25 call, 2026-02-03). NXP is the most auto-levered of the analog/embedded peers (vs TXN/ADI at ~33% auto) — the franchise sits on content-per-vehicle (radar, S32 processors, electrification, secure car access, networking) rather than unit growth. Heavy distribution channel: one distributor was 23% of revenue in 2025 (10-K, 2026-02-19). CEO transition complete: Rafael Sotomayor took over from Kurt Sievers (30-yr veteran) at the Q3 FY25 call (2025-10-28); Bill Betz CFO.

Position in the value chain

NXP runs a hybrid manufacturing model — internal front-end wafer fabs plus joint-venture fabs (VSMC/BSMC with TSMC in Singapore, ESMC in Dresden) and third-party foundries (TSMC) for advanced nodes; back-end assembly/test is mixed internal + subcontract (10-K, 2026-02-19). It designs automotive + industrial/IoT processors & analog, connectivity, and secure chips and sells, largely through distribution (~80% of I&IoT), into auto OEMs/Tier-1s, industrial, and IoT/edge customers. Because ~58% of revenue is automotive, the model carries the highest auto-cycle leverage in the analog/embedded peer set — content-per-vehicle (SDV, radar, electrification) is the secular driver, but the print swings with vehicle production and Tier-1 inventory.

Current state (latest quarter)

Q1 FY2026 (quarter ended 2026-03-29, call 2026-04-28): Revenue $3.18bn, +12% YoY, -5% QoQ — $31m above guide midpoint. Non-GAAP GM 57.1%, op margin 33% (+120bps YoY), EPS $3.05 (+$0.08). By end market: Automotive ~$1.78bn +6% YoY (+10% adjusted for divestitures; growth "not a story about unit growth" — content/SDV, Sotomayor), Industrial & IoT ~$628m +24% YoY (new i.MX/RP/MCX processors +75% YoY), Mobile ~$391m +16% YoY, Comm Infra ~$380m +21% YoY (10-Q, 2026-04-28; Q1 FY26 call). Data center >$500m guided for 2026 (vs ~$200m in 2025) on control-plane apps — accretive to GM (Betz). Channel inventory held at 11 weeks (target reached); DIO 165 days. Q2 FY26 guide: revenue $3.45bn (+18% YoY, +8% QoQ), GM 58%, op margin 34.7%, EPS $3.50 — a sharp sequential acceleration consistent with the up-cycle. Reaffirmed Analyst Day targets: double-digit revenue growth 2026 & 2027, GM toward 60%+. Portfolio cleanup ongoing: RF Power discontinued ($90m restructuring, drove the comms-infra reset); MEMS sensors sold to STMicro (~$900m proceeds, ~$300m revenue out, ~$630m GAAP gain booked Q1'26) (Q4 FY25 call, 2026-02-03).

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: Cyclical inflection is live and NXP is the highest-beta way to play it — Sievers called "the beginning of a new up cycle" at Q2 FY25 (2025-07-22), and the Q2 FY26 guide (+18% YoY) confirms the acceleration. Content-per-vehicle (S32N/S32K5 zonal processors, imaging radar, 10GbE, electrification) and a doubling of data-center revenue to >$500m give NXP-specific growth on top of the auto recovery (Q1 FY26 call, 2026-04-28). VSMC/ESMC JV ramp targets ~200bps of GM expansion by 2028 while capex normalizes to ~3% of revenue. NXP screened on Redburn's 12m relative-highs list with the broader semi/analog complex (briefing 2026-05-26). Sell-side reads NXP positively into the analog recovery alongside TXN/ADI/ON (GS Schneider, briefing 2026-05-20).
  • Bear: Auto concentration (~58%) is the double-edged sword — the most exposed name if vehicle production or Tier-1 restocking disappoints, and management itself flags Tier-1 inventory still below NXP cycle time (Q3 FY25 call, 2025-10-28). China is both opportunity and risk: "extremely competitive" local-for-local pressure plus tariff/export-control overhang (Q2 FY25 call; 10-K, 2026-02-19). The recovery is guidance-dependent — Q1 FY26 was still -5% QoQ, and the +18% YoY Q2 guide sets a high bar. Comms-infra remains structurally lower post-RF-Power exit. NXP gets thin standalone desk coverage in the briefing flow — mostly tagged as a read-through/peer on ADI/TXN prints (briefings 2026-05-20, 2026-06-03, 2026-06-16) — so it is under-followed vs the analog leaders.
  • Where the sell-side/buy-side stands: No standalone NXPI desk note in the recent briefing archive — NXP appears as analog-complex read-through on peer prints. GS (Jim Schneider) framed strong analog results as positive for the group incl. NXPI (briefing 2026-05-20); JPM (Harlan Sur) bundles NXPI in OW semi baskets (briefing 2026-05-20); flagged at 12m relative highs by Redburn (briefing 2026-05-26). The crowded analog trade is long TXN / short ADI; NXP is the auto-leveraged satellite to that debate (briefings 2026-06-03, 2026-06-17). See BBG consensus block below for current Street numbers.

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q2 FY26 print — call ~late-Jul 2026 (guide $3.45bn / EPS $3.50, +18% YoY): confirmation of the sequential acceleration is the key catalyst.
  • Data center >$500m 2026 trajectory: control-plane ramp and GM accretion (Q1 FY26 call).
  • Auto content ramps: S32N/S32K5 zonal processors (TTTech reference design Q3 sampling), imaging radar, 10GbE, electrification design wins (Q1 FY26 call).
  • VSMC/ESMC JV milestones: ~$3.4bn investment cycle (~50%+ complete), GM uplift toward 2028; capex normalization to ~3% of revenue.
  • China auto / EV demand and tariff/export-control headlines — local-for-local competitive intensity (Q2/Q3 FY25 calls; 10-K).
  • Edge-AI integration: Kinara ($1bn+ funnel, 30+ POCs), Aviva Links SerDes (production 2028) (Q1 FY26 call).

Risks

  • Auto-cycle concentration: ~58% of revenue from automotive — highest in the peer set; vehicle-production and Tier-1 inventory swings drive the print (10-K, 2026-02-19; Q3 FY25 call).
  • China / trade: local competition, tariffs, export controls; products sold across all major regions with material China exposure (10-K, 2026-02-19; Q2 FY25 call).
  • Distribution concentration: one distributor = 23% of 2025 revenue — channel-inventory dynamics (9→11 weeks) amplify quarterly volatility (10-K, 2026-02-19).
  • Hybrid-manufacturing / utilization: legacy 200mm fab consolidation and JV ramp; underutilization is a GM drag in soft quarters (Q2 FY25 call; 10-K).
  • Comms-infra structural reset: RF Power discontinuation lowers the comms-infra base over a 2+ year wind-down (Q4 FY25 call).
  • Guidance dependence / valuation: +18% YoY Q2 guide and 12m relative-high positioning leave little room for a cyclical disappointment (briefing 2026-05-26).

In the inbox (Outlook — recent sell-side flow)

  • JPM (Samik) 'Watts Up! 800V' _(2026-06-09)_: power-stack read-through; 'VIVEK on Analog' _(2026-06-09)_ + BofA analog call _(2026-06-09)_: auto/industrial recovery — NXP the highest auto-beta name.

MCHP — Microchip Technology Incorporated

Wiki · generated 2026-06-19 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\MCHP (filings + transcripts) · _briefings roll-up. Master index: ../INDEX.md.

Snapshot

Broad-line embedded-control semi: 8/16/32/64-bit mixed-signal microcontrollers, analog, FPGA, networking/connectivity, and data-center/memory products, sold as a "Total System Solution" into industrial, automotive, aerospace & defense, communications, data center and consumer. FY26 (ended Mar-31-26) net sales $4,713.1M, +7.1% y/y, split MCU $2,355.4M (50.0%), Analog $1,329.0M (28.2%), Other (incl. FPGA, DC, licensing) $1,028.7M (21.8%) (10-K FY26, 2026-05-21). ~75% of sales foreign (China 18%, Taiwan 15%); ~35% of FY26 sales from own US fabs, balance outsourced (10-K FY26). The story is mechanical cyclical torque: FY24 sales were $7,634.4M at the peak — MCHP cut almost in half through the destocking trough and is now climbing back, with operating leverage doing the heavy lifting.

Position in the value chain

MCHP buys wafers/substrates from external foundries (advanced nodes) and runs its own US fabs (~35% internal), then ships microcontrollers + analog + FPGA + connectivity into late-cycle industrial / auto / aero-defense / data-center customers. The torque sits at both ends: a thin sliver of incremental volume off the destocked base drops through at ~95% incremental gross margin (FQ2 FY26), while substrate/foundry tightness ("70-80% of nodes constrained," Q4 FY26) now caps how fast it can convert a book-to-bill well above 1.0.

End-market mix from FY26 Q4 call (2026-05-07).

Current state (latest quarter)

  • Q4 FY26 (ended Mar-31-26, reported 2026-05-07): Net sales $1.311B, +10.6% q/q, +35.1% y/y, above the high end of guide; cons ~$1.26B. Non-GAAP EPS $0.57 ($0.07 above midpoint, cons ~$0.50). Non-GAAP GM 61.6% (incl. $46.6M underutilization), op margin 30.6%, adj EBITDA $466.8M (Q4 FY26 call).
  • Recovery cadence: four straight up quarters off the trough — $1.075B (Q1) → $1.14B (Q2) → $1.186B (Q3) → $1.311B (Q4), with GM marching 54.3% → 56.7% → 60.5% → 61.6% (FY26 transcripts). The latest reported quarter (FQ3 FY26 in the 10-Q, ended Dec-25) showed net sales $1,186.0M vs $1,026.0M PY, GM 59.6% (10-Q 2026-02-05).
  • Inventory: company DOI 185 days (-16 q/q), distributor 26 days (historic low); sell-through +11.4%; April was the largest booking month in ~4 years, book-to-bill well above 1.0 (Q4 FY26 call).
  • Supply-gated upside: "We are constrained on substrates… 70% to 80% of process technology nodes are constrained" (Sanghi, Q4 FY26). Lead times extending; delinquencies rising "not at crisis level."
  • Balance sheet: total indebtedness $5.54B (10-K FY26); net debt/adj EBITDA 3.54x (from 4.18x prior qtr); buybacks paused for deleveraging; quarterly dividend $0.455/sh declared 2026-05-07 (10-K FY26). Capex tiny (~$92M FY26; ~$100M FY27 guide).
  • Guide — FQ1 FY27 (Jun-26): net sales +11% q/q (±1%) ≈ $1.44-1.47B (cons ~$1.34B); non-GAAP EPS $0.67-0.71, ~19% above Street (Q4 FY26 call; briefing 2026-05-08).

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: Textbook deep-cyclical recovery with extreme operating leverage — incremental GM ~95% / incremental op margin ~84.6% q/q at the early stage (Q2 FY26 call); LT model 65% GM / 40% op margin vs 61.6%/30.6% today (Q4 FY26). Distributor inventory at historic lows (26 days), book-to-bill >1.0, supply tightness building pricing/allocation power. New growth legs in data center (FY26 18% of mix; PCIe Gen6 switches, retimers, CXL controllers) and aero/defense (16%, NATO + aircraft ramp). UBS (Arcuri) reit Buy, "prefers TXN/MCHP for op leverage / later-downcycle play"; the FQ4 guide grew 11% with EPS ~19% above cons "validates the re-rating call" (briefings 2026-05-08, 2026-05-19).
  • Bear: Stock already re-rated hard (+35.1% y/y revenue, shares ran into the print). MS (Moore) reit EW, $94 — frames the DC disclosure as in-line and the price hikes as "defensive, not offensive," and flags MCHP's DC sub-segment as "not dissimilar to peers in size/growth but lagging TXN and ADI" (UBS Arcuri's own caveat, briefings 2026-05-08, 2026-06-02). Leverage still elevated (3.54x), China 18% of sales into intensifying local competition, and the recovery is now supply-gated — "Can I grow a business 50% in one quarter? No" (Sanghi). Automotive was the laggard end-market through the cycle.
  • Where the Street stands (briefings): GS reit Buy (Schneider); UBS reit Buy (Arcuri); JPM "Recovery Inflection"; Citi Buy (Malik); Barclays constructive; MS EW $94 (Moore) (2026-05-08). Read-through anchor for the analog complex (ADI/ON/NXPI/STM/IFX/POWI) — peers raising DC guides on the same inflection (2026-06-02). No Outlook desk notes pulled this session — see Sources.

Catalysts / what to watch

  • FQ1 FY27 print (late July/early Aug 2026) — does the +11% q/q guide land, and does GM cross into the low-60s toward the 65% target.
  • Substrate/foundry constraint trajectory — whether tightness becomes a pricing tailwind or a shipment cap.
  • Data-center ramps: PCIe Gen6 switch large win → production CQ1 2027 ($100M+ CY27 rev); retimers/CXL controllers (Q3-Q4 FY26 calls).
  • Deleveraging pace toward the 1.5x net-debt/EBITDA target; buyback resumption signal.
  • Tempe (Fab) sale completion — final piece of the nine-point recovery plan.

Risks

  • Supply-side cap on the recovery (substrate/foundry constraints on 70-80% of nodes) limiting near-term shipment upside (Q4 FY26).
  • Elevated leverage ($5.54B debt, 3.54x) and convertible/senior debt maturities; dividend competes with deleveraging (10-K FY26).
  • China 18% of sales amid intense local competition; ~75% foreign-customer concentration and tariff exposure (10-K FY26).
  • Distributor channel: no long-term purchase agreements, terminable with little notice; Arrow ~12% of sales (10-K FY26).
  • Demand round-trip risk if the macro/auto recovery stalls before utilization (and underutilization charges) normalize.

In the inbox (Outlook — recent sell-side flow)

  • 'VIVEK on Analog' expert call _(2026-06-09)_ + BofA TMT analog call _(2026-06-09)_: MCU/analog destocking-recovery torque; MS Equal-Weight ~$94 (per briefings), DC franchise lagging TXN/ADI.

ON — ON Semiconductor (onsemi)

Wiki · generated 2026-06-19 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\ON (10-K/10-Q filings + transcripts) · equity calls E:\equity_calls_transcripts · briefings E:\briefings\2026. Master index: 00_INDEX.md.

Snapshot

onsemi is a US-listed IDM intelligent-power and intelligent-sensing vendor — power discretes/modules (Si MOSFETs/IGBTs, SiC "EliteSiC", GaN incl. vertical GaN), analog/mixed-signal, and CMOS image sensors. Three reportable segments: Power Solutions Group (PSG, 47% of FY25 revenue), Analog & Mixed-Signal Group (AMG, 38%), Intelligent Sensing Group (ISG, 15%) (10-K, 2026-02-09). FY2025 revenue $6.0bn (Q4 FY25 call, 2026-02-09). By end-market: Automotive 51%, Industrial 28%, Other 21% (Other = AI data center, computing, consumer, networking) (10-K, 2026-02-09). The franchise is a cyclical auto/industrial power + image-sensor business — bottoming after seven quarters of auto decline — now layering a structural AI-datacenter-power (800V DC) ramp that is re-rating the analog/power group.

Position in the value chain

onsemi is vertically integrated in SiC — it brings substrate/boule growth largely in-house to assure supply, control cost and differentiate device performance (10-K, 2026-02-09) — then fabs/packages power devices, analog and image sensors and sells into automotive/EV, industrial (incl. energy storage), and AI data-center power. The incremental driver is 800V DC: management quantifies content at ~$115k per 800V rack vs ~$9.5k at standard 120kW, with AI-datacenter revenue set to double YoY in 2026 off a >$250m FY25 base (Q1 FY26 call, 2026-05-04). Citi frames onsemi as uniquely differentiated via vertical GaN (GaN leads sub-650V at the rack; SiC better >1,000V infrastructure) (Citi, briefing 2026-06-15). The same SiC franchise carries EV/industrial cyclical exposure — SiC GM sits below corporate average on underutilization. See 800v-dc-power · ai-datacenter-power.

Current state (latest quarter)

Q1 FY26 (quarter ended 2026-04-03, call 2026-05-04): Revenue $1.51bn, -1% QoQ / +5% YoY, above midpoint. Non-GAAP gross margin 38.5%, up sequentially for the third straight quarter despite seasonality; non-GAAP EPS $0.64. GAAP was a small net loss of $33.4m (-$0.08) driven by $329m of restructuring/impairment (2026 Manufacturing Realignment Program — ~650 heads, $147m non-cash equipment impairment) (10-Q, 2026-05-04). Segments: PSG $737m (+14% YoY, GM 27.2%, driven by auto/AI-DC), AMG $540m (-5% YoY, GM 53.6%), ISG $236m (+1% YoY, GM swung to +39.4% from -56% a year ago after FY25 inventory write-downs) (10-Q, 2026-05-04). End-markets: Auto $797m (flat QoQ, +5% YoY — ends seven-quarter decline), Industrial $417m (-6% QoQ), Other $299m (+5%). AI data center +30%+ QoQ, doubled YoY; guided to double again in 2026. Utilization up to 77% (from 68% in Q4). LTSA remaining performance obligations ~$6.5bn. Q2 FY26 guide: revenue $1.535–1.635bn, GM 38–40%, EPS $0.65–0.77 (Q1 FY26 call, 2026-05-04). Long-term model: 53% GM / 40% op margin / 25–30% FCF margin (Q4 FY25 call, 2026-02-09).

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: Cyclical trough is in — auto grew YoY for the first time in seven quarters and gross margin is climbing through the downturn (38.2% Q4 FY25 → 38.5% Q1 FY26) on FabRite footprint cuts (12% capacity reduction FY25), so incremental volume drops through at high margins toward the 53% target (Q1 FY26 / Q4 FY25 calls). The structural kicker is AI data-center power: >$250m FY25, doubling in 2026, with ~$115k of content per 800V rack and a uniquely complete "wall-to-core" power tree plus vertical GaN (first revenue 2027) and the Qorvo SiC JFET acquisition feeding AC-DC PSU content (Q1/Q3 FY25 calls; Citi 800V note, briefing 2026-06-15). Citi has onsemi on a 90-day upside Catalyst Watch into the Sept 16 Analyst Day, expecting a raised target model + renewed SiC momentum (Citi/Atif Malik, briefing 2026-06-15). FCF $1.4bn FY25 (24% margin) funds a $6bn buyback (~100% of FCF returned) (Q4 FY25 call).
  • Bear: It is a share game, not a TAM game — Citi's 800V top pick is TXN ("DC power share gains 2H26… gaining share vs Infineon/MPWR/Renesas"), with onsemi only Neutral; the GaN-at-rack vs SiC-at-infrastructure split means onsemi competes against TXN's quadrupled high-voltage GaN capacity (Citi, briefing 2026-06-15). Channel checks push the 800V earnings impact out 2–3 years — "every DC construction PM… no plans to do this anytime soon," and China can flood SiC/GaN like it did InP (@bubbleboi, briefing 2026-06-15; @trendforce, briefing 2026-06-17). The legacy book remains the drag: SiC GM below corporate average on underutilization, ~5% of FY25 revenue exiting in 2026, China EV/passenger weakness, and a heavy multi-year restructuring tab (>$0.9bn of charges across FY25 + Q1 FY26).
  • Where the sell-side/buy-side stands: Citi Neutral + 90-day upside catalyst watch into the Sept 16 Analyst Day (Citi/Atif Malik, briefing 2026-06-15). onsemi is in the FinTwit/crowd 800V DC basket at ~$46.2bn mkt cap, #2 behind Infineon (briefing 2026-06-08). BofA's 800V "best ideas" group (TXN/ADI/ON) cites onsemi as a beneficiary, but the desk's top power-semi picks skew TXN/ADI (per IFX page, BofA Arya, briefing 2026-05-26). Mostly false-positive inbox noise vs ONON (Swiss running shoes) through May (briefings 2026-05-12 / 05-28 / 06-07).

Catalysts / what to watch

  • 2026-09-16 — onsemi Analyst Day (Citi exp. ~Sep 13 print first) — the key event; watch for a raised long-term target model + SiC/800V framing (Citi, briefing 2026-06-15).
  • Q2 FY26 print (~early Aug 2026) — guide is $1.535–1.635bn / 38–40% GM; watch AI-DC doubling pace, utilization (77%→?), and auto/industrial sequential trajectory.
  • AI-DC revenue doubling YoY in 2026 — quarterly proof points on the >$250m FY25 base; 800V rack content ramp with high-voltage PSU makers.
  • Vertical GaN first revenue in 2027 + GaN design funnel (>$1.5bn, 20 products sampling 2026) — onsemi's differentiated 800V angle.
  • GM 800V/EV-traction VGaN collaboration and Treo platform toward $1bn (East Fishkill ramp).
  • Restructuring tail — additional FabRite / 2026 Manufacturing Realignment actions; depreciation down $45–50m in 2026.

Risks

  • Auto/EV + industrial cyclicality: 79% of revenue is auto+industrial; China passenger-vehicle softness, EV mix, tariff-driven order hesitancy (10-K 2026-02-09; Q2 FY25 call).
  • 800V share & timing: TXN/IFX/STM/MPWR/Renesas competing for the same content; channel checks flag a 2–3yr lag to earnings (Citi/@bubbleboi, briefing 2026-06-15).
  • SiC underutilization: SiC GM below corporate average; gross margin highly utilization-sensitive (68%→77% recovery still well below optimal) (Q1 FY26 / Q2 FY25 calls).
  • China supply flood: Chinese SiC/GaN vendors challenging IDM incumbents; potential oversupply/pricing pressure (@trendforce, briefing 2026-06-17; 10-K trade-restriction risk factors).
  • AI-demand / responsible-AI risk factor: 10-K explicitly flags that AI-power products "may not capture market share as expected" and AI deployment/returns could be materially lower than expected (10-K 2026-02-09, Item 1A).
  • Manufacturing concentration & restructuring execution: interdependent multi-facility network — a single-site disruption is disproportionate; large ongoing realignment charges (10-K 2026-02-09).
  • Customer/distributor concentration: one distributor ~12% of revenue Q1 FY26 (10-Q 2026-05-04).

In the inbox (Outlook — recent sell-side flow)

  • A two-pass Outlook pull was attempted (outlook.py --no-body --days 14) but returned no usable output in this environment (no callable mailbox / empty result). Sell-side stance below is therefore sourced from the briefing archive, not a live inbox.
  • Citi (Atif Malik) _(briefing 2026-06-15)_: Neutral, opened a 90-day upside Catalyst Watch into the Sept 16 Analyst Day; 800V DC note flags onsemi's vertical-GaN differentiation; analog units still ~30–40% below prior peaks, mature-node utilization ~80% vs optimal 85–90%.
  • Recurring caution: most "ON" inbox hits are false positives for ONON (Swiss running shoes) — genuine onsemi flow is thin (briefings 2026-05-12 / 05-28 / 06-07).

ANET — Arista Networks

Wiki · generated 2026-06-18 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\ANET (filings + transcripts) · _equity_calls · _briefings\by-ticker\ANET.md. Master index: ../INDEX.md.

🔖 Themes: Optical / CPO (Co-Packaged Optics & AI Interconnect)

Snapshot

Arista is the merchant high-end data-center / AI Ethernet switching vendor — software (EOS / CloudVision) layered on Broadcom-class merchant silicon, sold to the cloud titans, AI/specialty providers and enterprise. FY2025 revenue $9.0B, +28.6% YoY; mix: Cloud & AI Titans 48%, Enterprise & Financials 32%, AI & Specialty Providers (incl. Apple, Oracle, Anthropic) 20% (Q4 FY25 call, 2026-02-12). By product, core cloud/AI/DC ~65%, campus & routing ~18%, software/services subscription ~17%. The core debate is leverage to AI back-end Ethernet (scale-out 800G Etherlink today, scale-across in 2026, scale-up 2027+) against the question of how fast a record backlog/deferred balance converts to revenue, and gross-margin pressure from component inflation.

Current state (latest quarter — Q1 FY26, call 2026-05-05)

Beat-and-raise gated by supply. Q1 FY26 revenue $2.709B (+35.1% YoY), above the $2.6B guide; non-GAAP GM 62.4% (in 62–63% guide); non-GAAP op margin 47.8%; diluted EPS $0.87 (+31.8% YoY, vs ~$0.808 consensus); record OCF $1.69B (Q1 FY26 call, 2026-05-05).

  • Demand vs supply is the whole story. Ullal: "Our demand is actually the best I have ever seen in my Arista tenure"; supply is "a slightly different and opposite tale… demand is outstripping our supply" across wafers, silicon, CPUs, optics, memory, with ~52-week lead times. Purchase commitments raised to $8.9B (from $6.8B prior quarter); deferred-revenue acceptance cycles stretched to 6–8 quarters vs 2–4 historically (Q1 FY26 call, 2026-05-05).
  • Guidance raised. FY26 revenue to $11.5B (+27.7%) from prior $11.25B/25%; AI fabrics target $3.5B (from $3.25B, >2x prior year); campus $1.25B held; GM 62–64%, op margin ~46%. Q2 FY26: revenue ~$2.8B, EPS ~$0.88 (Q1 FY26 call, 2026-05-05).
  • Margin watch. GM stepped down from FY25's 64.6% toward 62–64% on memory/silicon cost inflation. Q4 FY25 (2026-02-12), Ullal on memory: "the situation has worsened significantly… the prices are horrendous" — flagging selective price increases on memory-intensive SKUs.
  • Product/roadmap: 800G Etherlink >100 cumulative customers; >150M cumulative Ethernet ports; three customers with >100k-GPU Ethernet deployments; XPO optics (12.8 Tbps/module) toward 1.6T/3.2T; founding member ESUN scale-up standard; UEC 1.0 complete (Q4 FY25, 2026-02-12; Q1 FY26, 2026-05-05).

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: AI front-end and traditional-compute network intensity is being structurally underestimated. 650 Group (Alan Weckel, 2026-06-04): agentic/MoE inference is pushing front-end from "one front-end port per 80 GPUs to one to four," everything moving to 400G+; data-center switching is a "40%+ growth this year… well north of $100B by end of decade" market where it is "not a winner take all." Scale-across is "~$500M last year… going to a $10B market." MS (Meta Marshall, 2026-05-12) raised FY25–28 revenue CAGR to ~20%+ from mid-teens and called the ~20% post-print pullback "overdone"; PT $190 (via Tom Wigg, 2026-06-12) on agentic front-end refresh, Dell'Oro AI front-end $1.8B'25→$6.3B'30 (MS up to ~$10B). Supply constraints are read as a positive by some: Tal Liani (BofA wrap, 2026-06-08) — constraints "actually help them because… they still have a backlog to grow." Google Virgo architecture seen as more Arista/Ethernet-friendly, opening a branded software-value lane (650 Group, 2026-06-04; JPM Meyers, 2026-06-08).
  • Bear: Backlog/deferred conversion risk into rich valuation. Tal Liani (BofA wrap, 2026-06-08) — across the big-three networking names, backlog/deferred "tripled, quadrupled" over four years; as Oracle/Microsoft builds finish 2H26, "these backlogs will decline… unless the orders are extremely strong to offset it" — flags it explicitly "because of the valuation." Note Street is at +34–35% FY26 vs ANET's 28% guide, i.e. a gap to defend. JPM (Samik Chatterjee) ran an "ANET: The Lost Charm" note and AMA "How to Get Its Groove Back" (2026-06-08/09) — relative underperformance vs AI-infra peers; EPS 2027E ~$5 referenced. GM compression from memory/silicon is real and ongoing (Q4 FY25, 2026-02-12). Deferred-rev practice is an idiosyncratic black box — Arista books long SLA acceptance windows that peers (CSCO, NVDA) do not, a startup-era legacy that lags revenue recognition (650 Group, 2026-06-04). Competitive: Cisco Silicon One has closed the gap to ~1–2 quarters at 800G and supply-chain resilience may be a "durable advantage"; NVIDIA strongest in neo-clouds; HPE/Nokia gaining share in the rising tide (650 Group, 2026-06-04).
  • Where the sell-side stands: MS (Marshall) OW, PT $180→$190 (2026-05-12 / 2026-06-12). Citi (Atif Malik) Buy, PT lowered to $176 on supply constraints (2026-05-06); Citi also flagged the 1.6T rack-scale portfolio launch with Meta/Microsoft/Oracle + AMD/AVGO silicon (2026-06-12). JPM (Chatterjee) constructive-but-critical / reset narrative, no rating change (2026-06-08). Barclays desk read: "well debated but… investors are positive, just some disagreement on how quickly deferred will turn into revenue" (2026-05-22).

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q2 FY26 print (guide ~$2.8B rev / ~$0.88 EPS) — the key tell on whether the 28% guide closes the gap to Street's 34–35%, and on GM trajectory.
  • 1.6T rack-scale portfolio ramp: per Citi (2026-06-12), 1 platform launches Q4'26, 2 in Q1'27 — partners Meta/Microsoft/Oracle, silicon AMD + AVGO.
  • Scale-across inflection (2026) — management targets ≥1/3 of AI revenue; scale-up (2027+) on eSun specs is the next leg.
  • Microsoft InfiniBand→Ethernet shift in 2026 (Tal Liani, 2026-06-08) — supportive for back-end; watch front-end deceleration offset.
  • OCP (October 2026) as the industry scale-up / CPO milestone window (650 Group, 2026-06-04).
  • Deferred-revenue balance trajectory — management said it will "flatten out"; the 2H26 hyperscaler build completions are the test.

Risks

  • Customer concentration: two customers were 16–20% of FY25 revenue; 1–2 additional 10%+ customers expected in 2026 — order/topology decisions by a handful of titans swing the model (Q4 FY25, 2026-02-12).
  • Supply chain: ~52-week lead times, $8.9B purchase commitments locked multiyear; component shortfall caps revenue near term (Q1 FY26, 2026-05-05).
  • Gross-margin erosion from memory ("horrendous" prices) and TSMC-concentrated silicon cost inflation (Q4 FY25, 2026-02-12).
  • Deferred-revenue volatility / conversion — long acceptance windows make quarters unpredictable; backlog drawdown risk as builds complete (CFO Breithaupt, Q4 FY25; Tal Liani, 2026-06-08).
  • Competition / share — Cisco Silicon One gap-closing + supply resilience, NVIDIA in neo-clouds, HPE/Nokia gaining; white-box (Celestica) in software-light workloads (650 Group, 2026-06-04).

CIEN — Ciena Corporation

Wiki · generated 2026-06-18 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\CIEN (filings + transcripts + decks) · _equity_calls · _briefings\by-ticker\CIEN.md. Master index: ../INDEX.md.

🔖 Themes: Optical / CPO (Co-Packaged Optics & AI Interconnect)

Snapshot

Ciena is the merchant leader in high-speed optical networking — the "highways" that carry data across metro, long-haul, subsea and now data-center interconnect (DCI). Three revenue pillars: Systems (optical line systems/RLS + WaveLogic coherent modems, plus Routing & Switching incl. DCOM), Interconnects (400G/800G ZR coherent pluggables + the Nubis CPO/linear-redriver assets), and Software & Services (Navigator/Blue Planet + install/maintenance). Customer base splits roughly into cloud/hyperscalers and service providers; in Q2-FY26 two cloud customers were each >10% of revenue and together ~1/3 of the quarter (Q2 FY26, 2026-06-04). The thesis is that Ciena sits directly in the path of AI-driven network spend — both the WAN connecting AI data centers ("scale across") and increasingly inside/around the campus.

Current state (latest quarter)

Q2 FY26 (2026-06-04) — another record, well above guide:

  • Revenue $1.57B, +40% YoY, $71M above guide top (Q2 FY26, 2026-06-04). Optical +42% (RLS and Waveserver each +>55%); Routing & Switching +88% on DCOM ramp.
  • Direct cloud revenue +70% YoY; service providers +28% (India SP revenue >2x YoY on MOFN). Adjusted GM 44.9% (+90bps vs guide, +4pts YoY); adj. op margin 19.5%; adj. EPS $1.64 (~4x YoY). FCF $219M (13.9% of rev); cash $1.4B; $83M buyback at avg $371.
  • Backlog $7.7B, +$600M QoQ (~$6.4B hardware, ~80% deliverable in next 12mo) — "visibility well beyond historical norms," confidence into 2027 (Marc Graff, Q2 FY26). For context, backlog was ~$5B at Q4-FY25 exit and jumped ~$2B to ~$7B in Q1-FY26 (Q1 FY26, 2026-03-05).
  • Guidance raised again (3rd time this FY): FY26 revenue $6.3B ±$100M (~+32% YoY), GM 44.5–45%, op margin ~19%. Q3 rev ~$1.625B, GM ~45%. (Q2 FY26)
  • New disclosures: industry's first multi-rail (RLS Hyper-Rail) order from a leading hyperscaler — deals "hundreds of millions over multiple years," revenue starts/linear in '27; DCOM second hyperscaler order + third in qual; coherent-module competitive takeaway win at a major hyperscaler; WaveLogic 5/6 Nano design-in at a major switch OEM (Gary Smith, Q2 FY26). TAM framed to ~double to ~$50B by 2029 (Q2 FY26).

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: Demand structurally outstripping supply through '26 into '27; backlog "going into the ground, not warehouses," with no COVID-style cancellations/push-outs (McFeely/Graff, Q2 FY26). ~70%+ photonics share with hyperscalers and a deep software/integration/services moat that component vendors struggle to cross (Ciena equity call, 2026-06-12). Margin tailwinds compounding: vertical integration (WaveLogic 6 + Nano "everything in there is Ciena"), 250+ cost-reduction programs, "value exchange"/pricing, and structurally higher-margin Hyper-Rail and DCOM — 45% GM is a "waypoint, not the destination" (Ciena equity call, 2026-06-12). JPM (Chatterjee) OW, PT $635, ~90% EPS CAGR FY25-28E (briefing, 2026-06-05). Citi (Atif Malik) PT raised to $658 on the print (briefing, 2026-06-05).
  • Bear: Valuation. UBS (Vogt) Neutral — "85x NTM = misaligned expectations," limited supply/margin upside (briefing, 2026-06-05); had FY26 EPS $6.75 vs Street $6.18 going in (briefing, 2026-05-31). Redburn's Mike (Rothschild call, 2026-05-19) loves the story but "can't get my numbers high enough to justify the share price" — even assuming a $7B scale-across TAM and ~50% Ciena share by 2030 he barely reaches the price; stock at ~2.5x PEG, on the sidelines. Tail risk: backlog draws down faster than orders refill once large data centers turn up live traffic (Ciena equity call, 2026-06-12). Supply is the swing factor in both directions — memory, pump lasers, ceramic packaging are the three constraints (Ciena equity call, 2026-06-12).
  • Where the sell-side stands: Split. OW/Buy JPM ($635), Citi ($658); EW MS (Marshall/Jaramillo) PT $490 (37x FY28E $13.26, supply gating near-term); Neutral UBS (Vogt) (briefing, 2026-06-05). Bull/bear gap is almost entirely multiple, not the growth trajectory — that's the actionable disagreement.

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q3 FY26 print (guide ~$1.625B / ~45% GM) — watch backlog trajectory (mgmt expects it to keep rising) and whether Q4 GM implied dip materializes (Graff downplayed it, Q2 FY26).
  • RLS Hyper-Rail GA / first revenue in FY27 — linear ramp, multi-hyperscaler + wholesale/MOFN SP adoption; the key margin-mix lever.
  • Nubis: Nitro linear redriver GA "this summer" (final chip back, performing well); Vesta 6.4T CPO samples — scale-up/scale-out ramp into '27/'28; mgmt frames ~$5B opportunity over 3-4 yrs (Q2 FY26; equity call 2026-06-12).
  • 800G pluggable ramp — mgmt targets >2x pluggable revenue vs 2025, sees exiting the year #1 or near-#1 share at 800G (equity call, 2026-06-12).
  • Coherent-light into the campus/data center, late 2027–2028 at 1.6T–3.2T (McFeely, Q2 FY26).
  • Supply/LTA progress — signing supplier LTAs paired with customer demand commitments; pump-laser and ceramic-packaging easing is the gating item for upside.

Risks

  • Customer concentration — small number of customers, two cloud providers ~1/3 of Q2 revenue; hyperscaler capex/AI digestion risk (10-K 2025-12-12 risk factors; Q2 FY26).
  • Supply chain — memory (HBM crowd-out), pump lasers for amplifiers, ceramic packaging; constraints capped revenue and could cap GM if inflation outpaces cost-downs (equity call, 2026-06-12; Q1 FY26).
  • Backlog durability — risk that orders fail to refill as live traffic converts backlog to revenue post-buildout (equity call, 2026-06-12).
  • Competition / tech transitions — Nokia/Cisco in coherent + line systems; Marvell, Broadcom as coherent/CPO move inside the data center; component vendors attempting to move up into systems (McFeely, Q2 FY26; equity call 2026-06-12).
  • Valuation de-rate — high-multiple AI name; any growth or supply wobble amplified (UBS, 2026-06-05; Redburn, 2026-05-19).

COHR — Coherent Corp.

Wiki · generated 2026-06-18 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\COHR (filings + transcripts + decks) · _equity_calls · _briefings\by-ticker\COHR.md. Master index: ../INDEX.md.

🔖 Themes: Optical / CPO (Co-Packaged Optics & AI Interconnect)

Snapshot

Coherent is a vertically-integrated photonics maker that has re-rated into one of the cleanest large-cap plays on AI optical interconnect. The story is the Data Center & Communications segment, now ~75% of revenue (Q3 FY26, 2026-05-06), spanning datacom transceivers (800G/1.6T), DCI/scale-across coherent transceivers (ZR/ZR+), optical circuit switching (OCS), and an emerging co-packaged optics (CPO) franchise. The legacy Industrial segment (lasers, engineered materials, semicap) is the smaller, cyclical balance. Coherent's structural edge is owning the indium phosphide (InP) supply chain — EMLs, CW lasers, photodiodes — which is the binding constraint for the entire transceiver industry. Per the 10-K (FY25, 2025-08-15) the legacy reporting structure is three segments (Networking, Materials, Lasers); management now guides on the DC&Comms vs Industrial split. CEO Jim Anderson, CFO Sherri Luther.

Current state (latest quarter)

Q3 FY26 (2026-05-06): Record revenue of $1.8B, +7% q/q and +21% y/y reported; +9% q/q and +27% y/y pro forma (ex-A&D and the divested Munich division). Non-GAAP gross margin 39.6%, +57bp q/q and +105bp y/y; non-GAAP operating margin 20.3%; non-GAAP EPS $1.41, +55% y/y. DC&Comms +>40% y/y; within it, data center +13% q/q / +37% y/y (second consecutive quarter of double-digit sequential growth) and communications +16% q/q / +60% y/y on DCI/scale-across. Industrial down modestly but semicap bookings inflecting. Backlog at a record; orders now extend into CY2028 and LTAs to end of decade; Q2 FY26 data center book-to-bill exceeded 4x (2026-02-04).

Balance sheet de-levered hard: net debt leverage 0.5x, down from 1.7x in Q2 and 2.1x a year ago; cash jumped to $3B after NVIDIA's $2B equity investment (announced 2026-03-02). Capex stepped up to $290M (vs $154M prior) and guided higher again — pure capacity-add. Q4 FY26 guide: revenue $1.91–2.05B, GM 39–41%, non-GAAP EPS $1.52–1.72 — an implied acceleration. Anderson framed the June quarter as "a new inflection point" and reiterated FY27 growth > FY26.

Operational drivers: 6-inch InP ramp running ahead of plan — internal InP capacity to double by CY-end (now a quarter early), then more than double again by CY27 (a ~4x over two years). 6-inch yields exceed 3-inch on all three device types; 6-inch produces >4x devices at <half the cost (Sherman TX, Järfälla Sweden, + new Zurich site for early CY27). First 6-inch-content transceivers shipped in Q3, already aiding revenue and GM. 800G to grow in CY26 with 1.6T ramping rapidly (EML + SiPh first, 200G VCSEL-based later). OCS TAM raised to >$4B, bottleneck cleared, ramping across two sites. CPO TAM put at >$15B ("conservative"), initial scale-out revenue H2 CY26, scale-up H2 CY27.

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: Demand "exceptionally strong with no signs of attenuation," visibility into CY2028, multi-year LTAs that bundle customer upfront capex + demand commitments (Anderson, 2026-05-06). InP vertical integration is the chokepoint — China controls 70%+ of global InP output and Coherent is ~1 year early to 6-inch (@ParadisLabs, 2026-06-13; briefing). NVIDIA's $2B equity stake + multi-year CPO supply deal to end of decade validates the franchise; NVDA 13F also disclosed a 7.79M-share holding and Druckenmiller/Duquesne took a stake (briefings, 2026-05-16/18). Multiple incremental growth vectors layering on (OCS, CPO, Multi-Rail ~$2B+ TAM H1 CY27, thermal/Thermadite + TEGs H2 CY27). Margin runway to the >42% investor-day target driven by 6-inch cost, yield and pricing optimization (Luther, 2026-05-06; MS, 2026-06-12). Sell-side PTs: Citi (Papa Sylla) Buy $420 — highest bull case, FY28E EPS ~$16, +52% vs Street; Jefferies (Blayne Curtis) Buy $375; Barclays (T. O'Malley) OW $350; JPM (Samik Chatterjee) OW, "buy the dip" (2026-05-07/06-11 briefings).
  • Bear: Coherent is seen as less in control of its own capacity than Lumentum — "it's just taken a little bit longer to ramp" (Meta Marshall, MS, 2026-05-07). On a relative-quality view, Lumentum's CPO lasers look better-specified and Coherent's liquid-crystal OCS is less field-tested than Lumentum's MEMS arrays (Mike, Rothschild/Redburn, 2026-05-19). Gross-margin fall-through is underwhelming given the 6-inch ramp and exits — incremental GM only ~44% y/y; "margins leave more to be desired" (O'Malley/Barclays; O'Malley question, 2026-05-06). InP substrate is a structural China-supply risk — the CEO reportedly traveled to China with a Trump delegation seeking InP (briefing, 2026-06-15). Stock fell ~11% on 2026-06-10 on CPO-delay concerns (JPM framed as overblown). Valuation: post-NVDA the space trades ~25x CY28 EPS for COHR/LITE — well off its historical teens (MS, 2026-05-07).
  • Where the sell-side/buy-side stands: Broadly constructive; the debate is relative-preference within the optical basket, not demand. MS preference order is Lumentum > Corning > Coherent > Ciena on "control of destiny" (Marshall, 2026-05-07). Buy-side whisper sits near ~$15 CY28 EPS for Coherent (Marshall, 2026-05-07). PTs: MS EW $330 (Marshall/Jaramillo, raised 2026-05-07), Jefferies Buy $375, Barclays OW $350, Citi Buy $420, JPM OW. FinTwit treats COHR as a "hold + accumulate" durable winner (@crux_capital_, 2026-06-15/16).

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q4 FY26 print (≈Aug 2026): FY26 close + first FY27 framing; watch GM toward >42% target and capex trajectory.
  • 6-inch InP milestone: internal capacity doubling expected next (June) quarter — a quarter early; Zurich third site starts early CY27.
  • CPO revenue inflection: initial scale-out H2 CY26, scale-up H2 CY27 (NVIDIA lead customer + others).
  • New LTAs: additional multi-customer LTAs expected to close near-term, including hyperscalers and system OEMs (Anderson, 2026-05-06).
  • New vectors: Multi-Rail revenue H1 CY27; thermal (Thermadite/TEG) H2 CY27; semicap industrial recovery through CY26.
  • JPM investor meetings (NYC, 2026-06-17): post-selloff management access (Anderson + IR Silverstein) — read-through to sentiment.

Risks

  • InP / materials supply (10-K, 2025-08-15): dependence on rare-earth minerals, noble gases and InP substrates; China controls majority of InP output — geopolitical/export-control exposure to a core input.
  • Execution on capacity ramp: 6-inch InP yield/qualification risk; OCS production bottlenecks (one cleared Q3, others possible); competitor (Lumentum) seen as further ahead on capacity control.
  • CPO timing & competitive position: scale-up CPO is H2 CY27 and unproven at volume; copper-vs-optics penetration debate (10–50% by 2030 per Corning internal range); peers question liquid-crystal OCS durability.
  • Valuation / multiple: re-rated to ~25x CY28; any demand-digestion or supply over-build undercuts the equity story.
  • Customer concentration / China end-market: hyperscaler-concentrated demand; export-control and complex regulatory exposure across a multi-country manufacturing footprint (10-K).

LITE — Lumentum Holdings

Wiki · generated 2026-06-18 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\LITE (filings + transcripts) · _equity_calls · _briefings\by-ticker\LITE.md. Master index: ../INDEX.md.

🔖 Themes: Optical / CPO (Co-Packaged Optics & AI Interconnect)

Snapshot

Lumentum is the cleanest US large-cap expression of the AI-datacenter photonics buildout. Two reportable segments — Cloud & Networking (the engine: optical/photonic chips, components, modules, subsystems for hyperscalers and NEMs) and Industrial Tech (cyclically soft) (LITE 10-K, FY25, 2025-08-19). What matters is the AI stack: indium-phosphide (InP) EML/CW/ultra-high-power (UHP) lasers, cloud transceivers (1.6T), optical circuit switches (OCS), and the four growth legs management keeps repeating — cloud transceivers, OCS, scale-out CPO, and scale-up CPO. The franchise pivot is real: legacy cyclical telco supplier → "foundational engine of the AI revolution" (Hurlston, Q2FY26, 2026-02-03). NVIDIA took a direct equity stake (+$2.02B to cash) on top of a take-or-pay laser supply agreement (Q3FY26, 2026-05-06). The buy-side debate is no longer demand — it's whether they can build it (execution/capacity), and whether the multiple holds.

Current state (latest quarter — Q3FY26, 2026-05-06)

  • Revenue $808.4M, +90% YoY, record quarter, above guide midpoint (Hurlston/Ali, Q3FY26).
  • Non-GAAP GM 47.9% (+540bps QoQ, +1,270bps YoY); non-GAAP OM 32.2% (+700bps QoQ, +2,140bps YoY); non-GAAP EPS $2.37. GAAP EPS $1.50 (Ali, Q3FY26).
  • EML supply-demand imbalance "greater than 30%" — i.e. undershipping customer demand by ~30%, and widening even as capacity is added (Hurlston to Koontz, Q3FY26). Targeting >50% growth in EML units by Dec-Q 2026; record EML shipments +100% YoY.
  • OCS: "multiyear, multibillion-dollar purchase agreement"; backlog well past $400M, majority shipping in 2027, three customers (Q2FY26 + Q3FY26). Dec-Q calendar exit-run-rate well above the prior ~$100M peg.
  • CPO/scale-out: ~$50M Dec-Q (cal Q4 '26); an incremental multi-$100M UHP order clicking in 1H'27; scale-up CPO first shipments late cal-2027 (Q2FY26). Pump-laser constraint flagged as "biggest… somewhat unanticipated" bottleneck (Hurlston to Rakesh, Q3FY26).
  • Q4FY26 guide: rev $960M–$1.01B, non-GAAP OM 35%–36%, non-GAAP EPS $2.85–$3.05 on ~102M shares (Ali, Q3FY26). At $805M Q3 print they are ~halfway to the OFC target of $2B quarterly rev / 40% OM in 18–24 months (Hurlston @ JPM, 2026-05-18).
  • Capacity: 5th InP fab acquired in Greensboro NC (six-inch from the start, ~2yr to HVP → 2028); 40% Sagamihara expansion front-loaded (>half delivered in Dec-Q); Caswell UK and Takao Japan being pulled into service. CapEx $125M, capacity-led. Cash $3.17B post-NVIDIA (Ali, Q3FY26).

Debate / thesis

  • Bull:
    • Demand isn't the question — supply is. Hurlston: "there's no demand problem, which is an enviable situation… our issue is execution" (Samik/JPM fireside, 2026-05-18). FundaAI framed Hurlston's Mizuho remarks as "a supply problem, not demand" (briefing, 2026-06-11).
    • Locked book, pricing power. ~90%+ of fab allocation under take-or-pay LTAs through cal-2027; incremental units auctioned at "even healthier pricing"; LTAs killed the old quarterly price-down ritual (Hurlston/Ali, Lumentum followup call, 2026-05-07). "Sold out through 2028" possible; expects to fill the entire order book through 2028 within two quarters (MS, morning briefing 2026-05-15).
    • Four growth legs still ahead. "The vast majority of this growth is still ahead of us" (Hurlston, Q2FY26). Scale-up CPO is "a brand new market that didn't exist before" — >10x the InP need of scale-out (Yuen/Hurlston, Q2FY26 + JPM 2026-05-18).
    • NVDA validation + moat. Jensen's three photonics investments = Lumentum, Coherent, Corning — "Not Credo. Not copper" (@Midnight_Captl, briefing 2026-05-12). $2B NVDA stake + take-or-pay; OCS Google design win (R64 OCS, in production). Hurlston: high-power laser reliability proven in subsea ("Mariana Trench") — multi-year head start vs new entrants (JPM, 2026-05-18).
    • Sell-side PTs (May '26, briefings): R&Co Redburn (Mike Harrison) Buy, PT $1,270, rev 20% above consensus, 153-page launch "Copper if you can, optical because you must"; JPM (Chatterjee) OW, PT $1,130 (FY27E $19.40 / FY28E $32, 35x C28F); Citi (Malik/Sylla) Buy ~$1,100 (FY28E ~$30); Jefferies (Jeff Pu) Buy $1,168.
  • Bear:
    • Execution is the whole tape. JPM's own framing: "Lots of irons in the fire; execution the concern" (Meyers, briefing 2026-05-08). Hurlston himself admits margin/manufacturing on transceivers is the soft spot.
    • Transceiver margin drag. LITE transceiver GM "in the 20s," vs Coherent mid-30s and Chinese/Innolight mid-40s; line of sight to 30s, still well below the ~50% corporate level even as the business roughly doubles over 4–5 quarters (Hurlston, followup call 2026-05-07).
    • Valuation/positioning. BofA (Vivek Arya) Neutral, PT $1,100 — best-in-sector growth but "upside well priced in" (CY26/27E +18%/+9%) (briefing 2026-05-06). Barclays (Rand): sentiment "a lot more mixed than a few months ago… pushback on huge EPS numbers" (briefing 2026-05-20). @ResearchQf: "most extended vs 4yr avg," 20–40% correction plausible (briefing 2026-05-13). Stock fell -8% on 2026-06-10 on CPO-timing noise.
    • CPO timing / cannibalization. JEF (Beavington) flagged a CPO OIO pushout to 2030-31 on interposer overheating; MS (Marshall) and JPM (Chatterjee) call CPO concerns "overblown" and buy-the-dip, but the binary timing debate is the recurring wobble (briefings 2026-06-11/12). Scale-out CPO cannibalizes pluggable transceivers (Hurlston, JPM 2026-05-18).
    • Substrate/equipment/China. China controls 70%+ of global InP output (briefing 2026-06-13); substrate "mostly under control" via Sumitomo LTA but "tighter than I'd like"; equipment (reactors, etch) now an inhibitor (Hurlston, JPM 2026-05-18).
  • Where the sell-side stands: Broadly constructive, PTs clustered ~$1,100–$1,270; the bull case is Redburn/JPM/Citi/Jefferies/MS; the swing voter is BofA Neutral on valuation. Independent (FundaAI, Crux, Irrational Analysis) overwhelmingly long. The actionable edge: the Street agrees on demand and disagrees on (1) CPO ramp timing and (2) whether the multiple has run ahead of the 2028 numbers.

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q4FY26 / FY26 print (fiscal year-end; Street tracking the $960M–$1.01B guide). Watch the OCS Dec-Q exit rate vs the >$100M peg and whether transceiver GM steps into the 30s.
  • Scale-out CPO inflection 2H cal-2026 (~$50M Q4); scale-up CPO first shipments late cal-2027 — the generational leg.
  • InP capacity milestones: Sagamihara 40%+ delivery, Caswell/Takao ramp, Greensboro six-inch yield (2028 HVP). Watch for a phase-four fab announcement (Hurlston: decision needed late-'26/early-'27 to serve '29).
  • New LTAs / pre-pay structures beyond NVIDIA (Hurlston targeting more "before next quarter," JPM 2026-05-18); AMD actively seeking CW laser supply (briefings 2026-06-16/18).
  • ELS module entry — ~2–2.5x content uplift vs selling bare lasers (Yuen, Q2FY26).
  • Already in the rear-view but flow-relevant: NDX-100 inclusion effective 2026-05-18 (replaced CSGP).

Risks

  • Customer concentration: Customer A 16.0% / Customer B 15.4% of FY25 net revenue (>10% each); transceiver demand now skewed to one sole-source customer (LITE 10-K FY25; Q2FY26).
  • Execution/manufacturing: test-time and "boutique component" bottlenecks (gaskets, LEDs) gating OCS; six-inch InP edge-effects unproven at HVP; new-laser-recipe requals run ~6 months (followup call 2026-05-07).
  • Supply chain: InP substrate dependence (Sumitomo LTA), semicap equipment lead times, China geopolitical/export exposure on InP (10-K FY25 risk factors; briefings).
  • Technology inflections / commoditization: SiPh share gain at 1.6T, Chinese entrants, CPO/NPO cannibalizing pluggables; 3.2T reverts to EML (Hurlston, JPM 2026-05-18).
  • Multiple risk: stock +136% YTD into May; extended technicals; CPO-timing headlines drive sharp drawdowns.

AAOI — Applied Optoelectronics

Wiki · generated 2026-06-18 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\AAOI (filings + transcripts + decks) · _equity_calls · _briefings\by-ticker\AAOI.md. Master index: ../INDEX.md.

🔖 Themes: Optical / CPO (Co-Packaged Optics & AI Interconnect)

Snapshot

Vertically-integrated optical-components maker: designs and builds laser-based fiber-optic products from the indium phosphide (InP) laser chip up through finished transceiver. Three end-markets: (1) data center transceivers (100G/400G/800G/1.6T) for hyperscalers — the AI story; (2) CATV access-network amplifiers (1.8 GHz / DOCSIS 4.0); (3) telecom/FTTH (de minimis). In Q1 FY26, mix was 54% data center / 44% CATV / 2% telecom-FTTH-other (Q1 FY26 transcript, 2026-05-07). The differentiator the whole bull case rests on: in-house InP laser fab (Sugar Land, TX) at a moment when laser/InP capacity is the binding constraint across the optical chain. US-anchored manufacturing footprint is a tariff and supply-security selling point. Volatile small/mid-cap; very high FinTwit interest (see Debate).

Current state (latest quarter)

Q1 FY26 (2026-05-07): Revenue $151.1M, +51% Y/Y, +13% Q/Q — fourth consecutive record quarter; in line with the $150–165M guide. Non-GAAP GM 29.2% (vs 31.4% Q4'25, 30.7% Q1'25; guide 29–31%). Non-GAAP loss/sh $(0.07) (in range). Data center rev $81.4M, +154% Y/Y (100G 41% of DC, 200G/400G 46.7%, 800G 5.6%, 10G/40G 5.6%); 400G sales +10x Y/Y. 800G rev only $4.6M in the quarter — the ramp is still ahead. CATV $66.8M, +24% Q/Q, above guide. Cash $449.4M (vs $216M at YE25 — raised via equity/debt); inventory $206M; capex $68.7M in Q1 and "above" that quarterly going forward (Q1 FY26).

Guidance / the bogey. Q2 FY26 rev $180–198M, GM 29–30%, EPS $(0.03)–$0.03. FY26 raised to >$1.1B rev with >$140M non-GAAP operating income (up from the ~$1.0B committed at Q4'25, 2026-02-26). Management is explicit that FY26 is capacity-gated, not demand-gated — Thompson Lin pegged true demand at $1.4–1.5B, internal target $1.2B (Q1 FY26). The marquee number: by mid-2027, ~$471M/month of DC transceiver revenue (≈$90M 100G/400G + $217M 800G + $164M 1.6T; ~780k transceivers/mo; ~40% US capacity) — raised from $378M/mo at Q4'25 (Q1 FY26). Shape: 2H'26 is a step function, not linear — Q3 and Q4 each +60–80% Q/Q as Texas capacity comes online (Q1 FY26). Capacity targets: ~100k/mo 800G+1.6T exiting Q1, ~150k/mo this quarter, >650k/mo by YE26 (~30% Texas), >930k/mo by YE27 (>50% Texas). 1.6T contributes late-2026, big ramp 2027 (Lin: "more than $2B for 1.6T transceivers" next year). Customers: a returning hyperscaler placed first 1.6T volume order + two 800G orders; expected back as a 10%+ customer (Q1 FY26). At Q4'25 mgmt guided three >10% hyperscale DC customers for the year, two roughly equal-sized (Q4'25). Margin path: 35% exit-26, 40% non-GAAP GM by Q3/Q4 2027 as mix shifts to 800G/1.6T + laser (Q1 FY26).

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: The asymmetry call. AAOI owns the scarce input — in-house InP lasers — at a moment hyperscalers are going upstream to lock laser/epiwafer/substrate capacity. @aleabitoreddit (Serenity, semis) ran it from "$30 → $170/$175" and frames $471M/mo = ~$5.6B ARR off a ~$13.5B MC in H1'27, "largest US transceiver supply chain for mass production of 800G/1.6T," with the major inflection in 2028 (briefing 2026-06-18). Game-theory leg: AMD actively scrambling for CW laser supply (flagged on Rosenblatt checks) → once NVDA/AMD lock LTAs, AMZN/MSFT "must follow" (briefings 2026-06-17/06-18). China's InP export easing reads as a direct capacity unlock for a laser maker (briefing 2026-06-16). Mgmt supports the moat claim: in-house lasers since 1990, mostly in-house-built production equipment ("not in direct competition" for tooling), targeting top-3 laser producer worldwide by YE27 and +350% laser-fab capacity (Q1 FY26).
  • Bear: It is an execution-and-dilution story trading on a 2027 projection. Crux Capital / Gaetano: "the story is there, but we need the execution follow-through" (briefing 2026-05-19) and "$30B or $3B?" — "plenty of execution risk: capacity must expand dramatically + capex ramp + dilution on table + margins improve + new orders qualified + qualified orders ship on time" (briefing 2026-05-08). @BenBajarin: the market must believe the 2027 ramp carries into a much larger 2028 base, not a one-year hyperscaler pull-forward (2026-05-08). The Q4'25 800G slip (firmware interop, pushed below the ~$4M plan into Q1) shows qualification timing risk is real (Q4'25). Jim Cramer (CNBC): the optical names are "parabolic… not going to be good for the market" (briefings 2026-05-12/05-13). Insider selling noted (briefings 2026-06-01). Heavy short interest + squeeze dynamics = two-way volatility ("wild swings until Q3 validation" — Crux, 2026-05-31).
  • Where the sell-side/buy-side stands: No formal broker PT in the briefing flow — coverage is FinTwit-dominated (Serenity high-conviction long; Crux execution-skeptic; ParadisLabs ranks AAOI behind LITE/COHR on order-book scale + commercial readiness, 2026-05-14). Sell-side names asking questions on the calls: Raymond James (Simon Leopold), Rosenblatt (Genovese), Wolfe (Notter), Needham (Koontz), Northland (Savageaux). AAOI appeared on Rothschild's "12m relative highs" list (briefing 2026-05-14). The actionable edge is the gap between FinTwit conviction and the absence of institutional sell-side ratings — validation hinges on the Q3 capacity print.

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q3 FY26 print — the validation event: did the step-function ramp (+60–80% Q/Q) and 800G volume actually land? This is the "Q3 validation" everyone cites.
  • 800G volume ramp from Q2 (first volume shipment done in Q1); 1.6T first revenue late-2026.
  • Texas: 210k sq ft facility initial production in Q3 (800G/1.6T dedicated); Pearland + Houston buildings online early 2027.
  • Laser-fab milestones: 4-inch → 6-inch InP by YE27; ELSFP/CPO laser ramp toward ~400k pcs/mo by 2027; 3-year LTAs under negotiation with ~3 customers (lasers + ELSFP).
  • Funding watch — further equity/debt raises to fund the capex ramp (dilution overhang).
  • IEEPA tariff refund (≥$5.7M, approved; timing unknown).

Risks

  • Customer concentration: top-10 = 98% of Q1 revenue; three >10% customers. 10-K (FY25, 2026-02-26) tags Microsoft and Digicomm as named DC/CATV customers (Oracle in FY24); Amazon subsidiary holds customer warrants (contra-revenue ~2.5%). Loss of any one is material.
  • Execution / capacity timing: the entire valuation discounts a mid-2027 $471M/mo number; equipment qualification + manufacturing cycle time push revenue 3–4 months past install — slippage compounds (Q1 FY26).
  • InP substrate supply: 4–5 suppliers (4 ex-China), ~1yr inventory, but volume scaling fast; China controls 70%+ of global InP output (briefing 2026-06-13) — geopolitical two-way risk.
  • Dilution / cash burn: still non-GAAP loss-making; capex >$68.7M/qtr funded partly by equity sales.
  • Margins: 40% GM target is a 2027 exit aspiration off 29% today; mix and ramp inefficiency are near-term headwinds.
  • China footprint: 1.2M sq ft Ningbo plant (transceiver/CATV) — tariff and geopolitical exposure the US build-out aims to offset.
  • Valuation/momentum: parabolic move, high short interest, FinTwit-driven — sentiment unwind risk independent of fundamentals.

Consensus estimates (BBG)

GLW — Corning Incorporated

Wiki · generated 2026-06-18 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\GLW (filings + transcripts + decks) · _equity_calls · _briefings\by-ticker\GLW.md. Master index: ../INDEX.md.

🔖 Themes: Optical / CPO (Co-Packaged Optics & AI Interconnect)

Snapshot

Corning is a materials-science compounder (175 years old as of 2026) that has re-rated into an AI infrastructure name. Five reporting segments as of Q1 2026: Optical Communications (~$1.8B/qtr, the growth engine — datacenter fiber/cable/connectivity for AI plus carrier fiber-to-the-home/DCI), Glass Innovations (newly combined Display + Specialty Materials; Gorilla Glass, semiconductor/EUV optics), Automotive (emission control + auto glass), Solar (newly carved out — polysilicon/wafers/modules), and Life Sciences + Emerging Growth. The whole equity story now runs through the "Springboard" plan and the optical/AI ramp. Q1 2026 sales $4.35B; FY2025 sales $16.4B (Q4-2025 call, 2026-01-28).

Current state (latest quarter — Q1 FY2026, 2026-04-28)

Sales +18% YoY to $4.35B, EPS +30% to $0.70, both at the high end of guide. Op margin +220bp to 20.2%, gross margin 39.1%, ROIC 13.5%, FCF $188M. Eighth consecutive quarter of YoY sales growth (Schlesinger, Q1-2026).

  • Optical $1.8B, +36% YoY (enterprise and carrier each +36%); net income $387M, +93% YoY. Off the up-to-$6B Meta agreement, Corning has now concluded two more hyperscaler LTAs of similar size/duration and is working on others (Weeks, Q1-2026). Carrier had its best quarter in years on FTTH + DCI; Lumen agreement expanded/extended.
  • Glass Innovations $1.4B, +1% YoY, 22.8% NI margin. Display volume down slightly sequentially (better than feared mid-single-digit guide). Mgmt flags memory-cost inflation to significantly hit the device market in 2026 but expects to outgrow it; launched Gorilla Glass Ceramic 3.
  • Solar $370M, +80% YoY, but net income only $7M (-$20M YoY). Wafer ramp is the drag — ~$0.04 EPS hit in Q1, plus an incremental $30M Q2 expense for an extended maintenance shutdown / permanent-power transition. Polysilicon already >20% op margin; modules cross 20% in Q2 (Schlesinger, Q1-2026).
  • Guidance: Q2 2026 sales ~$4.6B (+14% YoY), EPS $0.73–0.77 (+25%), absorbing ~$0.07 total solar-wafer drag. FY CapEx ~$1.7B, "could be a little above." Buybacks are the primary cash-return vehicle on top of the dividend (Schlesinger, Q1-2026).
  • Springboard: vs. the Q4 2023 base, +33% sales / +79% EPS / +390bp op margin / +470bp ROIC. The internal plan was lifted to +$11B incremental annualized sales by 2028 (Q4-2025 call) and at the May 6, 2026 NYC investor event was extended to 2030 with a ~$40B total-revenue-by-2030 target and a new $10B Photonics MAP (CPO/inside-the-box).

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: Optical is becoming a Display-like franchise — Weeks said he'd let optical ROIC exceed the glass businesses, and that gross/profit margins could "eclipse" Display (Q1-2026 Q&A with Wamsi Mohan, BofA). The hyperscaler/Nvidia LTAs use prepayments + take-or-pay-style commitments to de-risk capex, so Corning grows into AI without a cash-flow dip (Weeks, Q1-2026). The Nvidia partnership (announced ahead of May 6) adds a multi-billion upfront prepayment, ~$3.2B warrant for Nvidia to take equity, 10x US connectivity capacity, +50% fiber, and anchors the $10B Photonics build (Corning IR optical call, 2026-05-12). Morgan Stanley (Meta Marshall, 2026-05-07) ranks GLW #2 of the optical basket behind Lumentum on "control of its own destiny," noting the May 6 day "certainly exceeded" lowered expectations.
  • Bear: This is a content/adoption story, not pricing — mgmt explicitly reiterated reluctance to raise commodity fiber price (Marshall, 2026-05-07; Weeks, Q1-2026), so the thesis depends on innovation adoption and scale-up/CPO timing, which mgmt itself pegs at 2028+ with internal CPO penetration assumed anywhere 10–50% by 2030. After the Nvidia news GLW trades ~30x CY2028 vs. ~25x for Lumentum/Coherent — i.e. a premium to the optical pack (Marshall, 2026-05-07). Q4-2025 and Q1-2026 both saw "digestion on the print… not as much pricing power as people expected" (Marshall, 2026-05-07). Solar wafer ramp remains an open-ended EPS drag and execution risk (power/water/utility delays). DCI target of "$1B medium term" struck some investors as conservative vs. Arista's $1B already — read as either upside or as Corning under-indexed to scale-across (Corning IR call, 2026-05-12).
  • Where the sell-side stands: JPM (Samik Chatterjee) Neutral, PT $185 — initiated/framed around the Photonics TAM and ~$8 2030E EPS (briefing 2026-05-07; reiterated 2026-05-14). Reit. Neutral 2026-06-09, noting an Amazon deal is already "baked in Springboard" (briefings roll-up). So the marginal sell-side concern is valuation/expectations, not demand. Morgan Stanley would be "more aggressive buyers on down days," order: Lumentum > Corning > Coherent > Ciena (Marshall, 2026-05-07).

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q2 2026 print (late July 2026): solar-wafer plant restart timing, op-margin trajectory above 20%, whether the third+ hyperscaler LTAs are announced.
  • Conclusion/announcement of additional hyperscaler LTAs (Amazon read-through per JPM) and any customer-side disclosure.
  • Photonics MAP / CPO ramp — first revenue inflection mgmt frames as 2028+; Ruben Ultra hybrid optical-port mix is the swing variable.
  • Display memory-cost pass-through through 2H 2026; yen hedges in place through 2030.
  • Solar wafer crossing the 20% op-margin target and full $2.5B+ revenue build by 2028.
  • JPM TMT conf (attended 2026-05-19); ongoing buyback cadence.

Risks

  • Adoption/timing risk on scale-up + CPO (internal CPO-penetration range 10–50% by 2030) — the bull case is back-end-loaded.
  • Input/utility/single-supplier exposure: raw materials, precious metals, energy/water and limited-source equipment; solar wafer ramp already hit by utility-build delays (10-K Item 1A risk factors, 2026-02-12; Weeks, Q1-2026).
  • China / trade restrictions explicitly cited as a supply-chain and demand risk (10-K Item 1A, 2026-02-12).
  • Yen / FX: Display USD net income depends on hedging in a weak-yen environment (hedged 2026–2030; Schlesinger, Q4-2025).
  • Cyclicality + customer concentration: Display/auto/consumer-electronics demand swings; large LTAs concentrate optical exposure on a handful of hyperscalers + Nvidia.
  • Valuation: ~30x CY2028 leaves little margin for an adoption-timing slip (Marshall, 2026-05-07).

Consensus estimates (BBG)
