  • Briefings: no roll-up on disk (_briefings/by-ticker/AGX.md does not exist).

APH — Amphenol Corporation

Wiki · generated 2026-06-20 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\APH (filings + transcripts) · _equity_calls · _briefings. Master index: ../INDEX.md.

Snapshot

Amphenol is the world's #2 interconnect compounder — a serially-acquisitive, ultra-decentralized (145+ general managers) maker of connectors, cable, busbars, antennas and sensors. Three reportable segments at FY2025: Communications Solutions ~52% (high-speed, RF, power, fiber-optic connectors + cable + antennas), Harsh Environment Solutions ~26% (ruggedized mil-aero/industrial interconnect, printed circuits), Interconnect and Sensor Systems ~22% (busbars, power distribution, sensors) (10-K FY2025, 2026-02-11). By end market FY2025: IT datacom 36%, industrial 19%, automotive 15%, comms networks 10%, defense 9%, mobile devices 6%, commercial aero 5% (10-K, 2026-02-11). The equity story has flipped into a primary AI-interconnect name: IT datacom hit just over 40% of sales in Q1 2026 with AI driving essentially all sequential growth (Q1-2026 call, 2026-04-29). On top of organic, APH closed the $10.5B CommScope (CCS) acquisition Jan 1, 2026 — its largest ever — adding fiber-optic and high-speed datacom/comms-network capacity (Q4-2025 call, 2026-01-28).

Position in the value chain

Amphenol sits in the middle of the AI-infrastructure interconnect chain: it buys raw materials and components (copper, fiber, polymers, contacts, optics) and converts them into connectors, high-speed and power interconnect, cable assemblies, busbars / liquid-cooled busbars, antennas and sensors that ship into datacenter/IT & comms, auto, industrial and mil-aero customers. The strategic swing factor is power: as racks move to 800V DC and higher in-rack power densities, APH's content-per-rack rises across discrete power connectors, complex power cable assemblies and busbars (Norwitt, Q1-2026, 2026-04-29; JPM "Watts Up!" 800V note, 2026-06-09). See 800v-dc-power and ai-datacenter-power.

Current state (latest quarter — Q1 FY2026, 2026-04-29)

Record sales $7.62B, +58% USD / +57% local / +33% organic YoY; record adjusted diluted EPS $1.06, +68% (GAAP $0.72) (Q1-2026 call + 10-Q, 2026-05-01). Record orders $9.435B, +78%, book-to-bill 1.24:1 with all end markets ≥1.0. Adjusted op margin 27.3%, +380bp YoY (GAAP 24% after $249M CommScope acq. costs). FCF $831M (89% of NI); net leverage 1.6x after CCS.

  • IT datacom (~41% of sales): +99% USD / +81% organic, +27% QoQ — "virtually all" of the organic sequential growth from AI (Norwitt). Prior quarters: Q4-2025 +110% organic, Q3-2025 +128%, Q2-2025 +133% (~two-thirds of total growth from AI). The AI cohort is now the dominant growth driver across four straight quarters.
  • Power thesis: Norwitt — "We talk a lot about the data part of AI data centers… we should also talk a lot about the power part." APH cites the "broadest array of power interconnect from discrete connectors to very complex power cable assemblies, bus bars, liquid cooled bus bars," positioning as a "core partner" as racks "shift to higher and higher power capacities" (Q1-2026).
  • Other end markets (Q1-2026 organic): industrial +16%, defense +25%, commercial aero +20%, comms networks flat (+91% USD on Andrew), automotive +2% (soft), mobile +1%.
  • CommScope: growing "largely at the same pace of Amphenol's own organic growth"; FY2026 contribution ~$4.1B sales / $0.15 accretion. Opens cross-sell of antennas/sensors into building connectivity ("1+1=3").
  • Guidance Q2-2026: sales $8.1–$8.2B, adjusted EPS $1.14–$1.16 (+43–45% / +41–43% YoY).

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: APH is positioned to win regardless of the copper-vs-optical-vs-CPO architecture outcome — "more interconnect, no matter what" (Norwitt, Q1-2026). JPM (Samik Chatterjee) "Watts Up!" 800V note names APH, FLEX, TEL, WOLF best positioned for the AI-DC power-stack rewiring to 800V — new connectors, cabling and power distribution = multi-billion content opportunity (briefing 2026-06-09). JPM separately argues APH is "well positioned in both copper AND optical interconnects, contrary to investor perception," i.e. the market under-credits the optical optionality post-CommScope (briefing 2026-06-09). Record 1.24:1 book-to-bill and customers "opening up order apertures" to fund APH capacity give multi-quarter visibility (Q1-2026). Margin algo improving — Lampo now targets ~30% incremental conversion vs prior 25% (Q2-2025).
  • Bear: The May 2026 narrative was that APH is a copper-terminal-value share loser as CPO/optics scale, with NVDA's investment in GLW reframing GLW as the vertically-integrated CPO winner; APH's CommScope deal seen as "not vertically integrated like GLW" (briefing 2026-05-12, per JPM J. Meyers). Other risks: $130M China tax accrual + $160M reassessment pushed the tax rate to 27% (persisting through 2026); heavy M&A (~$3.8B/5 deals in 2025 + $10.5B CCS) raises integration and balance-sheet bar (net debt $14.2B); autos soft (+2% organic). Valuation/positioning is a crowded long — APH a "modest crowded long" on the buy side (JPM AI-power desk call, 2026-06-11).
  • Where the sell-side stands: JPM constructive — 800V infrastructure beneficiary + optical optionality underappreciated (briefings 2026-06-09); JPM hosted a "super meeting with Adam Norwitt" that drew attention (JPM desk, 2026-06-11). CEO presenting at the BofA conference (briefing 2026-06-09). Note: read-through only from briefings/equity calls — no broker PT captured in the local sources.

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q2-2026 print (late July 2026): guide is $8.1–$8.2B / $1.14–$1.16; watch IT datacom organic deceleration vs the +80–130% run, book-to-bill normalization, and CommScope organic pace.
  • 800V / power content ramp as hyperscalers shift to 800V DC and higher in-rack power (NVDA Rubin/Rubin Ultra cycle) — busbar/liquid-cooled-busbar attach is the swing variable; tracks 800v-dc-power / ai-datacenter-power.
  • CPO / optical mix: whether APH defends interconnect content as architectures shift; CommScope fiber integration and any CPO design wins.
  • M&A cadence: APH runs a continuous acquisition program in a fragmented industry; next bolt-ons + CCS integration milestones.
  • China tax / FX: whether the 27% rate or further China determinations recur.

Risks

  • Architecture / copper-terminal-value risk: CPO and optical scaling could erode copper interconnect content; competitive framing vs GLW's vertical CPO integration (briefing 2026-05-12).
  • End-market cyclicality & concentration: IT datacom now ~40% of sales — AI-capex sensitivity cuts both ways; auto/industrial/comms networks are slower-cycle (10-K Item 1A, 2026-02-11).
  • M&A integration & leverage: $10.5B CommScope is the largest deal in APH history; net debt $14.2B, leverage 1.6x; acquisition accounting (backlog amort, transaction charges) depressing GAAP margin.
  • Tax / China: elevated 27% adjusted tax rate from China determinations + jurisdiction mix, expected to persist through 2026 (Q1-2026).
  • Input costs / FX / supply chain: metals (copper/gold) cost and global manufacturing footprint exposure (10-K Item 1A, 2026-02-11).

In the inbox (Outlook — recent sell-side flow)

  • JPM (Samik) — 'Watts Up! Shift to 800V' _(2026-06-09)_: APH among best-positioned (with FLEX/TEL/WOLF) in the 800V DC power-stack rewiring.
  • Copper terminal-value debate _(briefing 2026-05-12)_: GLW bull case argues CPO/optics erode copper content; JPM counters APH's optical optionality is under-credited.

Sources

  • Filings: 10-K FY2025 (2026-02-11) — business/segments/end-markets + Item 1A risk factors · 10-Q Q1-2026 (2026-05-01) — net sales $7,620.1M, segment & geographic mix, acquisition costs.
  • Transcripts: Q1-2026 (2026-04-29) · Q4-2025 (2026-01-28) · Q3-2025 (2025-10-22) · Q2-2025 (2025-07-23).
  • Equity calls: JPM AI-power pitch (2026-06-11) — APH "modest crowded long," Norwitt meeting · Ciena optical (2026-06-12) — read-through: APH is a scale-up ACC cable partner (Ciena Nitro redriver → Luxshare/Amphenol/TE).
  • Briefings: 2026-06-09 company-specific — JPM "Watts Up!" 800V (APH/FLEX/TEL/WOLF best positioned) + APH mgmt meeting · 2026-05-12 company-specific — copper-terminal-value / GLW-CPO bear narrative. No local broker PT/rating captured. Outlook not queried (see note below).
  • Outlook: not available in this environment — email-sourced sell-side ratings/PTs to be appended separately.

TEL — TE Connectivity plc

Wiki · generated 2026-06-20 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\TEL (10-K FY2025, 10-Q FQ2 FY26, transcripts) · E:\equity_calls_transcripts · E:\briefings\2026. Master index: 00_INDEX.md.

NB: ticker TEL here = TE Connectivity (connectors & sensors), not Tokyo Electron.

Snapshot

TE Connectivity is a global leader in connectivity and sensor technologies — engineered connectors, terminals, sensors, relays, heat-shrink, wire/cable and antennas that connect and distribute power, data and signal in harsh environments. Post-FY24 reorg it runs two segments: Transportation Solutions 54% of sales (auto 75% of segment, commercial transport 15%, sensors 10%) and Industrial Solutions 46% (digital data networks 28%, automation & connected living 27%, aerospace/defense/marine 19%, energy 17%) (10-K FY25, 2025-11-10). FY2025 revenue $17.4bn, +8.9% y/y (+6.4% organic); ~93,000 employees, ~55,000 in manufacturing (10-K FY25). The live story for a tech/semis book is the AI datacenter ramp inside Industrial — DDN/AI revenue going from ~$300m (FY24) to >$800m (FY25) to approaching $2.4bn in FY26 (FQ2 FY26 call, 2026-04-22) — layered on a grid/energy build-out and a still-soft auto base. Connector/interconnect peer set: APH, Molex, Aptiv, Sumitomo, Yazaki, Sensata; on AI power/connectors it's read alongside APH and FLEX.

Position in the value chain

TE sits in the middle of the electronics chain: it buys raw materials (metals — its largest purchase category — plus oil-based resins/plastics) and components, then designs and manufactures the connectors, terminals and sensors that physically move power and signal through a system, and sells into auto, AI datacenter, industrial/automation and energy/grid customers. The AI-relevant content is rack power distribution and high-speed signal interconnect — including busbars, power connectors and cabling tied to the shift to 800V / HVDC rack architectures, where TE is cited (with APH/FLEX) as a connector-content beneficiary (JPM "Watts Up!", briefing 2026-06-09; Citi, briefing 2026-06-15). TE's VP/Head of ICT framed HVDC (400V→800V) as cutting energy loss 2-5% per conversion stage with liquid cooling mainstream by 2030 (expert call, briefing 2026-06-18).

Current state (latest quarter)

FQ2 FY26 (quarter ended 2026-03-27, call 2026-04-22): Net sales $4.7bn, +15% reported / +7% organic y/y; adjusted EPS $2.73, +24% y/y (above guide); adjusted operating margin 21.7%, +130 bps y/y. On a GAAP basis the 10-Q shows net sales $4,744m (+14.5% y/y), operating margin 20.1% (vs 18.1% PY), diluted EPS from continuing operations $2.90 (vs $0.04 PY — prior-year quarter carried a large discrete charge) (10-Q, filed 2026-04-24). Orders $5.3bn (+~$1bn y/y), book-to-bill 1.12. Industrial Solutions +27% reported / +17% organic, op margin ~22% (+260 bps), driven by digital data networks (+~50% y/y), energy and automation; DDN orders +60% y/y building 2027 backlog. Transportation +5% reported, with auto -4% organic (NA production weak) offset by commercial transport +17% organic. Energy +60% reported / +11% organic, ~60-67% utility/grid hardening. AI revenue raised ~$150m vs 90 days prior → approaching $2.4bn for FY26 (Curtin). Copper-and-optical strategy reinforced by the RampPhotonics acquisition; "copper will continue to be the workhorse in the rack." H1 FY26 FCF $1.3bn (record), ~100% returned to shareholders, dividend +10%; capex raised to ~6% of revenue, "almost entirely" for AI/DDN ramp (Mitts). FQ3 guide: ~$5.0bn / EPS ~$2.83 (+10% / +17% y/y).

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: TE is a direct, lower-beta way to own AI datacenter power and signal interconnect with a self-funding capital-return profile. The AI ramp is steep and visible — ~$300m (FY24) → >$800m (FY25) → ~$2.4bn (FY26), raised twice in the last two prints, with DDN orders +60% and book-to-bill 1.12 building 2027 backlog (FQ1/FQ2 FY26 calls). JPM (Samik Chatterjee) "Watts Up!" names APH, FLEX, TEL, WOLF best positioned in coverage on the 800V AI-DC power-stack rewiring — new connectors, cabling and power distribution required (briefing 2026-06-09). Citi flags APH/TEL connector content as a beneficiary of the 800V DC transition (briefings 2026-06-15). Industrial margins are inflecting hard (+260-500 bps y/y in recent quarters) and capex is being deployed into demand, not speculatively.
  • Bear: AI/DDN is still only ~$2.4bn of a $17-18bn company — Transportation (54% of sales) is the majority and auto is soft (auto -4% organic, NA production "a little worse," auto outperformance at the low end of the 4-6% target; FQ2 FY26 call). The 800V opportunity is real but timing is contested — @bubbleboi channel checks argue rack-level GaN/SiC/connector content is "2-3 years before hitting earnings" with no near-term DC-construction pull (briefing 2026-06-15, on the broader 800V basket). Input-cost inflation (metals, resins, freight) and clean-energy project "pauses" are live margin watch items (FQ1/FQ2 FY26 calls). It's a connector beneficiary, not the design-win owner of the AI silicon socket.
  • Where the sell-side/buy-side stands: JPM constructive — 800V infrastructure beneficiary, optical optionality underappreciated (briefing 2026-06-09). Citi groups TEL with APH on connector content into the 800V/Rubin Ultra ramp (briefings 2026-06-15). No single-name TEL rating/PT is captured in the briefing archive in this window; the stance is read-through from the 800V/AI-DC power basket and the connector peer flow (APH, FLEX). _No fresh sell-side PT was found in inbox — see Outlook note below._

Catalysts / what to watch

  • FQ3 FY26 print (~late Jul 2026): delivery vs the ~$5.0bn / $2.83 guide; whether FY26 AI revenue tracks/exceeds the ~$2.4bn target and 2027 backlog keeps building.
  • 800V / HVDC rack adoption curve: NVDA 800V "Rubin Ultra" ramp (2H27 per Citi/desk reads) — the content-per-rack and timing debate is the swing for TEL/APH connector revenue (briefings 2026-06-15/18).
  • Copper-vs-optical mix & RampPhotonics integration: how much scale-out optical TE captures alongside the copper "workhorse" (FQ2 FY26 call).
  • Auto inflection: NA production stabilization and a return toward the 4-6% content-outperformance target (FQ2 FY26 call).
  • Investor Day follow-through: product showcase / mid-term framing from the Nov 20, 2025 event and any updated AI-content TAM (FQ4 FY25 call).

Risks

  • End-market cyclicality / auto weight: Transportation is 54% of sales and auto is the largest single end market; NA auto production softness and the EV/charging mix shift drive Transportation results (10-K FY25; FQ2 FY26 call).
  • AI concentration & timing: the growth narrative leans on hyperscale DDN; demand can "fluctuate significantly" (10-K FY25, DDN end-market language) and the 800V content ramp timing is contested (briefing 2026-06-15).
  • Input-cost inflation: metals (largest purchase category), oil-based resins and freight; spot-market exposure pressures margin (FQ1/FQ2 FY26 calls).
  • FX: non-USD revenue meaningful — FX added ~4.4% to FQ2 reported growth and can reverse (10-Q FQ2 FY26).
  • Energy project pauses: clean-energy slippage within the otherwise strong grid/energy book (FQ2 FY26 call).
  • Tariffs / trade & tax: Irish-domiciled (plc) with global manufacturing — trade policy, tariffs and tax-regime changes are structural exposures (10-K FY25).

Themes

  • 800V DC power — TEL connector/busbar content in the 800V/HVDC rack transition.
  • AI datacenter power — DDN/AI revenue ramp (~$2.4bn FY26) and rack power/signal interconnect.

In the inbox (Outlook — recent sell-side flow)

  • JPM (Samik) — 'Watts Up! Shift to 800V' _(2026-06-09)_: TE Connectivity flagged best-positioned connector-content beneficiary in the 800V/HVDC stack (with APH/FLEX/WOLF).

Sources

  • Filings: 10-K FY2025 (2025-11-10) · 10-Q FQ2 FY26 (2026-04-24, qtr ended 2026-03-27).
  • Transcripts: FQ2 FY26 (2026-04-22) · FQ1 FY26 (2026-01-21) · FQ4/FY25 (2025-10-29) · FQ3 FY25 (2025-07-23).
  • Briefings: E:\briefings\2026 — 2026-06-09 (JPM "Watts Up!" 800V; APH/FLEX/TEL/WOLF best positioned), 2026-06-15 (Citi APH/TEL connector content into 800V/Rubin Ultra; @bubbleboi 2-3yr bear), 2026-06-18 (TE VP/Head of ICT expert call on HVDC 400→800V / liquid cooling).
  • Outlook: not pulled for this page — the outlook.py two-pass launcher returned no data in this environment (no reachable MAPI session; python alias unresolved). Sell-side stance is sourced from the dated briefing archive, which is itself built from the Outlook tape.

NVT — nVent Electric plc

Wiki · generated 2026-06-20 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\NVT (10-K FY2025, 10-Q Q1 2026, transcripts) · E:\briefings\2026. Master index: ../INDEX.md.

Snapshot

nVent is an electrical connection & protection pure-play, spun off from Pentair in 2018. Two reportable segments post-portfolio reshuffle: Systems Protection (FY2025 $2,592.9M, +42.2%; brands HOFFMAN enclosures, SCHROFF, TRACHTE control buildings, plus switchgear/bus systems from the Avail Electrical Products Group; liquid cooling + air cooling) and Electrical Connections (FY2025 $1,300.2M, +9.9%; brands CADDY, ERICO, ILSCO; bus systems, cable management, power connections). FY2025 continuing net sales $3,893.1M, +29.5% (+12.6% organic) (10-K filed 2026-02-17). The company sold its Thermal Management business to Brookfield for $1.6B net cash (2025, now discontinued ops) and redeployed into infrastructure: EPG/Avail ~$1.0B (closed 2025-05-01), Trachte ~$0.7B (2024), ECM ~$1.1B (2023). It sits squarely in the AI data-center power & cooling chain — DC sales hit ~$1B in 2025 (vs ~$600M in 2024), now the primary growth engine.

Position in the value chain

nVent converts commodity inputs (steel, copper, aluminum, electronic components) into engineered electrical infrastructure: connection/protection hardware, busbar/power distribution (EPG switchgear + bus systems, PDUs), liquid cooling (CDUs, modular cooling), and protective enclosures/control buildings. It sells primarily to AI data centers (hyperscalers), power utilities, industrial and broader infrastructure customers — increasingly direct to the AI buildout, where it supplies both white space (~80% of DC mix) and gray space. The data-center power + liquid-cooling franchise is the highest-growth, highest-AI-beta node of the chain (NVIDIA partner network; CDU roadmaps to 2030). See themes: 800v-dc-power, ai-datacenter-power.

Current state (latest quarter)

Q1 2026 (reported 2026-05-01) — record print. Sales $1.242B, +53% reported, +34% organic (acquisitions +17 pts, FX +2 pts); adjusted EPS $1.09, +63% YoY; FCF $54M (+21%). Systems Protection $895M, +70% (+50% organic), ROS 22.7% (+220 bps) with ~46 pts of organic from the infrastructure/data-center business; Electrical Connections $347M, +15% (+8% organic), ROS 24.4% (-390 bps) on copper inflation. Organic orders +~40% (DC-led); backlog grew to $2.6B (10-K reported FY2025 year-end backlog $2,349.9M, +213.6% YoY). Infrastructure now 55% of sales (vs 12% at spin-off). Mgmt raised FY2026 guidance to +26%–28% reported / +21%–23% organic and adjusted EPS $4.45–$4.55 (from $4.00–$4.15); Q2 guide adjusted EPS $1.12–$1.15 (Q1 2026 call, 2026-05-01).

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: AI data-center power/cooling super-cycle with multi-year visibility. DC sales ~$1B in 2025 (from ~$600M in 2024); backlog tripled to $2.3B at YE25 (~50% of 2026 guide) and grew to $2.6B in Q1 (Q4 2025 call 2026-02-06; Q1 2026 call). Liquid-cooling penetration "<30% of data centers" (mgmt) leaves long runway; NVIDIA partner network designation + CDU roadmaps to 2030 (Q3 2025 call 2025-10-31). Portfolio remix into infrastructure (EPG/Avail switchgear+busbar, Trachte control buildings) raises structural growth and AI beta. Net leverage 1.5x leaves M&A/buyback firepower.
  • Bear: Order growth is lumpy and concentration on AI DC projects raises whisper of a digestion air-pocket; backlog quality and bid-win variability flagged in 10-K. Electrical Connections margin hit -390 bps on copper inflation in Q1 — pricing lag risk. Tariff headwind ~$80M FY26 (after ~$90M prior year, ~$170M cumulative two-year). Heavy acquisition-led growth (EPG/Trachte/ECM) carries integration and goodwill-impairment risk. Liquid-cooling competition intensifying.
  • Where the Street sits: No NVT broker notes in the local briefing corpus (E:\briefings\2026) — repo coverage is thin. The only genuine NVT reference is the BofA Datacloud Cannes expert call, 6/8/2026 10am ET (Obin), grouping NVT with VRT/ETN/CARR/JCI as a datacenter-power/cooling complex (2026-05-30-morning.md). Sell-side PT/rating data not present in readable sources; flagged for an Outlook pull (Outlook unavailable this session — timed out).

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q2 2026 print (~early Aug 2026): EPS guide $1.12–$1.15; watch organic orders trajectory and whether DC momentum sustains the +40% order pace.
  • EPG flips to organic accounting in Q2 2026 (one-year anniversary) — first clean read on infrastructure organic.
  • Electrical Connections margin recovery toward historical (~28–30%) as copper pricing actions catch up.
  • Blaine, MN liquid-cooling facility ramp through 2026 (doubles LC footprint) + new CDU launches Q2–Q3.
  • M&A pipeline ("very strong" per mgmt) and tariff-relief developments.

Risks

  • Cyclical end-market demand (industrial, infrastructure, commercial/residential) and project-win lumpiness in DC/utility (10-K Risk Factors).
  • Raw-material inflation, esp. copper/aluminum/steel; tariff exposure ~$80M FY26.
  • Customer/project concentration in AI data centers; demand pull-forward / air-pocket risk.
  • Goodwill & intangible impairment from the EPG/Trachte/ECM acquisition stack (10-K).
  • FX (EMEA/developing markets), competition in liquid cooling and connections.

In the inbox (Outlook — recent sell-side flow)

  • BofA Datacloud (Cannes) expert-call calendar _(2026-06-08)_: nVent grouped with the datacenter-power/cooling names (VRT/ETN/CARR/JCI).
  • _Direct broker notes on nVent are thin in the inbox; coverage is mostly AI-DC-power read-through + the NVIDIA CDU partner-network angle._

Sources

  • Filings: 10-K FY2025 (filed 2026-02-17); 10-Q Q1 2026 (filed 2026-05-01).
  • Transcripts: Q1 2026 · Q4 2025 · Q3 2025 · Q2 2025.
  • Briefings: one NVT reference — BofA Datacloud Cannes expert call 6/8 (NVT/VRT/ETN/CARR/JCI), E:\briefings\2026\2026-05-30-morning.md. No broker notes; corpus coverage thin.
  • Outlook: attempted (two-pass outlook.py --no-body --days 21) — unavailable this session (timed out, no output).
  • Themes: 800v-dc-power · ai-datacenter-power.

VRT — Vertiv Holdings Co

Wiki · generated 2026-06-20 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\VRT (filings + transcripts) · _equity_calls · E:\briefings\2026. Master index: 00_INDEX.md · themes: ai-datacenter-power · 800v-dc-power.

Snapshot

Vertiv is the leading pure-play in critical digital infrastructure for data centers, communication networks, and commercial/industrial environments — it powers, cools, deploys and services the electronics that process and store data (10-K, 2026-02-13). Portfolio spans power (AC/DC power management, single- and three-phase UPS, low/medium-voltage switchgear, busbar/busway, rack PDU), thermal management (air- and liquid-cooled — chillers, CDUs, rear-door, direct-to-chip), integrated modular/prefab (SmartRun overhead busway, OneCore converged white-space), racks/enclosures (Great Lakes), and a large service & spares annuity. Reports three geographic segments: Americas (~68% of Q1'26 sales), APAC (~19%), EMEA (~12%). End markets: data centers (hyperscale/cloud, colo, neocloud, enterprise), comms networks, C&I — with hyperscale/cloud + neocloud (CoreWeave, Nebius) the AI growth engine. The directest listed AI-datacenter power/cooling pure-play, and NVIDIA's named 800 VDC ecosystem partner.

Position in the value chain

Vertiv sits in the middle of the AI-datacenter physical-infrastructure stack: it buys power and thermal components (power semis/SiC, transformers, compressors, copper busbar, controls) and integrates them into the gray-space-to-white-space systems — power train, thermal chain, racks, prefab modules, plus lifecycle services — that hyperscalers, colos and enterprises plug compute into. It is a direct AI-datacenter pure-play and, via the NVIDIA 800 VDC collaboration, co-designs the next-gen power architecture (centralized rectifiers, high-efficiency DC busways, rack-level DC-DC) for Rubin Ultra-era AI factories (Vertiv–NVIDIA PR, 2025-10; Q3 FY25, 2025-10-22).

Current state (latest quarter)

Q1 FY26 (2026-04-22) — a beat-and-raise; the AI-datacenter demand pull is intact and orders/backlog now opaque by mgmt choice.

  • Net sales $2,649.5M, +30.1% y/y (organic +23%, M&A +4%, FX +3%) (10-Q, 2026-04-22). Adjusted op profit $551M, +64%; adjusted op margin 20.8%, +430bps; adjusted diluted EPS $1.17, +83% (Q1 FY26 call). Adjusted FCF $653M; net leverage 0.2x; S&P 500 inclusion + investment-grade ratings achieved.
  • Regional split: Americas $1,814.4M (+44% organic), 27.0% segment margin; APAC $513.7M (+12% organic); EMEA $321.4M (−29% organic, lapping soft prior orders — guided back to growth H2'26) (10-Q).
  • FY26 guide raised: net sales ~$13.75B (+34%), adjusted EPS $6.35 (+51%, up from $6.20 at Q4), op margin ~23.3%, FCF ~$2.2B; capex elevated to 3–4% of sales for capacity/tech. Q2'26 guide EPS ~$1.40 (+47%) (Q1 FY26 call).
  • Backlog $15.0B at YE2025 (vs $7.2B YE2024); ~majority firm, shipping over 12–18 months (10-K, 2026-02-13). Backlog Q1'26 "a bit more elongated," giving "good visibility into 2027." Mgmt stopped quarterly orders/backlog disclosure at Q4'25 (annual only in 10-K).
  • 800V HVDC products launch H2'26, validation underway, volume 2027+; aligned to NVIDIA Rubin Ultra. Liquid cooling "technically demanding and financially consequential"; two thermal/structural bolt-ons announced. "Bring Your Own Power" partnership (C Power Energy) for speed-to-power.

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: Cleanest listed way to own the power + cooling content per MW of the AI build-out. Q4'25 showed the order pull in raw form — organic orders +152% y/y, +117% q/q, book-to-bill 2.9x, backlog doubling to $15.0B (Q4 FY25, 2026-02-11) — and FY26 EPS guide stepped to $6.35 (+51%) on 30–35% incremental margins (Q1 FY26, 2026-04-22). The NVIDIA 800 VDC collaboration positions Vertiv at the center of the biggest DC power-architecture change in 20+ years: scalable 800 VDC systems + energy storage, components (centralized rectifiers, DC busways, rack DC-DC) finalized, reference architecture already in early-design on several gigawatt-scale AI factories, launch H2'26 → Rubin Ultra 2027 (Vertiv–NVIDIA PR, 2025-10). The 800V theme expert frames UPS players as adaptable rather than disrupted — "Vertiv will just adapt," shifting UPS know-how into BES/PSU for 800V (UBS 800V power, 2026-05-14). Concentrated buy-side conviction: Cooperman concentrated VRT ~$800M as "the gigawatt name" (briefing, 2026-05-08).
  • Bear: Long-cycle backlog is exposed to any AI-capex air-pocket — and mgmt's decision to stop reporting quarterly orders/backlog removes the single best leading indicator just as the cycle peaks the comps (Q4 FY25, 2026-02-11). EMEA is a live execution sore (−29% organic in Q1'26; power constraints + regulatory; restructuring ongoing) (10-Q; Q3 FY25). On 800V, the architecture eliminates the traditional UPS ("in an 800V system you will not have a UPS… you will have a BES") and kills low-frequency transformer layers — Vertiv has to pivot a core profit pool to battery/SST converters, and the safety/protection spec (DC arc, breakers) isn't finalized (UBS 800V power, 2026-05-14; 800v-dc-power). Tariffs a 2025 margin drag (EMEA-led), guided to net-offset by Q1'26. Multiple is rich and priced off out-year EPS.
  • Where sell-side/buy-side stands: Buy-side treats VRT as the AI-datacenter power proxy — a top concentrated holding for Cooperman ($800M, "gigawatt name," briefing 2026-05-08). The 800V acceleration (Vera Rubin 800V HVDC pulled ahead to Q3 2026, small volume, per Taiwanese media) is flagged as "a direct catalyst for $VRT and the power-semi supply chain," alongside Delta, Schneider, Eaton (briefings 2026-06-15). _No specific broker rating/PT for VRT was found in the readable briefings/equity-call corpus; Outlook was unavailable this session (see Sources)._

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q2 FY26 print (~late July 2026): test the +27% net-sales / $1.40 EPS guide; watch EMEA inflecting to growth and any qualitative orders/pipeline color now that quantitative disclosure is gone.
  • 800V HVDC commercial launch (H2 2026) → volume shipping 2027 into NVIDIA Rubin Ultra; Vera Rubin 800V reportedly pulled to small-volume Q3 2026 (briefing 2026-06-15). Track design wins on gigawatt AI factories.
  • Capacity ramp: elevated capex (3–4% of sales); switchgear/busbar/integrated-power capacity already >2x since 2021; Pune thermal plant; Great Lakes rack capacity (10-K).
  • Liquid-cooling mix shift (CDU/direct-to-chip; PurgeRite fluid management) as rack densities climb; M&A bolt-ons in thermal/structural.
  • EMEA restructuring payoff into 2026 margins.

Risks

  • AI-capex cyclicality / order reversal — long-cycle backlog tied to hyperscaler spend; "unpredictable placing or cancelling of customer orders" and failure to realize backlog explicitly flagged (10-K risk factors, 2026-02-13).
  • Reduced transparency — no more quarterly orders/backlog; harder to detect a turn.
  • Customer concentration / purchasing power — large hyperscale/colo/neocloud buyers carry pricing leverage (10-K).
  • 800V architecture disruption — eliminates traditional UPS + low-frequency transformers; execution risk in pivoting to BES/SST; unfinished DC safety/protection spec (UBS 800V, 2026-05-14).
  • Supply chain / components — "may not be able to procure critical parts at any price"; component cost inflation (10-K).
  • Tariffs / global trade — margin headwind, EMEA-exposed; guided to net-offset (10-K; Q2–Q3 FY25).
  • EMEA execution — power constraints, regulatory, restructuring (Q3 FY25; 10-Q).
  • Fixed-price contracts / large-project execution and competition vs Schneider/Eaton (10-K).

In the inbox (Outlook — recent sell-side flow)

  • Vertiv ↔ NVIDIA 800 VDC collaboration _(PR, advancing Rubin-Ultra-era AI-factory power)_: Vertiv co-designs the 800VDC reference architecture.
  • UBS 800V power call + briefings: orders +152% / 2.9x book-to-bill, $15B backlog — cleanest listed AI-DC power+cooling proxy.

Sources

  • Filings: 10-K 2026-02-13 (FY2025; business + risk factors + $15.0B backlog) · 10-Q 2026-04-22 (Q1 FY26 segment detail).
  • Transcripts: Q1 FY26 (2026-04-22) · Q4 FY25 (2026-02-11) · Q3 FY25 (2025-10-22) · Q2 FY25 (2025-07-30). (transcripts via The Motley Fool / The Globe and Mail.)
  • Equity calls: UBS 800V power (2026-05-14) — VRT/Eaton/Schneider UPS→BES adaptation, 800V architecture.
  • Briefings: E:\briefings\2026 — Cooperman VRT ~$800M "gigawatt name" (2026-05-08); Vera Rubin 800V HVDC pulled to Q3 2026, direct VRT catalyst (2026-06-15); Vertiv AlphaSense liquid-cooling expert call (2026-05-08). _No _briefings/by-ticker/VRT.md roll-up present._
  • Other: Vertiv–NVIDIA 800 VDC collaboration PR (Oct 2025), via investors.vertiv.com / prnewswire.
  • Outlook: attempted via E:\.claude\scripts\outlook.py (two-pass) — unavailable this session (script ran but returned no data / no JSON written; MAPI not connected). No sell-side emails pulled.

Themes

  • AI Data-Center Power & Nuclear
  • 800V DC Data-Center Power

FLEX — Flex Ltd.

Wiki · generated 2026-06-20 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\FLEX (filings + transcripts) · _briefings. Master index: 00_INDEX.md.

Snapshot

Flex is the world's #2 contract electronics manufacturer / EMS-ODM (~$27.9B FY26 revenue, FYE Mar-31), pivoting from low-margin assembly toward an "end-to-end manufacturing partner" with proprietary datacenter power and cooling content. As of FY26 it reorganized into three reportable segments (10-K, 2026-05-20): ITS — Integrated Technology Solutions (Communications, Lifestyle; $11.1B, 40% of sales, -2% y/y); RMS — Regulated Manufacturing Solutions (Industrial, Automotive, Healthcare; $10.2B, 36%, +5%); and CPI — Cloud and Power Infrastructure (Cloud & Cooling, Power; $6.6B, 24%, +38% y/y). The CPI segment is the AI-datacenter story: Anord Mardix critical power (switchgear, busway, modular power pods, turnkey datacenter electrical), Crown medium-voltage switchgear/grid, JetCool direct-to-chip liquid cooling, plus embedded power (power shelves, BBUs, DC/DC) riding the 800V DC and 1MW+ rack transition (10-K FY25, 2025-05-21; Q3 FY26, 2026-02-04). On May 5, 2026 Flex announced it will spin off CPI into a separate public company (target Q1 CY2027), splitting the AI-power pure-play from the legacy EMS base. No customer >10% of sales; top-10 = 45% (10-K FY26).

Position in the value chain

Flex sits in the middle of the AI-datacenter and broader-electronics chain: it buys components from suppliers, then designs/builds/integrates at scale — and increasingly sells its own power and cooling IP (Anord Mardix critical power, JetCool liquid cooling) into hyperscaler/neocloud/utility buildouts. The differentiated content is 800V DC power delivery + direct-to-chip liquid cooling for high-density AI racks, where Flex claims a "very small group of competitors" (Q3 FY26, 2026-02-04). See 800v-dc-power and ai-datacenter-power.

Current state (latest quarter)

Q4 / FY26 (reported 2026-05-06) — beat-and-raise, with the CPI spin-off as the headline.

  • Q4 revenue $7.5B, +17% y/y; adj. gross margin 9.9% (record, +50 bps); adj. operating margin 6.7% (+50 bps); adj. EPS $0.93, +27% (Q4 FY26 call, 2026-05-06).
  • FY26: revenue $27.9B, +8%; adj. EPS $3.30, +25%; adj. operating margin 6.3% (+70 bps); FCF ~$1.1B; CapEx $625M (2.2% of sales) (10-K 2026-05-20; Q4 call).
  • Segment FY26 (10-K MD&A): ITS $11.1B (-2%, 5.4% income margin); RMS $10.2B (+5%, 6.0%); CPI $6.6B (+38%, 9.2% margin, -100 bps on cloud-ramp investment). CPI sub-mix: Cloud & Cooling +29%, Power +61% y/y. CPI was $3.2B in FY24 → $4.8B FY25 → $6.6B FY26.
  • Spin-off: CPI to become a standalone public co (power + thermal + compute integration for AI datacenters / utilities), target Q1 CY2027, intended tax-free. CEO Revathi Advaithi → CPI CEO; Michael Hartung → Flex (RemainCo) CEO (Q4 FY26 call).
  • FY27 guide (combined, pre-spin): revenue $32.3–33.8B (+18% midpoint); adj. op margin 7.0–7.1%; adj. EPS $4.21–4.51 (+32% midpoint); CapEx $1.4–1.6B (steps up for compute/power capacity); CPI +65–75% growth (multi-year contracts incl. Google), ramping 80%+ in FY28.
  • Closed EP2 (Electrical Power Products) acquisition the week of the print — utility-grade power / grid modernization.

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: CPI is a scarce, vertically-integrated AI-power play — Flex claims it is the only provider doing end-to-end cloud IT integration plus a full power/cooling portfolio (rack-to-utility), with "very small group of competitors" in 800V DC / 1MW+ racks (Q3 FY26, 2026-02-04). JPM (Samik Chatterjee) "Watts Up! 800V AI DC power stack" names APH, FLEX, TEL, WOLF as best positioned for the 800V rewiring of the AI rack — new connectors/cabling/power distribution = multi-billion TAM (briefing 2026-06-09). The spin (target Q1 CY2027) crystallizes a pure-play AI-power SpinCo at a higher multiple vs. the EMS base. FY27 guide of +18% revenue / +32% EPS is well above legacy EMS growth, with CPI +65–75% the engine. NVIDIA (modular DC systems), LG (gigawatt-scale thermal) and Equinix (liquid-cooling) partnerships + a Google multi-year contract underpin backlog. FLEX joins the S&P 500 on June 22, 2026 alongside MRVL — passive-flow tailwind (briefings 2026-06-09).
  • Bear: CPI margins compressed -100 bps in FY26 (9.2%) on cloud-ramp investment, and cloud margins sit below power margins inside CPI (Stifel Q&A, Q4 FY26) — growth is dilutive near-term and CapEx steps to $1.4–1.6B in FY27. EMS is structurally low-margin (consolidated FY26 op margin only 6.3%) and ITS revenue is shrinking (-2%, Lifestyle -9%). Hyperscaler in-housing risk (Amazon developing its own cooling; "warrant deal" immaterial to FY26) and customer concentration (top-10 = 45%). Spin execution / cost and dis-synergies between CPI and the manufacturing base; "no assurance" the spin completes on terms or timeline (10-K risk factors). Tariff exposure is "low-calorie revenue" — pass-through dilutes margin (JPM Q&A, Q1 FY26).
  • Where the sell-side stands: JPM (Chatterjee) constructive — 800V beneficiary, pre-quiet-period H&N catch-up covered FLEX alongside CLS/SANM/STX/WDC (briefings 2026-06-09, 2026-06-12). UBS bucketed FLEX in its earnings-callback / 800V-power complex (briefing 2026-05-08). FLEX repeatedly on 52-week-high lists through the AI-hardware run (briefings 2026-05-12/14). _No single-name PT in inbox/briefings read._

Catalysts / what to watch

  • CPI spin-off (target Q1 CY2027) — Form 10 / structure, RemainCo vs SpinCo financials, multiple re-rate; key swing factor.
  • Q1 FY27 print (~late July 2026): guide $7.35–7.65B revenue / $0.86–0.92 EPS; CPI +20–30% (ramps 2H FY27) — watch the CPI growth cadence and cloud-ramp margin drag.
  • June 22, 2026: S&P 500 inclusion effective (passive buy flow).
  • CapEx step-up to $1.4–1.6B in FY27 — compute capacity additions; FCF conversion guided ~60% (ex-spin costs) vs 80%+ historically.
  • Hyperscaler/neocloud contract conversions (Google named); NVIDIA / LG / Equinix program ramps; EP2 integration.

Risks

  • Spin-off execution — may not complete on terms/timeline; cost and dis-synergy risk (10-K FY26 risk factors).
  • CPI margin dilution — investments in cloud/power capacity pressure CPI margins; demand outside Flex's control (10-K).
  • Customer concentration — top-10 = 45% of sales; hyperscaler in-sourcing of cooling/power (Q1/Q3 FY26 calls).
  • Cyclicality / demand variability — order cancellations, short product lifecycles; ITS/Lifestyle consumer weakness (10-K; Q3 FY26).
  • Tariffs / trade policy — pass-through "low-calorie revenue," margin headwind; geopolitical exposure (10-K; Q1 FY26 call).
  • Capital intensity — customer-specific equipment impairment/obsolescence; rising CapEx into FY27 (10-K).
  • Structurally low EMS margins (~6% consolidated) limit RemainCo re-rating.

Note: FLEX not yet present in _data/estimates.json (asof 2026-06-19) — consensus block will populate once estimates are fetched for the ticker.

In the inbox (Outlook — recent sell-side flow)

  • JPM (Samik) — 'Watts Up! Shift to 800V' _(2026-06-09)_: FLEX among best-positioned (Anord Mardix critical power + 800V/1MW rack); JPM notes 'long crowding in Flex has picked up substantially.'
  • S&P 500 inclusion _(June 22, alongside MRVL)_ — passive demand tailwind.

Sources

  • Filings: 10-K FY26 (2026-05-20) · 10-K FY25 (2025-05-21) (Anord Mardix / Crown / JetCool / power-pod detail) · 10-Q Q3 FY26 (2026-02-06).
  • Transcripts: Q4 FY26 (2026-05-06) · Q3 FY26 (2026-02-04) · Q2 FY26 (2025-10-29) · Q1 FY26 (2025-07-24).
  • Briefings: JPM "Watts Up! 800V" (2026-06-09); H&N pre-quiet-period catch-up (2026-06-12); UBS callback bucket (2026-05-08); 52-week-high tape-tells (2026-05-12/14); S&P 500 inclusion (2026-06-09).
  • Themes: 800v-dc-power · ai-datacenter-power.
  • Outlook: queried (outlook.py --no-body --days 14) — no FLEX-specific sell-side emails returned in this environment.

WOLF — Wolfspeed, Inc.

Wiki · generated 2026-06-20 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\WOLF (10-K/10-Q filings + transcripts) · briefings E:\briefings\2026. Master index: 00_INDEX.md.

Snapshot

Wolfspeed is a US-listed, vertically integrated, pure-play silicon-carbide (SiC) company — it grows its own SiC crystal/substrates and sells (1) SiC + GaN materials (wafers/substrates) and (2) SiC power devices (MOSFETs/modules). Two product lines: Power Products ~67% of revenue, Materials Products ~33% (10-Q, 2026-05-07; Power $218.4M vs Materials $100.3M over the Successor period 9/30/25–3/29/26). End-markets are Automotive/EV + fast charging and Industrial & Energy (AI data centers, grid modernization, renewables/storage) (10-Q, 2026-05-07). The headline is the balance sheet, not the end-markets: Wolfspeed filed a prepackaged Chapter 11 (RSA 2025-06-22, confirmed 2025-09-08) and emerged 2025-09-29, cutting total debt ~70% and adopting fresh-start accounting — old equity was nearly wiped (existing holders kept ~1.3M of 25.8M new shares, ~5%; convertible noteholders took the rest) (10-Q, 2026-05-07). It is a sub-$200M-quarter, gross-loss, cash-burning turnaround with a SiC-800V optionality call layered on top — own the restructuring before you own the theme.

Position in the value chain

Wolfspeed is vertically integrated in SiC — it grows its own SiC boules/crystal and substrates (assuring supply, controlling cost), then fabs/packages SiC power devices, and also sells bare SiC/GaN materials to third parties (10-Q, 2026-05-07). It sells into EV/automotive, industrial & energy (grid, renewables/storage), and the emerging 800V AI-datacenter power stack — the latter the only real growth vector right now (datacenter rev +50% QoQ in Q2 FY26, +30% QoQ in Q3 FY26; 10kV MOSFET launched; customers migrating 400V→800V) (Q3/Q2 FY26 calls). But the operative fact is the post-Chapter-11 balance sheet: ~70% debt cut, fresh-start basis, near-total equity wipeout, still running negative gross margin on Mohawk Valley underutilization. SiC sits at infrastructure voltages (>1,000V) in the 800V debate; JPM names WOLF among 800V beneficiaries but it is a small-cap, levered, going-concern-recovered name — distressed-equity profile, not a clean compounder. See 800v-dc-power.

Current state (latest quarter)

Q3 FY26 (quarter ended 2026-03-29, call 2026-05-05): Revenue $150.2M, -19% YoY (vs $185.4M PY); Power $100.1M (90% from Mohawk Valley 200mm, 10% last-time 150mm inventory), Materials $50.1M (flat QoQ). GAAP gross loss -$40.0M (-26.6%); non-GAAP GM -20.6%, a double-digit-ppt improvement vs Q2's -34% on mix + fresh-start inventory digestion; underutilization ~$46M is the primary GM drag. Operating loss -$114.3M, net loss -$119.9M, EPS -$3.05 (39.3M wtd shares, Successor). Adj. EBITDA ~-$62M; operating cash flow ~-$84M; net CapEx ~$5M ($38M gross less $33M NY-State Mohawk incentives) (10-Q + call, 2026-05-05). Liquidity: cash + ST investments $1,164.8M at 3/29/26 (up from $955.4M at 6/29/25); first debt maturity 2030 (10-Q, 2026-05-07). Q3 actions: ~$476M gross private placements, senior-secured-note balance cut 43%, CFIUS approval released equity to Renesas (>$400M equity uplift). Q4 FY26 guide: revenue $140–160M, still-negative GM (sequential improvement). Demand: automotive "uncertain"/soft; AI data center the growth vector (+30% QoQ; 400V→800V migration; TOL D portfolio; first commercial 10kV SiC MOSFET) (call 2026-05-05). _Note: post-emergence "Successor" financials are not comparable to prior-year "Predecessor" periods (fresh-start basis)._

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: The balance sheet is fixed — Chapter 11 cut total debt ~70%, interest expense ~60%, pushed first maturity to 2030, and left ~$1.2B liquidity, removing the going-concern overhang that defined the 2024–25 story (10-Q, 2026-05-07; Q2/Q3 FY26 calls). On a clean basis it is a vertically integrated, US-made, pure-play SiC franchise with the only fully-ramped 200mm device fab (Mohawk Valley) and a 10kV MOSFET / 300mm crystal tech edge. The call option is 800V AI-datacenter power: datacenter rev +50% then +30% QoQ, customers migrating 400V→800V, and JPM names WOLF among best-positioned 800V beneficiaries ("Watts Up!" 800V AI-DC stack, JPM/Samik Chatterjee, briefing 2026-06-09). FinTwit treats WOLF as the levered 800V power-semi torque play — "+300% or -50%" (@insane_analyst, briefing 2026-05-16); Serenity: "very stupid to be a bear… your WOLF power-semi basket should go brrr from 800VDC acceleration" (briefing 2026-06-15). 800V was pulled ahead to Q3 2026 (NVDA Vera Rubin, briefing 2026-06-15).
  • Bear: It is still a gross-loss, cash-burning, sub-$200M-quarter business — Q3 FY26 GM -20.6%, op loss -$114M, OCF -$84M, revenue down YoY and guided flat — with the core EV/automotive demand "uncertain" and Mohawk Valley badly underutilized (~$46M/qtr drag) (10-Q + call, 2026-05-05). Old equity was essentially wiped in the restructuring (~5% retained), so the new equity carries the recovery story but at a distressed multiple/float and a sky-high cost of capital on the next refi (CFO: rate steps up mid-CY26; "very high cost of capital… something to be looked at," Q2 FY26 call). On the theme itself, SiC is contested: Citi's 800V framing favors GaN at the rack / SiC at >1,000V infrastructure and crowns TXN/IFX/ON, not WOLF; China can flood SiC/GaN and Chinese vendors are challenging incumbents incl. Wolfspeed (@trendforce, briefing 2026-06-17); @insane_analyst flags "SiC garbage, only GaN matters" skepticism (briefing 2026-06-15). The desk consensus this spring was short-biased WOLF (briefing 2026-05-31).
  • Where the sell-side/buy-side stands: No formal broker ratings/PTs surfaced in the briefing archive (small-cap, post-bankruptcy, thin coverage). Positioning was short-biased among the priority desks in late May (briefing 2026-05-31). JPM constructive on WOLF only as one name in its 800V beneficiary basket (briefing 2026-06-09). FinTwit owns the upside narrative as a high-beta 800V/distressed call (briefings 2026-05-06, 05-16, 06-09, 06-15). FinTwit-compiled 800V basket ranks WOLF the smallest at ~$2.2B mkt cap (vs IFX $116B, ON $46B) (briefing 2026-06-09).

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q4 FY26 print (~Aug 2026) — guide $140–160M; watch GM path toward break-even, underutilization trend, datacenter QoQ growth, and automotive trajectory.
  • PCIM (June 2026) — additional 10kV / high-voltage device announcements flagged on the Q3 call.
  • 800V AI-DC ramp — 400V→800V customer migration; SiC infrastructure design-ins; NVDA Vera Rubin 800V pulled ahead to Q3 2026 (briefing 2026-06-15). Track the chip ramp = track the 800V ramp (800v-dc-power).
  • Mohawk Valley 200mm utilization — the single biggest GM lever; all device production now at Mohawk, Durham 150mm shut.
  • Refinancing — next debt step-up mid-CY26; cost-of-capital and any liability-management actions on the new secured stack.
  • Materials 200mm / 300mm qualification — substrate qual progress; 300mm SiC for AI HPC packaging (long-dated).

Risks

  • Post-bankruptcy / distressed-equity profile: emerged from Chapter 11 2025-09-29; old equity ~wiped (~5% retained); new equity is a recovery instrument with a high cost of capital on refi (10-Q 2026-05-07; Q2 FY26 call).
  • Still loss-making & cash-burning: Q3 FY26 GM -20.6%, op loss -$114M, OCF -$84M; profitability hinges entirely on Mohawk Valley utilization ramping (10-Q 2026-05-07).
  • Going concern history: FY25 10-K (filed pre-confirmation) carried explicit substantial-doubt going-concern language and NYSE delisting risk; the latest 10-Q now asserts viable going-concern post-emergence — but the model is unproven on a Successor basis (10-K 2025-08-26; 10-Q 2026-05-07).
  • End-market cyclicality: EV/automotive demand "uncertain"/soft; concentrated SiC exposure to one cyclical vertical historically (Q3 FY26 call; 10-K).
  • 800V is a share game, contested for SiC: GaN-at-rack vs SiC-at-infrastructure split favors others (Citi); China SiC/GaN flood risk; "SiC garbage" skepticism (briefings 2026-06-15, 06-17).
  • Fresh-start non-comparability + intangible amortization: Successor financials carry large intangible amort (~+$60M/qtr in Q2) and are not comparable to history; reported EPS noisy (Q2/Q3 FY26 calls).
  • Renesas overhang/governance: Renesas holds convertible 2L notes + warrants + board/governance rights (capped at 39.9% voting, 9.9% voting limitation) — dilution/control considerations (10-Q 2026-05-07).
  • Litigation: consolidated securities class action re: Mohawk Valley operational disclosures Aug 2023–Nov 2024 (10-Q 2026-05-07).

In the inbox (Outlook — recent sell-side flow)

  • A two-pass Outlook pull was attempted but no callable mailbox / usable output was available in this environment. Sell-side/FinTwit stance below is therefore sourced from the briefing archive (E:\briefings\2026), not a live inbox.
  • JPM (Samik Chatterjee) _(briefing 2026-06-09)_ — "Watts Up!" 800V AI-DC power-stack note: shift to 800V creates multi-billion-dollar opportunities; APH/FLEX/TEL/WOLF best positioned in JPM coverage.
  • Desk positioning _(briefing 2026-05-31)_ — semis short-biased on WOLF (alongside QCOM/SWKS/NVDA/QRVO) into late May.
  • FinTwit — high-beta distressed/800V call: "+300% or -50%" (@insane_analyst, 2026-05-16); "sizing up WOLF (800V DC)" but skeptical on SiC vs GaN (@insane_analyst, 2026-06-15); Serenity bullish on 800V acceleration (2026-06-15); WOLF smallest in crowd 800V basket at ~$2.2B (2026-06-09); "reduced highest-cost first-lien debt by 43%", stock +9% AH (@thetranscript_, 2026-05-06).
  • @trendforce _(briefing 2026-06-17)_ — Chinese SiC/GaN vendors challenging IDM leaders incl. Wolfspeed in certain segments.

Sources

  • Filings: 10-K FY2025 (2025-08-26) · 10-Q Q3 FY26 (2026-05-07).
  • Transcripts: Q3 FY26 (2026-05-05) · Q2 FY26 (2026-02-04) · Q1 FY26 (2025-10-29) · Q4 FY25 (2025-08-25). _Two latest read in full; older two saved for reference._
  • Themes: 800v-dc-power.
  • Briefings: E:\briefings\2026 — 2026-06-09 (JPM "Watts Up!" 800V, WOLF beneficiary), 2026-06-15 (800V pulled ahead; FinTwit WOLF), 2026-06-17 (China SiC/GaN flood), 2026-05-31 (desk short-biased WOLF), 2026-05-16 (FinTwit +300%/-50%), 2026-05-06 (43% first-lien cut, +9% AH).

NVTS — Navitas Semiconductor

Wiki · generated 2026-06-20 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\NVTS (10-K/10-Q filings + transcripts) · briefings E:\briefings\2026. Master index: 00_INDEX.md.

Snapshot

Navitas is a fabless, small-cap (~$45.9m FY25 revenue) wide-bandgap power-semiconductor pure-play — GaN power ICs ("GaNFast") plus SiC MOSFETs/diodes ("GeneSiC", acquired Aug-2022) — that has executed a hard strategic pivot ("Navitas 2.0") away from its legacy China mobile/consumer fast-charger business toward high-power markets: AI data centers, energy/grid infrastructure, performance computing, industrial electrification (10-K FY2025, 2026-02-27). FY2025 revenue fell 45% to $45.9m (from $83.3m) as China mobile/consumer rolled off; net loss $117.0m (10-K, 2026-02-27). The entire equity story now hangs on one swing factor: the May-2025 selection by NVIDIA to co-develop the next-gen 800V HVDC datacenter power architecture for Kyber/Rubin-Ultra rack-scale systems (Navitas IR, 2025-05-21). This is a high-beta, binary-feel name — material AI-DC P&L contribution is not guided until 2027, against a cash-burning P&L and a forced GaN-foundry migration off TSMC.

Position in the value chain

Navitas sits in the middle of the AI-power stack: it does no fabrication itself (fabless), sourcing GaN wafers from TSMC (sole historical GaN foundry, ceasing GaN production July-2027) plus GlobalFoundries (new US 8-inch GaN partnership, production 2027) and Powerchip, and SiC from X-Fab on Navitas-developed process (10-K, 2026-02-27). It designs and sells GaN + high-voltage SiC power ICs that span "the entire power path from the utility grid to the GPU." The NVIDIA 800V HVDC design win is the swing: GaN content is framed at ~$10–15k per MW and SiC AC-DC PSU content at ~$5–8k per MW, with PSU scaling (10kW→18.5kW) driving non-linear SiC content (Q1 2026 call, 2026-05-05). The offsetting reality is small-cap / cash-burn risk — $45.9m FY25 revenue, ~$12m/qtr operating loss, breakeven needing revenue "in the high 30s [millions/qtr]" vs $8.6m actual (Q1 2026 call). See 800v-dc-power · ai-datacenter-power.

Current state (latest quarter)

Q1 2026 (quarter ended 2026-03-31; call 2026-05-05): Revenue $8.6m, +18% sequentially (Q4'25 $7.3m), above the high end of guidance; high-power markets +35% YoY and now the majority of revenue, "AI infrastructure" (data center + grid) +50% QoQ (Q1 2026 call). Non-GAAP gross margin 39.0% (+30bps); non-GAAP operating loss $11.7m; non-GAAP net loss -$0.04. GAAP net loss $33.8m / -$0.15 (incl. ~$10.3m stock comp, $4.7m intangible amortization, earnout fair-value swings) (10-Q, 2026-05-05). Cash $221m, zero debt (down from $237m at YE25 on working-capital build; inventory $14.9m); ~230m diluted shares. Customer concentration is extreme — Distributor A was 59% of Q1 revenue (10-Q, 2026-05-05). Q2 2026 guide: revenue $10.0m ± $0.5m (16%+ sequential), non-GAAP GM 39.25% ± 75bps, opex $14.5–15.5m (Q1 2026 call). Mobile/low-end consumer is "rapidly diminishing… insignificant by year-end." Management TAM: $3.5bn by 2030 at 60%+ CAGR.

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: The NVIDIA 800V HVDC selection (May-2025) legitimizes Navitas as one of very few suppliers with both GaN and SiC across the grid-to-GPU path — "Having both makes a huge difference" (Q1 2026 call). High-power is now the majority of revenue and growing (+35% YoY, AI-DC+grid +50% QoQ); gross margin is climbing through the pivot (38.5%→39.0%) and the legacy China-mobile drag is almost fully shed. Reference designs are real: a 10kW all-GaN 800V-to-50V DC-DC platform at 98.5% peak efficiency, co-developed with a customer (Q4 2025 call, 2026-02-24). Morgan Stanley's J. Moore nudged the PT to $13.70 (from $12.50) — "tracking but proofpoints pending… Stepping up the Voltage" (briefing 2026-05-06). $221m cash, zero debt = runway to fund the ramp.
  • Bear: It is pre-revenue on the actual story — material AI-DC P&L contribution isn't until 2027 (Q3/Q1 calls), while the company burns ~$12m/qtr operating loss on $8.6m of revenue and needs "high-30s [millions/qtr]" to break even (Q1 2026 call) — a >4x revenue gap. FinTwit is openly skeptical of the narrative: @insane_analyst flagged "dumb ppl falling for Navitas CEO TAM bullshit. SiC is garbage. Only GaN portfolio of value" (briefings 2026-06-15/06-16). The TSMC GaN exit (July 2027) forces an unproven migration to GlobalFoundries/Powerchip with re-qualification risk (10-K, 2026-02-27). 59% single-distributor concentration (10-Q) and a small, volatile float make this a momentum trade on NVDA headlines, not fundamentals.
  • Where the sell-side/buy-side stands: Morgan Stanley (Joseph Moore) PT $13.70, "tracking but proofpoints pending" (briefing 2026-05-06); Deutsche Bank's Ross Seymore "Stepping up the Voltage" (same). Coverage is thin and intermittent — across May–June 2026 NVTS repeatedly shows 0 sell-side emails in the briefing universe (e.g. 2026-05-12/05-22/05-28/05-31/06-18), with flow appearing only as FinTwit/52-week-high-list name-drops. Net: a small-cap where the Street is in wait-and-see mode pending 2027 proof points.

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q2 2026 print (~early Aug 2026) — guide $10.0m ± $0.5m / 39.25% GM; watch high-power mix, AI-DC+grid sequential pace, and whether mobile is fully shed.
  • In-rack GaN proof points (Q1–Q2 2027 per mgmt) — board/system-level testing now; the gate to NVIDIA 800V production revenue.
  • NVIDIA Kyber/Rubin-Ultra 800V HVDC ramp (2H26 standard arrival; deployment ~2027) — the single biggest swing; tied to the broader 800V timeline (see 800v-dc-power).
  • GlobalFoundries US 8-inch GaN qualification → production 2027 — execution proof that the TSMC-exit migration is de-risked.
  • Path to breakeven — revenue "high-30s/qtr" vs $8.6m today; watch the sequential ramp slope and opex discipline ($14.5–15.5m).
  • Capital adequacy — $221m cash vs ~$12m/qtr operating burn; any ATM/raise (small ATM costs already in the filings).

Risks

  • Cash burn / going-concern-adjacent runway: persistent net losses ($117.0m FY25), ~$12m/qtr operating loss, breakeven >4x current revenue; "may require additional capital… might not be available on acceptable terms, if at all" (10-K risk factors, 2026-02-27).
  • Binary AI-DC dependence: the thesis rests on NVIDIA 800V adoption and timing — no material P&L until 2027; if the 800V transition slips or content is contested, there is little else.
  • Customer/distributor concentration: Distributor A = 59% of Q1 2026 revenue (10-Q, 2026-05-05).
  • Foundry transition risk: TSMC ceasing GaN production July-2027; migration to GlobalFoundries/Powerchip requires re-qualification, risks delays/scrap/cost (10-K, 2026-02-27).
  • SiC credibility / competition: FinTwit questions the SiC franchise value ("SiC is garbage," @insane_analyst, briefing 2026-06-16); competes against far larger IDMs (Infineon, onsemi, TI, STM) and a potential China GaN/SiC supply flood.
  • Small-cap volatility: thin float, momentum-driven, high beta to NVDA/800V headlines; legacy China mobile/consumer + tariff exposure during wind-down (10-K, 2026-02-27).

In the inbox (Outlook — recent sell-side flow)

  • A two-pass Outlook pull was attempted (outlook.py --no-body --days 14) but returned no usable output in this environment (empty result). Sell-side/FinTwit stance below is therefore sourced from the briefing archive, not a live inbox.
  • Morgan Stanley (Joseph Moore) _(briefing 2026-05-06)_: PT $13.70 (from $12.50), "tracking but proofpoints pending"; thematic header "Stepping up the Voltage" (DB/Seymore).
  • FinTwit _(briefings 2026-06-15/06-16)_: @insane_analyst bearish on the SiC narrative ("only GaN portfolio of value"); @bubbleboi mentions NVTS as a potential AI-DC-power-stack re-entry (no fundamental conviction). Recurring pattern: NVTS shows 0 sell-side emails on most days (2026-05-12 / 05-22 / 05-28 / 05-31 / 06-18) — coverage is thin and headline-driven.

Sources

  • Filings: 10-K FY2025 (2026-02-27) · 10-Q Q1 2026 (2026-05-05).
  • Transcripts: Q1 2026 (2026-05-05) · Q4 2025 (2026-02-24) · Q3 2025 (2025-11-03) · Q2 2025 (2025-08-04). _Two latest read in full; Q2'25 is the full Refinitiv edited transcript, Q3'25/Q4'25/Q1'26 are sourced from public transcript coverage (Motley Fool / Yahoo / Seeking Alpha) and cross-checked to the 10-Q/10-K._
  • Press: NVIDIA selects Navitas — 800V HVDC (Navitas IR, 2025-05-21).
  • Themes: 800v-dc-power · ai-datacenter-power.
  • Briefings: E:\briefings\2026 — 2026-05-06 (MS PT $13.70; DB "Stepping up the Voltage"), 2026-06-15/06-16 (FinTwit SiC skepticism / AI-DC-power read-through), plus thin/zero-coverage days (05-12 / 05-22 / 05-28 / 05-31 / 06-18).
  • Consensus: BBG estimates auto-injected from _data/estimates.json (NVTS US Equity, USD) — not present as of 2026-06-20.

PANW — Palo Alto Networks, Inc.

Wiki · generated 2026-06-20 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\PANW (10-K FY25, 10-Q FQ3 FY26, transcripts) · _equity_calls · E:\briefings\2026\*.md. Master index: ../INDEX.md.

Snapshot

The largest pure-play enterprise cybersecurity vendor, pivoting from a product (firewall) company to a multi-platform recurring-revenue franchise. Three platforms: Strata (network security — ML-powered NGFWs + Prisma Access/SD-WAN SASE), Prisma Cloud / Cortex Cloud (cloud-native protection, CNAPP), and Cortex (AI-driven security operations — XSIAM, XDR, XSOAR, Xpanse) — now extended into identity (CyberArk) and observability (Chronosphere). FY25 revenue $9,221.5M, +14.9% YoY; subscription & support is the growth engine, with recurring revenue now ~46% of product revenue vs 22% three years ago (10-K FY25, 2025-08-29; Q3 FY26 call, 2026-06-02). The strategic spine is "platformization" — consolidating point products onto PANW platforms — and, increasingly, securing customers' AI/agentic deployments. NGS ARR is the headline KPI: $5.6B exiting FY25, guided to $8.9–8.95B for FY26 (inflated by M&A).

Position in the value chain

PANW sits between upstream infrastructure/threat-intelligence inputs and downstream enterprise/government buyers, packaging detection + prevention + AI-driven response into platforms rather than point tools. The differentiated angle is platformization (land-and-consolidate across network, cloud, SecOps, identity, observability) plus an emerging agentic-SecOps posture — using AI to defend against AI-accelerated attacks (XSIAM, AgentiX, Prisma AIRS) while monetizing the expanding AI threat surface.

Current state (latest quarter)

Q3 FY26 (qtr ended Apr 30, 2026; reported 2026-06-02). A broad beat clearing all bogeys after a ~60% one-month run into the print.

  • NGS ARR $8.13B, +60% YoY (incl. ~$1.6B from CyberArk + Chronosphere); RPO $18.4B (10-Q, 2026-06-03), +36% YoY; revenue $3.00B, +31% YoY (10-Q: $3,002M vs $2,289M PY).
  • Organic NNARR $370M vs $350M guide; organic RPO bookings +25% YoY (Barclays/Kalia, 2026-06-03).
  • GAAP net loss $(177)M / $(0.22) for the quarter, driven by CyberArk/Chronosphere acquisition accounting; 9-mo GAAP net income $589M (10-Q, 2026-06-03). Non-GAAP adjusted FCF $910M, +57% YoY (Q3 FY26 call).
  • Segment color: NGFW bookings ~+40% ("strongest hardware quarter in a decade"); SASE ARR $1.6B +40% with ~50 displacement wins ~$200M; XSIAM ARR >$600M, +100% YoY (740+ customers); Prisma AIRS customers >300 (tripled QoQ). Platformized customers ~2,280 (+110 net new), 120% net retention (Q3 FY26 call).
  • Guide raised: FY26 NGS ARR $8.9–8.95B (+59–60%), RPO $20.9–21.0B, revenue $11.415–11.425B (+24%), op margin 28.9–29.2%, adj FCF margin 37.5%; FQ4 EPS ~$0.97 vs Street ~$0.94 (briefing 2026-06-03; Q3 call). Stock fell ~1% post-print — "healthy breather," not a verdict (Jefferies/Favuzza, 2026-06-03).

Debate / thesis

  • Bull:
    • "Cleared all bogeys; customers clearly turning to PANW to secure AI adoption; single-digit cyber share = long runway." MS (Marshall/Weiss) raised PT to $320 from $253, OW; FY26E rev +23.8%, FY27E +18.2% (2026-06-03).
    • "Broad platform strength; NetSec/Prisma SASE displacement is the key differentiator vs ZS; constructive on FY27 acceleration." DB (Zelnick) Buy, PT $350 — "strongest Q3 in several years" in SASE (2026-06-03).
    • "Solid execution with AI tailwinds and M&A synergies ahead of plan" — XSIAM, Prisma AIRS, CyberArk all beating. JPM (Essex/Lee) OW (2026-06-03).
    • IR-meeting takeaways: 4Q26 organic NNARR guide up $20M+ at midpoint; FY27 could add $1.9B+ total NNARR; accelerating NetSec can support FCF margins. Barclays (Kalia) OW, PT $315 (note 2026-06-08, in briefing 2026-06-17).
    • Agentic-AI demand inflection. PANW expert (ex-PANW, BTG Mythos call 2026-06-18): Nikesh "referenced 800 customer meetings since April" on Mythos/AI security; "this is now the Mythos moment" (also acknowledged on the CRWD call). Unit 42 Frontier AI Defense: 800+ meetings in six weeks (Q3 FY26 call).
  • Bear:
    • "Beat-and-raise, but the organic business is underwhelming" — qualitative caution on product-revenue sustainability. Redburn/Rothschild Neutral (2026-06-03).
    • Headline NGS ARR/RPO growth is heavily flattered by M&A (~$1.6B of NGS ARR from CyberArk + Chronosphere); organic subscription revenue decelerated (Barclays, 2026-06-03; partly explained by virtual-firewall rev-rec mechanics per IR, 2026-06-17).
    • Product-outlook conservatism flagged: pull-forward dynamics + supply constraints; ~10% hardware price increase taken in April amid rising memory/storage component costs (Q3 FY26 call; DB 2026-06-03).
    • Integration/dilution risk: CyberArk consumed $21.1B (112M shares + $2.3B cash); GAAP loss this quarter; large goodwill/intangibles (10-Q, 2026-06-03).
  • Where the sell-side stands (post-print, 2026-06-03): MS $320 OW · Barclays $315 OW · DB $350 Buy · JPM OW · Susquehanna Pos · Redburn Neutral. Skew decisively positive; the debate is organic-vs-inorganic quality, not direction.

Catalysts / what to watch

  • FQ4 FY26 print — late August 2026 (FY-end Jul 31). Critical reads: organic NNARR (Barclays flags 4Q26 guide up $20M+; FY27 building toward $1.9B+ NNARR), seasonal Q4 strength, first clean view of CyberArk/Chronosphere in-period contribution.
  • CyberArk + Chronosphere GTM cross-sell ramp (~1,000 cross-org engagements initiated as of Q3).
  • AI-security monetization: Prisma AIRS, XSIAM/AgentiX, "Mythos/Fable" agentic-defense pipeline conversion.
  • Long-term targets: NGS ARR $20B by FY30; 40%+ adjusted FCF margin by FY28 (Q1 FY26 call).
  • Read-throughs: CRWD, FTNT, ZS, NET (platform/AI-security peers); PANW's print sets the cyber bar.

Risks

  • Organic growth deceleration masked by M&A; durability of product revenue vs pull-forward (Redburn; Barclays).
  • Acquisition integration + dilution + GAAP losses; large goodwill/intangible base from CyberArk ($21.1B) and Chronosphere ($3.0B) (10-Q, 2026-06-03).
  • Hardware cost inflation (memory/storage) pressuring product gross margin; price increases risk demand (Q3 FY26 call).
  • Intense competition + scarce AI/ML engineering talent in the Bay Area; high attrition (10-K FY25 risk factors).
  • Macro/geopolitical sensitivity of large enterprise + government deals; revenue-growth rate "may not be indicative of future performance" (10-K FY25 Item 1A).
  • Concentration in NGS ARR as the market's primary lens — any organic ARR miss is outsized to the multiple.

In the inbox (Outlook — recent sell-side flow)

  • BTG Mythos expert call _(2026-06-18)_ + Glasswing/Mythos cyber note _(2026-06-04)_: AI-native cyber is reshaping the security value chain — PANW positioned as the platform consolidator.
  • Post-print sell-side reset _(briefings, Jun-2026)_: PTs ~$315–350 after a clean Q3; debate = organic vs M&A-flattered NGS ARR (CyberArk $21.1B + Chronosphere $3.0B).

Sources

  • Filings: 10-K FY25 (2025-08-29); 10-Q FQ3 FY26 (2026-06-03).
  • Transcripts: Q3 FY26 (2026-06-02); Q2 FY26 (2026-02-17); Q1 FY26 (2025-11-19); Q4 FY25 (2025-08-18).
  • Equity calls: BTG Mythos expert call (2026-06-18) — agentic-AI security demand, "Mythos moment," 800 PANW customer meetings since April.
  • Briefings: 2026-06-03 (post-print sell-side roll-up, 12 emails), 2026-06-17 (IR-meeting takeaways), 2026-05-11 (FQ3 preview). No _briefings/by-ticker/PANW.md roll-up exists yet.
  • Outlook: attempted (outlook.py --no-body --days 14); no usable output / session unavailable — email color to be added separately.

CRWD — CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc.

Wiki · generated 2026-06-20 · sources: E:\Wiki Felipe\CRWD (filings + transcripts) · E:\briefings\2026. Master index: ../00_INDEX.md.

Fidelity flag: the two transcripts read (Q1 FY27 2026-06-03, Q4 FY26 2026-03-03) are third-party reconstructions (fool.com via WebFetch) — close paraphrase, not verbatim; quotation marks retained only where the source carried them. The WebFetch summary mislabeled Q1 FY27 as "ended Jan 31 2027" — it ended April 30 2026 (confirmed by the 10-Q filed 2026-06-04). Q3/Q2 FY26 saved from WebSearch headline summaries (metrics only). No CRWD-specific sell-side PT/rating exists in the on-disk briefings — only read-throughs and expert-call invites (see Debate).

Snapshot

CrowdStrike is the cloud-native cybersecurity platform built around Falcon — a single lightweight sensor that ingests endpoint, cloud-workload, identity and third-party telemetry once and reuses it across detection, investigation and response. The 10-K (FY26, 2026-03-05) positions Falcon as "the definitive platform for cybersecurity consolidation," displacing legacy point products. The model is ~95% subscription. Mix has broadened well beyond the EDR/XDR core: Cloud security (>$800m ARR), Next-Gen SIEM (>$600m), Identity (>$520m) plus the Charlotte AI agentic layer and the new AIDR (AI Detection & Response) line. It sits in the security-software layer of the AI stack — buying cloud infra/telemetry upstream, selling consolidation + agentic SecOps to enterprises downstream. Two structural angles: platform consolidation (Falcon Flex multi-module commitments) and agentic-AI SecOps (Charlotte AI as the reasoning engine; CRWD reframing itself as "critical AI infrastructure," Kurtz Q1 FY27).

Position in the value chain

Cloud infra + telemetry feed the Falcon cloud-native platform, which fans out across endpoint/XDR, identity, cloud security, Next-Gen SIEM and Charlotte AI, sold as a consolidation play into the enterprise. The edge vs. point-product rivals is single-sensor data reuse + agentic automation: Charlotte AI usage +6x y/y with ARR "more than tripled" (Q4 FY26), and AIDR "adoption happen[ing] this fast" — Kurtz frames the AIDR TAM as "structurally larger" than EDR (7 attack surfaces vs 1) as enterprises rush to secure their own AI deployments (Q1 FY27, 2026-06-03).

Current state (Q1 FY27, call 2026-06-03)

Record Q1, beat-and-raise. Total revenue $1.39bn (+26% y/y); subscription $1.32bn (+26%). Ending ARR $5.51bn (+24% y/y, accelerating); net new ARR $256m (+32% y/y) — record Q1, above guide. Non-GAAP op income $326m (24% margin, +62% y/y); non-GAAP EPS $1.10 (pre-split); FCF $468.5m (34% margin, all-time high); non-GAAP gross margin 79% (sub 81%); Rule of 40 = 59 (Q1 FY27, 2026-06-03).

  • FY27 guide raised: net new ARR to $1.279–1.303bn (+27–29% midpoint), up >520bps / >$50m vs the initial Q4 guide of $1.21–1.26bn; revenue $5.915–5.959bn (+23–24%); FCF margin ≥30% (Q1 FY27 vs Q4 FY26, 2026-03-03).
  • Falcon Flex (consolidation engine): ending ARR $1.9bn (+99% y/y), 300+ new accounts; ReFlex base 480 (25% of Flex), avg +26% ARR uplift; 130+ multi-reflexers avg +51% over initial contract (Q1 FY27). At Q4 FY26: Flex $1.69bn (+120%), 1,600+ adopters, avg ARR >$1m.
  • Emerging modules: Cloud + SIEM + Identity combined ARR >$2bn; Next-Gen SIEM >$600m; AIDR +250%+ sequentially with Q2 pipeline >$50m; FalconShield ARR nearly quadrupled y/y; Signal + Seraphic acquisitions added $7.8m NN ARR (Q1 FY27).
  • Capital/structure: 4-for-1 forward split effective 2026-07-02; cash $4.55bn; ex-NVIDIA Bartley Richardson hired as Chief AI & Autonomous System Officer (Q1 FY27).

Debate / thesis

  • Bull: Net-new-ARR re-acceleration with margin leverage — NN ARR +32% y/y on a $5.5bn base, FCF margin 34%, op margin +62% y/y (Q1 FY27). Consolidation is compounding: Flex ARR +99–120% y/y, module attach deepening (Q4 FY26: 50% of customers on 6+ modules, 34% on 7+, 24% on 8+). Durable retention at scale — gross retention 97%, net retention 115% (Q4 FY26). AI-security is the new growth vector: Charlotte AI usage +6x / ARR >3x y/y, AIDR "adoption happen[ing] this fast," CRWD reframed as "critical AI infrastructure" (Kurtz, Q1 FY27). Persistent 52-week-high / momentum-leader callouts through May 2026 (StockMKTNewz lists, briefings 2026-05-15→05-22). FTNT print read-through "positive for PANW/S/CRWD" (Bernstein/Seidman, briefing 2026-05-14).
  • Bear: (1) The July 19, 2024 outage is still the lead risk factor in both the FY26 10-K (2026-03-05) and the Q1 FY27 10-Q (2026-06-04) — "expected to continue to have an adverse effect," with open lawsuits/claims and ongoing "customer commitment packages" and a "Strategic Plan." (2) Valuation/crowding — name repeatedly on 52-week-high lists; ZS flagged as "most crowded short in security" by peers, a tape-risk proxy for the group (briefing 2026-05-19). (3) Intense competition + reliance on customers renewing and adding modules — the consolidation thesis cuts both ways if attach stalls (10-K risk factors). (4) As a cyber vendor, CRWD is itself a perennial attack target; a perceived failure-to-detect would hit brand/retention (10-K).
  • Where the sell-side/buy-side stands: No CRWD-specific PT or rating on disk. On-disk coverage is read-through and expert-channel only: Barclays/Kalia ran a Westcon Europe cyber-distributor April-Q channel call (PANW/CRWD/ZS/OKTA, briefings 2026-05-07→05-11); JPM/Essex security-software webinar incl. CRWD (2026-05-15); JPM "State of Security" cross-ref (PANW/NET/CRWD). All-In Pod (David Sacks) cited PANW/CRWD in the "FDA for AI" / Mythos framing — frontier labs gaining cyber capability in 3–6 months drives systems-hardening demand (briefing ~mid-May 2026).

Catalysts / what to watch

  • Q2 FY27 print — by cadence ~late Aug/Sep 2026 (Q2 FY26 was 2025-08-27). Guide: net new ARR $284–286m (+28–29%), revenue $1.436–1.442bn, FCF margin ~24.5% (seasonally low) (Q1 FY27 guide).
  • 4-for-1 stock split effective 2026-07-02.
  • AIDR ramp — first full quarters of the new AI-detection line; watch the >$50m Q2 pipeline convert; whether Charlotte AI agentic monetization scales beyond the >3x ARR base (Q1 FY27).
  • Flex/ReFlex durability — reflex uplift cadence (+26% avg, +51% multi-reflex) is the leading indicator for the consolidation flywheel.
  • Outage tail — resolution of July-19-related litigation and run-off of customer commitment packages (10-Q, 2026-06-04).

Risks

  • July 19 Incident: ongoing adverse effect, litigation/claims, customer commitment package run-off, "Strategic Plan" execution (10-K FY26 + 10-Q Q1 FY27).
  • History of GAAP losses; profitability not assured in all periods (10-K).
  • Dependence on customers renewing subscriptions and adding modules; failure to land new logos.
  • Intense competition / potential market-share loss; long, unpredictable sales cycles.
  • Product failure (real or perceived) to detect/prevent incidents → brand/retention damage.
  • CRWD itself is a high-value cyberattack target; compromise of its systems would damage reputation and results (10-K).
AI-monetization angle: CRWD is a securing-AI / agentic-SecOps play rather than a token-consumption story — Charlotte AI ARR >3x y/y and AIDR are sold as protection for enterprise AI buildouts. See cross-theme: tokenmaxxing (CRWD not in the exposure table; relevance is the demand pull from frontier-lab AI adoption, "Mythos Moment," Q1 FY27).

In the inbox (Outlook — recent sell-side flow)

  • Morgan Stanley TMT _(2026-06-05)_: 'CRWD bullish on AI demand.'
  • BTG Mythos expert call _(2026-06-18)_: AI-driven-attack escalation = secular tailwind for AI-native cyber (Falcon / Charlotte AI / AIDR).

Sources

  • Filings: 10-K FY26 (2026-03-05) — Falcon platform overview + risk factors · 10-Q Q1 FY27 (2026-06-04) — July 19 / Strategic Plan language.
  • Transcripts: Q1 FY27 (2026-06-03) · Q4 FY26 (2026-03-03) — both read · Q3 FY26 (2025-12-02) · Q2 FY26 (2025-08-27) — saved, headline-only. _All third-party paraphrase — see fidelity flag._
